source.subject: Discovery follow-up: buyers, security checkpoints, and invoicing path
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Park <evelyn.park@quantahealth.com>, Markus Healy <markus.healy@quantahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 09 May 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Discovery follow-up: buyers, security checkpoints, and invoicing path

Hi Evelyn / Markus —

Thanks again for taking the hour today. Quick recap from our side and suggested next steps so we can keep momentum on procurement and onboarding: 

Key outcomes from the call
- Use case validated: real-time summarization + clinical note generation for provider workflows (target pilots: ED + outpatient).
- Primary success criteria: sub-250ms median latency at 2 concurrent sessions, <20% uplift to current infra spend.
- Initial scope: 3-month pilot, up to 5 models for A/B comparison, logging retained per your compliance window.

Information we need to confirm
1) Economic buyer: who will sign the PO/contract and own budget approval?
2) Procurement contact: official PO / invoicing email and preferred process (PO first vs invoice net-30).
3) Security questionnaire routing: do we send SOC2 artifacts directly to procurement or to security@quantahealth?

Attachments: vendor-onboarding-checklist.xlsx (see tab 2 for PO/invoice flow)

Proposed immediate next steps
- Can Markus confirm whether he is the economic buyer or point me to the approver? (quick reply here works)
- If procurement prefers a short mutual action plan (MAP), we can draft one and drop it into the shared Drive.
- Suggest a 30-minute intake with your procurement + security teams next week to review the checklist and timelines. Proposed slots: Tue 2026-05-12 10:00–10:30 PT or Wed 2026-05-13 08:30–09:00 PT.

I’ve copied Marissa (Customer Success) who will lead onboarding detail and Ben (contracts) will prepare the procurement packet once we have contacts.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0184

---
Attachment: vendor-onboarding-checklist.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Evelyn Park <evelyn.park@quantahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Markus Healy <markus.healy@quantahealth.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@quantahealth.com>
Date: Sun, 10 May 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discovery follow-up: buyers, security checkpoints, and invoicing path

Logan —

Appreciate the clear recap. Quick answers below: 

- Economic buyer: Markus Healy (VP Product) will own budget approval for this pilot. Markus is looped here.
- Procurement: procurement@quantahealth.com is our vendor intake mailbox. They require a completed vendor packet + a PO for anything > $15k.
- Security routing: please send SOC2 and the questionnaire to security-requests@quantahealth.com — we’ll triage to InfoSec. Procurement will want to see the SOC2 summary and a reference contact.

On timing: the pilot budget review is on our product investment board the week of May 24; if we can provide a simple MAP + vendor packet by May 18, Markus can flag it for the meeting.

Also, our legal team prefers to get draft commercial terms before issuing a PO; if Ben can share a standard SOW and sample commercial language that’d be helpful.

I’m happy to take the intake meeting. Of the proposed slots, Tue 2026-05-12 10:00 PT works for me.

Thanks —
Evelyn Park
Director, Clinical Programs | QuantaHealth
evelyn.park@quantahealth.com

---
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Park <evelyn.park@quantahealth.com>, Markus Healy <markus.healy@quantahealth.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@quantahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 11 May 2026 08:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discovery follow-up: buyers, security checkpoints, and invoicing path

Thanks Evelyn — that clarifies a lot. Markus, appreciate you joining the thread. 

Ben — can you assemble the vendor packet (SOW template, sample commercial terms, SOC2 summary pointer) and a one-page MAP that outlines milestones + approval gates aimed at the May 24 product board? Target to share by EOD Tue May 18.

Marissa — please plan to run the intake meeting Tue 2026-05-12 10:00 PT. Agenda: 1) procurement intake checklist review (5 min), 2) security triage and artifact handoff (10 min), 3) MAP and timelines (10 min), 4) next steps and owners (5 min).

Proposed timeline for procurement/onboarding
- May 12: intake meeting with procurement + security
- May 18: Redwood shares packet + MAP (Ben)
- May 24: Quanta product board decision
- Early June: If approved, Quanta issues PO; onboarding kickoff week of Jun 7

I’ve attached a quick mutual action plan draft and a link to the Drive folder. If legal prefers different clauses, we can highlight the redlines up front so the PO isn’t delayed.

Regards,
Logan

---
Quoted message from Evelyn Park <evelyn.park@quantahealth.com> on Sun, May 10, 2026 at 09:04 AM -0700:
> Logan —
> 
> Appreciate the clear recap. Quick answers below: 
> 
> - Economic buyer: Markus Healy (VP Product) will own budget approval for this pilot. Markus is looped here.
> - Procurement: procurement@quantahealth.com is our vendor intake mailbox. They require a completed vendor packet + a PO for anything > $15k.
> - Security routing: please send SOC2 and the questionnaire to security-requests@quantahealth.com — we’ll triage to InfoSec. Procurement will want to see the SOC2 summary and a reference contact.
> 
> On timing: the pilot budget review is on our product investment board the week of May 24; if we can provide a simple MAP + vendor packet by May 18, Markus can flag it for the meeting.
> 
> Also, our legal team prefers to get draft commercial terms before issuing a PO; if Ben can share a standard SOW and sample commercial language that’d be helpful.
> 
> I’m happy to take the intake meeting. Of the proposed slots, Tue 2026-05-12 10:00 PT works for me.
> 
> Thanks —
> Evelyn
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Park <evelyn.park@quantahealth.com>, Markus Healy <markus.healy@quantahealth.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@quantahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 11 May 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discovery follow-up: buyers, security checkpoints, and invoicing path

Quick note —

I’ll prepare the vendor packet and MAP; target deliverables to the Drive folder by Tue EOD (May 18) as requested. Draft contents will include: SOW template, sample commercial terms, SOC2 summary pointer, onboarding checklist, and a preliminary invoice schedule.

A couple of asks for Quanta to speed review:
- Preferred billing contact / PO email (we see procurement@quantahealth.com but sometimes finance prefers billing@)
- Any mandatory vendor terms (e.g., data residency, specific indemnities) your legal team requires up front so we can flag them in our draft

Attachment: prelim-invoice-schedule.csv (csv)

I’ll be on the intake call Tue 2026-05-12 10:00 PT.

Ben Carter
Principal Contracts Manager | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

source.subject: On-site demo & exec dinner planning — Beryl Systems (SOC2 + procurement coordination)
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
To: Alex Chen <alex.chen@berylsys.com>, procurement@berylsys.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: On-site demo & exec dinner planning — Beryl Systems (SOC2 + procurement coordination)

Alex —

Thanks again for taking time to talk last week. Per our conversation, we'd like to propose an on-site proof-of-concept at Beryl's Palo Alto office the week of June 8 or June 14. High-level asks from your side were SOC2 artifact review, an architecture walkthrough for procurement, and an exec-level demo + dinner with your leadership.

Proposal (rough):
- June 14: 10:00–12:00 Technical demo (Monica to lead, bring sample run)
- June 14: 17:30 Exec dinner (Logan + Beryl execs)
- June 13: optional security deep-dive (Dev to present SOC2 evidence and runtime network checklist)

Please confirm who from security and procurement should be on the invites (I cc'd procurement@berylsys.com but happy to mirror specific people). Also: does your procurement team prefer a short SOW + PO process or a longer MSAs discussion? I attached a draft SOW and a short SOC2 extract to speed review.

If those dates won't work, suggest two alternatives and we'll send calendar invites.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.inference.com | +1 415-555-0172
---
Attachments: redwood-sow-draft-v1.pdf (SOW stub, 2 pages), site-security-pack_SOC2-extract.xlsx (SOC2 excerpts)

From: Alex Chen <alex.chen@berylsys.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: procurement@berylsys.com, Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@berylsys.com>, Maya Iyer <maya.iyer@berylsys.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: On-site demo & exec dinner planning — Beryl Systems (SOC2 + procurement coordination)

Logan —

Appreciate the plans. Quick notes from our side:
- June 14 looks promising for the exec dinner, but our CTO is traveling that week; can we hold a tentative block and I'll confirm by EOD Tuesday?
- Security (Sanjay) wants to see the SOC2 extract you attached and a short network diagram showing any required egress rules for the demo VM. He also asked if the demo can be run in an air-gapped fashion or via a controlled on-prem proxy.
- Procurement (Maya) — we prefer a short SOW + PO route for POCs under $25k. Can you provide an estimate and a simple PO-ready SOW?

We'll also add an internal systems engineer (Sanjay) and procurement lead (Maya) to help coordinate logistics. Can Monica run a 30-minute pre-demo call the week prior to validate a sample dataset and required credentials?

Thanks,
Alex Chen
Director of Engineering, Beryl Systems
alex.chen@berylsys.com | +1 650-555-2261

On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 09:12 Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com> wrote:
> Alex —
> >Thanks again for taking time to talk last week....

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
To: Alex Chen <alex.chen@berylsys.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.inference.com>, Maya Iyer <maya.iyer@berylsys.com>, procurement@berylsys.com
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: On-site demo & exec dinner planning — Beryl Systems (SOC2 + procurement coordination)

Thanks Alex — great context. Tentative holds are fine.

I’ll propose two concrete tracks so we can lock something quickly:
A) On-site (preferred):
  - Technical demo: June 14, 10:00–12:00 (Monica leads)
  - Security deep-dive: June 13, 14:00–15:30 (Dev leads)
  - Exec dinner: June 14, 17:30–19:30 (Logan + Redwood execs)
B) Remote-first / hybrid (if on-prem restrictions):
  - Pre-demo tech validation (remote): June 11, 11:00–11:30
  - Demo (remote with on-prem proxy): June 14, 10:00–11:30

Monica — can you confirm you can run the pre-demo call on June 11 if we go hybrid? Also attaching a short demo agenda and an internal network checklist for your security team to review.

Maya / Procurement — attached is a one-page PO-ready SOW estimate (ballpark). If we need changes we can update and DocuSign the final.

I'll send calendar invites once we have a thumbs up on which track to lock.

Best,
Logan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
---
Attachments: demo-agenda-beryl-20260514.docx, network-checklist.pdf

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference.com>
To: Alex Chen <alex.chen@berylsys.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.inference.com>, Maya Iyer <maya.iyer@berylsys.com>
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 10:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: On-site demo & exec dinner planning — Beryl Systems (SOC2 + procurement coordination)

Hi all —

Thanks for looping me in. Quick technical notes:
- I can run the 30-minute pre-demo on June 11 at 11:00 PT to validate credentials and a small sample. Please provide a 200–500MB sample and a service account with read-only access.
- For an on-prem demo: we'll bring a pre-built container image and a small GPU instance. If you prefer an air-gapped run, I’ll need the network checklist completed and a temporary SSH tunnel endpoint.
- I'll prepare a minimal runbook showing required ports, hostnames, and max tokens used in the demo so your security team can review.

Attaching: redwood-infra-network-checklist.pdf and a short KV-cache note.

Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.inference.com | +1 212-555-0199

---
Attachments: network-checklist.pdf, kv-cache-note.txt

From: Alex Chen <alex.chen@berylsys.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@berylsys.com>, Maya Iyer <maya.iyer@berylsys.com>, procurement@berylsys.com
Date: Thu, 6 May 2026 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: On-site demo & exec dinner planning — Beryl Systems (SOC2 + procurement coordination)

Logan / Monica —

Confirming we’ll go with Track A (on-site) for now. Sanjay will attend the security deep-dive on June 13 and I’ll confirm CTO availability for the 14 exec dinner by Wednesday. Maya will prepare a PO request once you send the final SOW; please send the DocuSign link when ready.

Monica — I’ll upload a 300MB sample dataset to the secure bucket and send credentials to the service account email you give me.

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Alex

On Tue, May 5, 2026 at 08:45 Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com> wrote:
> Thanks Alex — great context....

Subject: MSA redlines attached — next steps for signatures

Logan / Redwood team,

Attached are the MSA redlines we discussed (MSA_Redline_v2.docx) and the completed Order Form (OrderForm_ElmStreet_20260505.pdf). Legal at ElmStreet asked that we confirm who at Redwood will sign the MSA vs who will sign the Order Form, plus the preferred e-sign routing so we can route on our side as well.

Our timeline: we’d like final signatures by Friday 5/8. Can you confirm:
- Redwood signer for MSA (name / title)
- Redwood signer for Order Form (name / title)
- Any POA or authority docs needed from ElmStreet?

Thanks — I can jump on a 15-min call if helpful.

—James
James Keller
Head of Procurement, ElmStreet Health
attachments: MSA_Redline_v2.docx (docx), OrderForm_ElmStreet_20260505.pdf (pdf)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Helen Quinn <helen.quinn@brightwell.com>, James Keller <james.keller@elmstreethealth.com>
Date: Wed, 6 May 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA redlines attached — next steps for signatures

Thanks James — looping in Ben (Finance) and Soojin (CEO/legal approval) to confirm authority and routing.

Ben / Soojin — quick ask: can you confirm signing authority matrix for ElmStreet deal (ELM-3241)? My proposed approach:
- Order Form: sign by Redwood Finance (Ben) then ElmStreet Procurement (James).
- MSA (legal-level changes): sign by Redwood CEO (Soojin) then ElmStreet CFO (TBD).

I can prepare two separate e-sign envelopes if that makes review easier. Also uploading a short PDF mapping (esig routing map) to the Drive link below.

If either of you need me to route redline questions to Brightwell counsel (Helen), please advise.

Logan
Director, Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

From: Helen Quinn <helen.quinn@brightwell.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: James Keller <james.keller@elmstreethealth.com>
Date: Wed, 6 May 2026 12:05:00 -0400
Subject: RE: MSA redlines attached — next steps for signatures

Logan / team — reviewed the redlines you sent. A couple of notes for routing/authority:
- The indemnity paragraph has a substantive comment from ElmStreet counsel; if Redwood is willing to accept the language, counsel here recommends an executive-level signatory on the MSA (CEO or general counsel).
- Order Forms can be handled by Finance or Procurement signatories (Ben’s role is appropriate) provided the Order Form references the fully executed MSA.
- ElmStreet will need either a signed POA for Mary Alvarez (their CFO) or confirmation that Mary has signing authority on file. James — can procurement share Mary’s contact and whether a POA will be provided?

I can run the final MSA through Brightwell’s execution checklist and confirm envelope sequencing. If Redwood prefers, we can merge into a single envelope with two documents and an enforced signing order, but separate envelopes reduce complexity for countersigning.

—Helen
Helen Quinn
Counsel, Brightwell Legal (external)
attachment: review_notes_HQ.pdf (stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Helen Quinn <helen.quinn@brightwell.com>
Cc: James Keller <james.keller@elmstreethealth.com>
Date: Wed, 6 May 2026 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA redlines attached — next steps for signatures

Logan / all — confirming Redwood signing authority for this deal:
- Order Form (standard value band): I am authorized to sign Order Forms up to $500,000 in ARR. I will sign the Order Form once legal confirms the MSA language.
- Master Services Agreement (MSA): CEO-level sign required for any substantive legal concessions. Soojin will sign the MSA for execution on Redwood’s behalf.

I’ve attached a scanned POA template ElmStreet can provide if needed (POA_ElmStreet_signed.pdf). James — if you can send the name and email for your CFO / final ElmStreet signer, we’ll lock the routing.

Ben Carter
VP Finance, Redwood Inference
attachments: POA_ElmStreet_signed.pdf (pdf)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Helen Quinn <helen.quinn@brightwell.com>, James Keller <james.keller@elmstreethealth.com>
Date: Wed, 6 May 2026 21:22:00 -0700
Subject: Final routing plan & clean-copy handoff (ELM-3241)

Team — summary and agreed next steps (finalize by EOD Thursday 5/7 to meet ElmStreet’s 5/8 window):

1) Who signs what
- Order Form: Ben Carter (Redwood) -> James Keller (ElmStreet Procurement).
- MSA: Soojin Lee (Redwood CEO) -> Mary Alvarez (ElmStreet CFO) — pending confirmation of Mary’s email/POA.

2) Envelope design
- Create two separate e-sign envelopes to minimize cross-document countersigning complexity.
  a) Envelope A: OrderForm_ElmStreet_20260505.pdf
     - Signing order: Ben Carter -> James Keller
     - Authentication: email + access code (elmstreet-procurement code)
  b) Envelope B: MSA_Redline_v2.docx
     - Signing order: Soojin Lee -> Mary Alvarez
     - Authentication: ID check + signature capture; include Brightwell counsel (Helen) as reviewer (cc on completed copies).

3) Clean copies & links
- I uploaded final clean copies and the routing map to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1GqX-example-clean-copies (see files: MSA_Redline_v2.docx, OrderForm_ElmStreet_20260505.pdf, POA_ElmStreet_signed.pdf).

4) Open items (action owners)
- James: confirm Mary Alvarez contact + whether ElmStreet will provide a POA (due: EOD Thu 5/7).
- Helen: confirm MSA redlines are acceptable for CEO signature and flag any last-minute items (due: Thu noon PT).
- Ben: prepare and initial Order Form envelope (due: Thu EOD PT).
- Soojin: authorize MSA for signature once Helen confirms (standby).

If everyone agrees, I’ll create the two envelopes and share DocuSign/esig links for review. Please reply with a one-line ack.

Thanks — Logan
Director, Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

Subject: Mutual action plan + exec liaison mapping — confirm EB slot

Hi Julia — great meeting earlier. As discussed, I put together a one-page mutual action plan (draft) and a proposed executive liaison roster so we can lock the EB (executive briefing) date that aligns to your procurement/runway. High level asks below: 

- Confirm final exec sponsor (we heard CTO or Head of Engineering) and any deputies who should attend the EB.
- Agree MAP milestones and decision gates (POC, security sign-off, procurement terms).
- Pick an EB date/time window next week (we've blocked Tue/Wed 10–12 PT as options).

I've attached a short MAP spreadsheet (MAP_Atlas_v1.xlsx) and a 1-page agenda. If you prefer, we can fold this into your procurement checklist. 

Next steps: if you confirm sponsor and a primary EB attendee, Ben and I will map internal execs to match and circulate a locked invite.

Thanks — look forward to getting this across the finish line.

— Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

- Procurement: procurement@atlascorp.com will need the standard security artifacts and statement of work; can you confirm SOC2 and data-retention commitments in the MAP?
- EB timing: Tue 5/11 10:30–11:30 PT looks promising for Rajiv; if that works, let's lock it.

Thanks — Julia

Logan

source.subject: EB gating plan & exec sponsor rotation for Aurora MAP
messages:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Liz Harper <liz@aurora-analytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: EB gating plan & exec sponsor rotation for Aurora MAP

Hi Liz —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. I promised to send a short proposal for how we can map executive sponsors (EB/board-level) into the mutual action plan so the EB meeting cadence supports your internal procurement gating. High level:

- Proposed exec sponsor rotation: product exec -> security exec -> CFO (final signoff).
- Suggested EB meeting cadence: 60-minute readout every two weeks for the evaluation period (first 6 weeks), then weekly during the final two-week close sprint.
- Evaluation milestones tied to MAP: integration POC (week 2), performance/e2e inference trial (week 4), security signoff (week 6).

Attached is a quick MAP draft (Aurora_MAP_v2.xlsx) that aligns milestones to sponsors and a small .ics with the EB slot blocks I think will work (exec-briefing-slots.ics).

Action items I propose:
1) You confirm the three sponsor roles and who will represent Aurora at each meeting.

source.subject: NovusCare: on-prem connectivity decisions & next actions
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Priya Menon <priya.menon@novuscare.com>, Carl Jensen <cjensen@novuscare.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: NovusCare: on-prem connectivity decisions & next actions

Hi Priya / Carl — thanks again for the time today. Quick written recap so we have a shared starting point and clear next actions.

Call highlights / pain:
- Regulated PHI handling: NovusCare requires data to remain on-prem (no multi-tenant cloud residency). Primary driver is state-level patient data residency plus upcoming audit window (Q3).
- Air-gap requirements for the inference service: security team prefers outbound-restricted deployment with limited egress to Redwood (allowlist) for telemetry and patching; fully inbound-open endpoints are not acceptable.
- KMS integration: you need HSM-backed key management (customer-managed) and key rotation controls.
- Maintenance & procurement: hardware timeline is tight — procurement target is end of May; IT can only schedule network changes during weekend maintenance windows.

Technical constraints mentioned:
- No persistent public IPs on the inference nodes; outbound-only NAT via approved proxy (one egress IP range).
- All traffic must be TLS 1.2+; port 443 only for inference traffic. No SSH from internet.
- CIDR ranges are v4-only; we will need the specific subnets and firewall ACLs to build the acceptance checklist.

Agreed next steps (owners & rough ETA):
1) Logan: share a succinct acceptance checklist + sample network diagram by EOD today (attached: network map stub).
2) Marissa: schedule a 60-minute technical workshop with your NetOps & Security to walk the connection pattern and KMS flow (proposed next-week windows below).
3) Priya/Carl: confirm the allowed egress IP ranges and give the procurement target date for on-prem hardware (we noted end of May).
4) Ben: start the commercial conversation (SOW estimate + licensing options) once we confirm the deployment topology.

Attachments: novuscare_network_map.png (high-level sketch), redwood_onprem_acceptance_checklist.xlsx (initial checklist).

Proposed workshop slots (PST):
- Tue Apr 20: 09:00–10:00 or 15:00–16:00
- Wed Apr 21: 08:00–09:00 or 13:00–14:00

If any of the bullets above are incorrect or missing priorities, call them out and I'll update. Thanks again — Logan

--
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
logan@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0184

From: Priya Menon <priya.menon@novuscare.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Carl Jensen <cjensen@novuscare.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, NovusCare Security <security@novuscare.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: NovusCare: on-prem connectivity decisions & next actions

Logan — thanks for the clear recap and for capturing the constraints. A few clarifications from our side:

- Correct on the residency requirement — data for the clinical model must remain within our on-prem cluster. Backup metadata (not PHI) may be allowed to land in encrypted object storage but only under strict controls; we'll confirm scope.
- Regarding egress: security will only approve outbound to a small, static IP set or via an approved proxy appliance. We will not permit direct inbound access from the internet to the inference nodes.
- KMS: we are evaluating two options — customer HSM (preferred) or cloud KMS with strict key escrow policies. We will share the org's KMS decision by Apr 28.
- Procurement: procurement window is tight. Hardware PO is targeted for 05/27; we need Redwood's installation/support SOW and rough cost delta (air-gapped pricing) to include in procurement package.

Workshop: Wed Apr 21 13:00–14:00 PST works best for the security + NetOps folks. Please send an invite to the security alias and Carl.

Can you also add a short section in the acceptance checklist that lists what evidence artifacts we will need for the upcoming audit (SOC/HIPAA baseline)? That will help our compliance lead.

Thanks — Priya
Director of IT, NovusCare
priya.menon@novuscare.com | +1 617-555-0199

On Sat, Apr 18, 2026 at 09:12 AM Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Priya / Carl — thanks again for the time today. Quick written recap so we have a shared starting point and clear next actions.
> 
> [snip]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Priya Menon <priya.menon@novuscare.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Carl Jensen <cjensen@novuscare.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 14:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: NovusCare: on-prem connectivity decisions & next actions

Thanks Priya — adding a couple of concrete items and attaching the acceptance checklist variant we suggested for air-gapped on-prem installs.

Short technical notes (for the workshop):
- Connection model: outbound-only via a hardened proxy appliance or NAT gateway. We'll provide a small agent that initiates connections to Redwood telemetry endpoints and model update buckets — that agent can be run inside your subnet with no inbound rules required.
- Ports/protocols: primarily 443/TLS for inference and S3-compatible endpoints for model sync. If you require mutual TLS for model fetch, we support mTLS and can provide cert rotation guidance.
- KMS flow: we support customer-managed keys via PKCS#11-compatible HSMs. We'll circulate our KMS integration doc pre-work so your security team can validate key lifecycle policies.
- Acceptance artifacts: sample audit evidence list is included in the checklist (SOC2 evidence pointers, network ACL screenshots, KMS config snippets).

Action items I will take before Wed:
- Share the completed acceptance checklist (attached)
- Drop a short tech pre-read (2 pages) describing the agent behavior and cert flow
- Propose calendar invite for Wed Apr 21 13:00–14:00 PST

Marissa
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachments listed: redwood_onprem_acceptance_checklist.xlsx

On Sat, Apr 18, 2026 at 11:03 AM Priya Menon <priya.menon@novuscare.com> wrote:
> Logan — thanks for the clear recap and for capturing the constraints. A few clarifications from our side:...

From: Priya Menon <priya.menon@novuscare.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Carl Jensen <cjensen@novuscare.com>, NovusCare Security <security@novuscare.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Apr 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: NovusCare: on-prem connectivity decisions & next actions

Marissa / Logan — thanks for turning this around quickly. Confirming: Wed Apr 21 13:00–14:00 PST on my calendar (security alias invited).

Before the workshop, please include in the checklist a clear list of the minimum evidence items our auditors will ask for (we call these Tier 1 items). Also: can you attach a high-level SOW stub or cost band we can drop into procurement? If you can provide a two-line pricing range for an air-gapped deployment (one-time setup + monthly support), that will materially help us hit the PO deadline (05/27).

We'd prefer the DocuSign flow if possible — please include a link to a preliminary SOW or estimate in the follow-up.

Priya

Before the workshop, please include in the checklist a clear list of the minimum evidence items our auditors will ask for (we call these Tier 1 items). Also: can you attach a high-level SOW stub or cost band we can drop into procurement? If you can provide a two-line pricing range for an air-gapped deployment (one-time setup + monthly support), that will materially help us hit the PO deadline (05/27).

We'd prefer the DocuSign flow if possible — please include a link to a preliminary SOW or estimate in the follow-up.

Priya

text:
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Priya Menon <priya.menon@novuscare.com>, Carl Jensen <cjensen@novuscare.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: NovusCare: on-prem connectivity decisions & next actions

Hi Priya / Carl — thanks again for the time today. Quick written recap so we have a shared starting point and clear next actions.

Call highlights / pain:
- Regulated PHI handling: NovusCare requires data to remain on-prem (no multi-tenant cloud residency). Primary driver is state-level patient data residency plus upcoming audit window (Q3).
- Air-gap requirements for the inference service: security team prefers outbound-restricted deployment with limited egress to Redwood (allowlist) for telemetry and patching; fully inbound-open endpoints are not acceptable.
- KMS integration: you need HSM-backed key management (customer-managed) and key rotation controls.
- Maintenance & procurement: hardware timeline is tight — procurement target is end of May; IT can only schedule network changes during weekend maintenance windows.

Technical constraints mentioned:
- No persistent public IPs on the inference nodes; outbound-only NAT via approved proxy (one egress IP range).
- All traffic must be TLS 1.2+; port 443 only for inference traffic. No SSH from internet.
- CIDR ranges are v4-only; we will need the specific subnets and firewall ACLs to build the acceptance checklist.

Agreed next steps (owners & rough ETA):
1) Logan: share a succinct acceptance checklist + sample network diagram by EOD today (attached: network map stub).
2) Marissa: schedule a 60-minute technical workshop with your NetOps & Security to walk the connection pattern and KMS flow (proposed next-week windows below).
3) Priya/Carl: confirm the allowed egress IP ranges and give the procurement target date for on-prem hardware (we noted end of May).
4) Ben: start the commercial conversation (SOW estimate + licensing options) once we confirm the deployment topology.

Attachments: novuscare_network_map.png (high-level sketch), redwood_onprem_acceptance_checklist.xlsx (initial checklist).

Proposed workshop slots (PST):
- Tue Apr 20: 09:00–10:00 or 15:00–16:00
- Wed Apr 21: 08:00–09:00 or 13:00–14:00

If any of the bullets above are incorrect or missing priorities, call them out and I'll update. Thanks again — Logan

--
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
logan@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0184


From: Priya Menon <priya.menon@novuscare.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Carl Jensen <cjensen@novuscare.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, NovusCare Security <security@novuscare.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: NovusCare: on-prem connectivity decisions & next actions

Logan — thanks for the clear recap and for capturing the constraints. A few clarifications from our side:

- Correct on the residency requirement — data for the clinical model must remain within our on-prem cluster. Backup metadata (not PHI) may be allowed to land in encrypted object storage but only under strict controls; we'll confirm scope.
- Regarding egress: security will only approve outbound to a small, static IP set or via an approved proxy appliance. We will not permit direct inbound access from the internet to the inference nodes.
- KMS: we are evaluating two options — customer HSM (preferred) or cloud KMS with strict key escrow policies. We will share the org's KMS decision by Apr 28.
- Procurement: procurement window is tight. Hardware PO is targeted for 05/27; we need Redwood's installation/support SOW and rough cost delta (air-gapped pricing) to include in procurement package.

Workshop: Wed Apr 21 13:00–14:00 PST works best for the security + NetOps folks. Please send an invite to the security alias and Carl.

Can you also add a short section in the acceptance checklist that lists what evidence artifacts we will need for the upcoming audit (SOC/HIPAA baseline)? That will help our compliance lead.

Thanks — Priya
Director of IT, NovusCare
priya.menon@novuscare.com | +1 617-555-0199

- Connection model: outbound-only via a hardened proxy appliance or NAT gateway. We'll provide a small agent that initiates connections to Redwood telemetry endpoints and model update buckets — that agent can be run inside your subnet with no inbound rules required.
- Ports/protocols: primarily 443/TLS for inference and S3-compatible endpoints for model sync. If you require mutual TLS for model fetch, we support mTLS and can provide cert rotation guidance.
- KMS flow: we support customer-managed keys via PKCS#11-compatible HSMs. We'll circulate our KMS integration doc pre-work so your security team can validate key lifecycle policies.
- Acceptance artifacts: sample audit evidence list is included in the checklist (SOC2 evidence pointers, network ACL screenshots, KMS config snippets).

Action items I will take before Wed:
- Share the completed acceptance checklist (attached)
- Drop a short tech pre-read (2 pages) describing the agent behavior and cert flow
- Propose calendar invite for Wed Apr 21 13:00–14:00 PST

Marissa
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

source.subject: Post-call: GenBio on-prem air-gapped deployment — transition & hardware constraints
source.thread:
From: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@genbio.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Sara Lin <sara.lin@genbio.com>; procurement@genbio.com
Date: 2026-04-11 14:02 PDT
Subject: Re: Discovery call — on-prem requirements (recap)

Thanks for the walkthrough today, Logan — helpful discussion. To recap our high-level constraints and questions: 

- GenBio will be air-gapped for PHI/clinical data processing in our Cambridge lab cluster. No outbound internet from the inference nodes; controlled egress via a jump host.
- We expect a sustained baseline inference load equivalent to ~1200 context tokens/sec (model: LlamaX-13B) with peaks ~3x for batch offline runs.
- We need a formal maintenance/upgrade window policy because our compliance team requires quarterly patching and an explicit pre-approved change window.
- Spares and replacement parts: we can only keep minimal spares on-site (2U of hot-swap NVMe + 1 spare GPU), anything beyond that must be delivered by Redwoods-approved courier with on-site techs approved in advance.

Questions we still have:
1) Can Redwood support a fully offline install (no outbound telemetry) while still providing security evidence (hashes, signed binaries) for verification?
2) How do you handle kernel/firmware updates in air-gapped environments — will you bundle recommended patches and provide a full upgrade staging plan?
3) What does the acceptance criteria for an on-prem handoff look like (operational runbook + test workload acceptance)?

Attaching the rack diagram and the high-level inventory for the initial cluster. Happy to confirm times for next steps.

—Tom
CTO, GenBio Therapeutics


On Mon, Apr 11, 2026 at 14:25 Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com> wrote:

> From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
> To: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@genbio.com>
> Cc: Sara Lin <sara.lin@genbio.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference.com>, procurement@genbio.com
> Date: 2026-04-11 14:25 PDT
> Subject: Re: Discovery call — on-prem requirements (recap)
>
> Tom — thanks for the clear recap. Below is our post-call summary and immediate next steps. I put the acceptance criteria and upgrade flow up front so your compliance team can see it quickly.
>
> Summary (what we heard)
> - Air-gapped, no outbound traffic from inference hosts. Jump-host pattern for controlled egress.
> - Baseline steady-state throughput ~1200 tokens/sec, peak ~3600 tokens/sec during batch windows.
> - Minimal on-site spares; delivery and on-site techs must be pre-authorized.
> - Quarterly maintenance windows required; any emergency patching must be coordinated.
>
> Acceptance criteria for handoff (minimum)
> 1) Successful end-to-end inference test: run our test harness (we'll ship a signed binary) against a representative dataset for 48 hours with no OOMs and <150ms p50 token latency for small requests.
> 2) Runbook delivered and validated: operator guide + offline upgrade instructions + rollback procedure.
> 3) Audit artifacts: signed checksums for binaries, firmware images, and an inventory manifest.
>
> Upgrade and patch process (air-gapped)> - We’ll provide a staged upgrade bundle (signed and checksummed) that you ingest through the jump host.
> - Pre-stage on a non-production node, run the Redwood validation harness, sign off, then schedule production window.
> - For kernel/firmware updates that require physical reboots, we will coordinate with your on-site tech and provide a detailed MOP (method of procedure).
>
> Spares & logistics> - We can accept the minimal-spares approach. For anything beyond the listed on-site spare (1 GPU + NVMe spare), we will plan courier deliveries with pre-approved tech clearance.
>
> Immediate next steps (action owners & dates)
> - Logan / Redwood: produce a first-pass SOW and the upgrade MOP by 2026-04-15 (attachment: proposed-sow-genbio.docx).
> - Karthik: prepare a short hardware-compatibility checklist focused on kernel/BIOS/firmware combinations we support (due 2026-04-14).
> - Tom / GenBio: share the final rack-elevation and assigned on-site contact for courier authorization (due 2026-04-13).
>
> Notes: We can support options for Dedicated capacity or hybrid mode if you want a non-air-gapped staging cluster in the cloud for canary/validation runs. I flagged that as optional in the SOW.
>
> Will follow up with SOW + MOP in the next two business days.
>
> Cheers,
> Logan
> Solutions Lead — Redwood Inference
> mobile: +1 (415) 555-0199
>
> Attachment stubs: proposed-sow-genbio.docx (docx, 128KB), redwood_onprem_requirements_v1.pdf (pdf, 420KB)


On Mon, Apr 11, 2026 at 15:10 Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com> wrote:

> From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
> To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference.com>
> Cc: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@genbio.com>, Sara Lin <sara.lin@genbio.com>, procurement@genbio.com
> Date: 2026-04-11 15:10 PDT
> Subject: Re: Discovery call — on-prem requirements (recap)
>
> Quick operational notes from my side:
> - We will tag the upgrade MOP with exact firmware versions we validate. If GenBio wants a longer maintenance window for same-day rollback, we should capture that in the SOW options.
> - Spare policy: propose SLA-backed courier (4–8 hour delivery) for critical GPUs; we’ll include a local-equivalent pricing line item in the SOW.
> - For compliance evidence: we can provide signed binary artifacts + SLSA-like build metadata; need to confirm what level of provenance your auditors need.
>
> I’ll loop in our logistics partner for an initial quote after we get your site contact.
>
> Ben Carter
> Sr. Customer Reliability Engineer — Redwood Inference
> ben.carter@redwood.inference.com
>
>
On Mon, Apr 11, 2026 at 16:45 Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference.com> wrote:

> From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference.com>
> To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
> Cc: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@genbio.com>, Sara Lin <sara.lin@genbio.com>
> Date: 2026-04-11 16:45 PDT
> Subject: Re: Discovery call — on-prem requirements (recap)
>
> Technical clarifications for the MOP/SOW:
> - We need the exact GPU model and BIOS revisions you plan to ship. Our runtime has certified paths for A100 and GH200-class cards; anything older may need a fallback quantization profile.
> - Please confirm whether the jump host will allow rsync/dropbox-style file transfer or only removable-media ingestion workflows. The SOW will include both pathways as templates but we need to know which one you can operationalize.
> - For the validation harness: we will include a small synthetic workload + your representative trace. The 48 hour run in an air-gapped environment should include both steady-state and a single simulated batch spike.
>
> I'll prep the hardware-compatibility checklist and list of firmware/driver combinations we recommend.
>
> Karthik Iyer
> Principal Infra Engineer — Redwood Inference
> karthik.iyer@redwood.inference.com
>
>
-- End of thread --

source.subject: Request: TTFT and sustained-tps carveout for ScoutAI real-time ingestion
messages:
From: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@scoutai.com
Date: Wed, 1 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the low-latency streaming looked very promising. We pushed the first few traces through overnight and found two things that matter for our production PoC:

- We need a consistent time-to-first-token (TTFT) under ~150ms for short-turn utterances (20-40 tokens) to keep downstream speaker-turn detection reliable.
- For longer inference sessions we require a sustained token emission of ~60 tokens/sec for the steady-state agent workload.

Given our current usage profile (bursts up to 120 concurrent streams, median request length 150 tokens), our cost envelope doesn't allow us to move to a fully Dedicated cluster right now. Can Redwood provide a temporary SLA carveout or exception for the hosted streaming endpoint to meet those TTFT/TPS numbers during our 90-day pilot? We can provide sample traces (attached) and are prepared to sign an addendum for a pilot-level credit if that helps.

Attached: scoutai_latency-trace-20260401.zip

Thanks,
Maria Lopez
Head of Platform, ScoutAI
maria@scoutai.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

---

Note: procurement@scoutai.com is looped for contract/PO coordination.

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 1 Apr 2026 12:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Maria — appreciate the clear ask and the traces. Quick read-through: the traces show a mix of short-turn calls and sustained agent sessions which explains the competing TTFT vs sustained throughput requirements.

We can do a short-term carveout, but there are a couple of tradeoffs and ops constraints I want to surface up front:

1) Hosted tier guarantees are calibrated for median workloads; hard real-time guarantees (TTFT <150ms) at scale typically require reserved capacity or a hybrid config (prefix-cached + burst pool).
2) We can offer a 90-day pilot SLA addendum with a scoped TTFT/TPS commitment for a limited pool (up to 60 concurrent streams) with fallback to best-effort if your bursts exceed that.
3) To make this safe for other tenants, we'd ask to:
   - Enable prefix caching for your common prompts
   - Route your critical streams through a latency-priority pool (no model-shift during pilot)
   - Share key telemetry (p99 TTFT, sustained tokens/sec) daily for the pilot period

If ScoutAI accepts those controls, I can put together an internal approval packet (SRE/Contracts) and a draft addendum for signature. Jordan — can you weigh in on whether our latency-priority pool can be carved out on hosted for 60 streams and what the monitoring/rollback looks like? Monica, please tag any account constraints (credits, legal redlines).

Thanks — I’ll aim to summarize into a single addendum draft once I have SRE/Contracts feedback.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
logan@redwoodinference.com
(650) 555-0112

Attachment: pilot_run_plan.xlsx

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 2 Apr 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Looping in SRE and Contracts (cc'd below internally — we will not include external SRE in this thread until we have an agreed scope). Quick notes from infra perspective:

- We can provision a latency-priority pool on hosted by reserving a small fraction of preemptible GPUs + one non-preemptible node per 20 streams. For 60 streams that's achievable for 90 days with an ops-runbook.
- Expected p95 TTFT: 120-170ms depending on input variability; p99 will hover around 250-400ms for worst-case fused prompts. We can meet the 150ms TTFT target for p50 and p90 with the caching + priority pool; p99 guarantee will be tougher without full Dedicated.
- Sustained token/sec: we can cap and shape to ~60 tokens/sec per active stream if you accept a soft cap that triggers graceful degradation (drop to 30 tokens/sec) when the pool hits 85% utilization.

Operational controls we'll require in the addendum:
- Scoped concurrency (max 60 concurrent priority streams)
- Telemetry sharing (daily p50/p90/p99 TTFT and tokens/sec)
- Auto-fallback rules (what triggers degrade and how to notify)
- Pilot termination triggers (if latency/regression >X for 3 days)

Monica — if Sales is on board, I can draft the SLO language for Contracts to review.

— Jordan
Jordan Blake
Principal SRE Liaison, Redwood Inference

From: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: ScoutAI Procurement <procurement@scoutai.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Apr 2026 14:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Thanks — this looks reasonable and in line with what our eng team expects. A few clarifying points before we sign an addendum:

- Concurrency: we can limit the latency-priority traffic client-side to 60 streams for the pilot (we'll enforce at the SDK level).
- Degrade behavior: acceptable, provided we get a clear on-call notification and a 20-minute SLA for mitigation actions when priority pool utilization crosses 85%.
- Telemetry: we'll give you daily traces and can surface aggregated p50/p90/p99 via our internal dashboard (we'll share a read-only link).

If you can share a draft addendum with those controls and the proposed pilot credit, procurement will fast-track review. HubSpot deal: SBX-44923.

Thanks,
Maria

Attachment: none

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>, ScoutAI Procurement <procurement@scoutai.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 4 Apr 2026 17:38:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC — Draft Addendum + Next Steps

Maria — attaching a first-pass SLA addendum and the pilot run plan. Key points in the draft:

- Scope: 90-day pilot, latency-priority pool capped at 60 concurrent streams.
- TTFT commitments: p50 <120ms, p90 <150ms for requests <=40 tokens; p99 is best-effort and not SLA-backed in hosted tier.
- Sustained tokens/sec: scoped target 60 tokens/sec per stream; automatic shaping to 30 tokens/sec when pool util >85%.
- Credits: 20% service credit for each full day the p90 TTFT target is missed for the pilot (capped at 20% of pilot spend).
- Telemetry & Ops: daily telemetry feed and a 20-minute on-call notification window when utilization crosses 85% (manual mitigation thereafter).

Next steps:
1) Maria / procurement — confirm you accept the scope above and I will route the addendum to Contracts for review.
2) If accepted, sign DocuSign (link included) and we will schedule a one-hour runbook walkthrough with SRE.

Attachments: SLA_addendum_draft.docx, pilot_run_plan.xlsx
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/sign/ABCD-EXAMPLE
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/44923

— Logan
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Logan Wright | Redwood Inference
logan@redwoodinference.com | (650) 555-0112

- Telemetry: we'll give you daily traces and can surface aggregated p50/p90/p99 via our internal dashboard (we'll share a read-only link).

If you can share a draft addendum with those controls and the proposed pilot credit, procurement will fast-track review. HubSpot deal: SBX-44923.

Thanks,
Maria

Attachment: none

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>, ScoutAI Procurement <procurement@scoutai.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 4 Apr 2026 17:38:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC — Draft Addendum + Next Steps

Maria — attaching a first-pass SLA addendum and the pilot run plan. Key points in the draft:

- Scope: 90-day pilot, latency-priority pool capped at 60 concurrent streams.
- TTFT commitments: p50 <120ms, p90 <150ms for requests <=40 tokens; p99 is best-effort and not SLA-backed in hosted tier.
- Sustained tokens/sec: scoped target 60 tokens/sec per stream; automatic shaping to 30 tokens/sec when pool util >85%.
- Credits: 20% service credit for each full day the p90 TTFT target is missed for the pilot (capped at 20% of pilot spend).
- Telemetry & Ops: daily telemetry feed and a 20-minute on-call notification window when utilization crosses 85% (manual mitigation thereafter).

Next steps:
1) Maria / procurement — confirm you accept the scope above and I will route the addendum to Contracts for review.
2) If accepted, sign DocuSign (link included) and we will schedule a one-hour runbook walkthrough with SRE.

Attachments: SLA_addendum_draft.docx, pilot_run_plan.xlsx
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/sign/ABCD-EXAMPLE
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/44923

— Logan
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Logan Wright | Redwood Inference
logan@redwoodinference.com | (650) 555-0112
text:
From: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@scoutai.com
Date: Wed, 1 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the low-latency streaming looked very promising. We pushed the first few traces through overnight and found two things that matter for our production PoC:

- We need a consistent time-to-first-token (TTFT) under ~150ms for short-turn utterances (20-40 tokens) to keep downstream speaker-turn detection reliable.
- For longer inference sessions we require a sustained token emission of ~60 tokens/sec for the steady-state agent workload.

Given our current usage profile (bursts up to 120 concurrent streams, median request length 150 tokens), our cost envelope doesn't allow us to move to a fully Dedicated cluster right now. Can Redwood provide a temporary SLA carveout or exception for the hosted streaming endpoint to meet those TTFT/TPS numbers during our 90-day pilot? We can provide sample traces (attached) and are prepared to sign an addendum for a pilot-level credit if that helps.

Attached: scoutai_latency-trace-20260401.zip

Thanks,
Maria Lopez
Head of Platform, ScoutAI
maria@scoutai.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

---

Note: procurement@scoutai.com is looped for contract/PO coordination.

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 1 Apr 2026 12:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Maria — appreciate the clear ask and the traces. Quick read-through: the traces show a mix of short-turn calls and sustained agent sessions which explains the competing TTFT vs sustained throughput requirements.

We can do a short-term carveout, but there are a couple of tradeoffs and ops constraints I want to surface up front:

1) Hosted tier guarantees are calibrated for median workloads; hard real-time guarantees (TTFT <150ms) at scale typically require reserved capacity or a hybrid config (prefix-cached + burst pool).
2) We can offer a 90-day pilot SLA addendum with a scoped TTFT/TPS commitment for a limited pool (up to 60 concurrent streams) with fallback to best-effort if your bursts exceed that.
3) To make this safe for other tenants, we'd ask to:
   - Enable prefix caching for your common prompts
   - Route your critical streams through a latency-priority pool (no model-shift during pilot)
   - Share key telemetry (p99 TTFT, sustained tokens/sec) daily for the pilot period

If ScoutAI accepts those controls, I can put together an internal approval packet (SRE/Contracts) and a draft addendum for signature. Jordan — can you weigh in on whether our latency-priority pool can be carved out on hosted for 60 streams and what the monitoring/rollback looks like? Monica, please tag any account constraints (credits, legal redlines).

Thanks — I’ll aim to summarize into a single addendum draft once I have SRE/Contracts feedback.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
logan@redwoodinference.com
(650) 555-0112

Attachment: pilot_run_plan.xlsx

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 2 Apr 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: TTFT and sustained TPS carveout for ScoutAI PoC

Looping in SRE and Contracts (cc'd below internally — we will not include external SRE in this thread until we have an agreed scope). Quick notes from infra perspective:

- We can provision a latency-priority pool on hosted by reserving a small fraction of preemptible GPUs + one non-preemptible node per 20 streams. For 60 streams that's achievable for 90 days with an ops-runbook.
- Expected p95 TTFT: 120-170ms depending on input variability; p99 will hover around 250-400ms for worst-case fused prompts. We can meet the 150ms TTFT target for p50 and p90 with the caching + priority pool; p99 guarantee will be tougher without full Dedicated.
- Sustained token/sec: we can cap and shape to ~60 tokens/sec per active stream if you accept a soft cap that triggers graceful degradation (drop to 30 tokens/sec) when the pool hits 85% utilization.

Operational controls we'll require in the addendum:
- Scoped concurrency (max 60 concurrent priority streams)
- Telemetry sharing (daily p50/p90/p99 TTFT and tokens/sec)
- Auto-fallback rules (what triggers degrade and how to notify)
- Pilot termination triggers (if latency/regression >X for 3 days)

Monica — if Sales is on board, I can draft the SLO language for Contracts to review.

— Jordan
Jordan Blake
Principal SRE Liaison, Redwood Inference

From: Maria Lopez <maria@scoutai.com>

messages:
From: Morgan Ellis <morgan@novahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Ravi Patel <ravi.patel@novahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot: MAP and concrete acceptance tests

Logan —

Thanks for the demo last week. The execs at NovaHealth want a short, measurable mutual action plan we can use to greenlight a pilot and make a close decision. High level asks from our side: 

- Performance: p95 inference latency <= 200ms for 512-token clinical prompts (baseline traffic ~10 qps).
- Reliability: no more than one incident requiring manual failover during a 2-week trial.
- Functional: named-entity extraction F1 >= 0.90 on the lab-results dataset (we'll provide a 500-record labeled sample).
- Security/compliance: data-in-transit + at-rest with retention policy controls, audit logs for requests.

Can you send a MAP that maps these to concrete acceptance tests and owners? We also need a realistic close timeline and a runbook for smoke tests (what success looks like on day 1, day 7, day 14).

Procurement/contract: Ravi will drive PO timing, but please include the procurement checkpoint and a DocuSign step in the plan.

Attached: none (we'll upload the labeled sample to the shared drive once we have the trial plan).

Thanks,
Morgan
Product Lead, NovaHealth
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Morgan Ellis <morgan@novahealth.com>
Cc: Ravi Patel <ravi.patel@novahealth.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2026 13:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot: MAP and concrete acceptance tests

Morgan —

Appreciate the clarity. I've sketched a first-pass MAP and an acceptance-test matrix that ties each success signal to an owner and an evidence artifact. Key points in the draft:

1) Timeline (two-week pilot):
   - Day 0: onboarding + test dataset ingestion (NovaHealth uploads labeled sample to shared drive).
   - Day 1: smoke runbook executed by Redwood CS + NovaHealth (latency and end-to-end sanity checks).
   - Day 7: mid-pilot regression run (functional metrics on 250 records).
   - Day 14: full evaluation + sign-off & procurement checkpoint (PO or DocuSign).

2) Acceptance tests (examples):
   - Latency: automated load script (10 qps sustained, 512-token median) -> p95 <= 200ms (owner: Infra SE, Avery Johnson). Evidence: load test report (CSV).
   - Accuracy: evaluation harness runs against labeled sample -> entity extraction F1 >= 0.90 (owner: CS, Grace Oconnor). Evidence: eval report (JSON/CSV).
   - Reliability: 14-day incident log with no manual failovers (owner: Support). Evidence: incidents.csv + postmortem if any.
   - Security: audit-log delivery + retention config validated (owner: Security/Eng). Evidence: audit log sample + retention policy doc.

I've attached the MAP spreadsheet (MAP_NovaHealth_v0.1.xlsx) and a smoke-test matrix (nova-smoke-test-matrix.csv). Both are drafts — please review and mark any must-have changes.

Links: Drive folder with test-data + draft MAP: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2bExampleDriveLink
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/1234567

Next steps: propose a 30m sync on Wed 10:00am PT to walk the MAP and lock owners. If that works for you I'll send a calendar invite.

—Logan
Lead Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Morgan Ellis <morgan@novahealth.com>, Ravi Patel <ravi.patel@novahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2026 09:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Pilot: MAP and concrete acceptance tests — action items for infra & CS

Avery / Grace —

Forwarding Morgan's note and the MAP draft I shared. Quick asks to prepare before Wed's sync: 

Avery (Infra):
 - Build the 10 qps load script (512-token sim). Prefer k6 or locust; we need a reproducible config and a p95 metric.
 - Create an evidence artifact template (load-report.csv) and ensure we can run it from our dedicated GPU pool.

Grace (CS):
 - Prepare the eval harness to run the 500-record labeled sample. Provide a script that outputs F1, precision, recall, and a confusion breakdown (eval-report.csv).
 - Draft the smoke runbook (day 0 checks) with clear pass/fail gates; include contact list and rollback steps.

Attachments: MAP_NovaHealth_v0.1.xlsx (draft), nova-smoke-test-matrix.csv (draft).

Quote of Morgan's original: 
> Logan —
> 
> Thanks for the demo last week. The execs at NovaHealth want a short, measurable mutual action plan we can use to greenlight a pilot and make a close decision. High level asks from our side: 
> 
> - Performance: p95 inference latency <= 200ms for 512-token clinical prompts (baseline traffic ~10 qps).
> - Reliability: no more than one incident requiring manual failover during a 2-week trial.
> - Functional: named-entity extraction F1 >= 0.90 on the lab-results dataset (we'll provide a 500-record labeled sample).
> - Security/compliance: data-in-transit + at-rest with retention policy controls, audit logs for requests.
> 
I'll follow up with calendar invites and link the Drive folder. Please flag any blockers in the morning standup.

Thanks — Logan
From: Morgan Ellis <morgan@novahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Ravi Patel <ravi.patel@novahealth.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot: MAP and concrete acceptance tests

Logan / team —

Thanks for the fast turnaround and the draft MAP. A few clarifications before Wed: 

- Procurement: add a PO timing checkpoint between Day 7 and Day 14 (Ravi will confirm exact lead time). Include DocuSign link in the Day 14 checklist.
- SSO / SAML: we need an acceptance test for SSO provisioning to ensure our users can sign in (owner: Infra). Evidence: SSO test user sign-in log (sso-test.log).
- Data residency: confirm that logs and audit artifacts for the pilot will be retained in us-east-1.

If you can add these three items to the MAP and re-share by tomorrow morning we'll be ready to run the Day 0 smoke tests next week. Wed 10:00am PT works for the sync — please send the invite.

Morgan
Product Lead, NovaHealth

- SSO / SAML: we need an acceptance test for SSO provisioning to ensure our users can sign in (owner: Infra). Evidence: SSO test user sign-in log (sso-test.log).
- Data residency: confirm that logs and audit artifacts for the pilot will be retained in us-east-1.

If you can add these three items to the MAP and re-share by tomorrow morning we'll be ready to run the Day 0 smoke tests next week. Wed 10:00am PT works for the sync — please send the invite.

Morgan
Product Lead, NovaHealth

source.subject: Post-call: SLO checklist and test plan for Redwood vs MajorCloud
messages:
From: Alex Russell <alexr@acme-analytics.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Thanks for the demo — questions on uptime, rate limits, failover

Hi Logan / Marissa,

Thanks for the walkthrough earlier — appreciate the performance numbers you showed for the 8k-context model. We’re running a head-to-head evaluation across a couple of inference providers (MajorCloud, QuickServe, plus Redwood) and I want to make sure our next-step testing exercise captures the things that matter for production.

High-level asks from our side for the test plan:
- Clear uptime/SLA commitments and what’s covered vs edge cases (maintenance windows, regional disruptions)
- Token- and request-rate limits per API key / account and graceful behavior when limits are hit
- Observability hooks: latency percentiles, per-route error rates, and detailed error codes for retries
- Failover behavior: cross-region failover time and how session/KV cache is preserved (if at all)
- Any soft-limits or backoff behavior we should be aware of

Can you send a short checklist we can use to validate providers? Also, procurement (cc’d) will ask for an SLO matrix and a link to SOC/compliance docs. If you can provide a quick test window (we’re thinking 2 weeks) and the suggested test scenarios, we’ll schedule the load tests on our side.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Russell
Head of Platform Engineering
Acme Analytics

---
Attachments: MajorCloud_eval_notes.pdf (pdf)
From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Alex Russell <alexr@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-15T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Thanks for the demo — questions on uptime, rate limits, failover

Alex — great to hear, and thanks for outlining the validation needs. Short recap from our call plus the checklist and proposed tests.

Quick call recap (2 lines): Redwood provides configurable SLOs per-tenant (uptime, p95/p99 latency targets) with documented failover policies that can route to a compatible quantized variant in another region. We also expose token-level billing and per-key QPS throttles with clear error codes.

Checklist / test plan (suggested, high priority first):
1) Uptime & failover
   - Verify control-plane and data-plane uptime: attempt regional failover during a simulated AZ/region outage and measure time-to-first-successful-request (expected < 30s for our standard failover policy).
   - Confirm retention of KV/prefix cache semantics: cache warm vs cold behavior post-failover.
2) Rate limits & throttling
   - Ramp single API key to peak sustained QPS for 30 minutes and measure error codes and retry headers (429/Retry-After behavior).
   - Multi-key burst test to surface global account quotas and per-route shaping.
3) Error budget & graceful degradation
   - Induce degraded CPU on a dedicated worker pool and validate automatic routing to lower-cost variant and per-token cost signals.
   - Observe how errors are surfaced (status codes, descriptive messages) for specific failure modes.
4) Observability & telemetry
   - Validate p50/p95/p99 latency reporting in Console for the test period and trace-level sample pulls for failed requests.
   - Confirm log retention window and ability to pull request-level transcripts for a 30-day window (we can provide extra retention if needed).

Deliverables we will attach: SLO comparison spreadsheet (rwd_slo-comparison-20260315.xlsx), short failover diagram (redwood-failover-architecture.png), link to SOC2 and penetration test summary.

Proposed schedule:
- Test window: 2026-03-20 to 2026-04-03 (2 weeks) — please confirm if that works for your team.
- Redwood will provide a small test account + API keys and a short runbook for each test.

Next steps (action items):
- Alex: confirm test window and list the critical workloads (model + avg context length) you want us to prioritize.
- Procurement: we’ll share the SLO matrix and links to compliance docs once NDA/standard engagement form is in place.
- Logan/Marissa/Vivek: prep the SLO matrix and runbook; I’ll schedule a pre-test sync for 03/19.

Thanks — I’ll attach the SLO matrix now.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-15T12:14:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: pricing/credits and SLA notes for Acme

Internal note — copy of what to be prepared to answer for procurement. Do not forward without review.

Summary: Acme will use inference for near-real-time analytics pipelines (high sustained QPS, sensitive to p99 latency). They will run parallel tests against MajorCloud; expect procurement to ask for committed tier pricing and a short credit for performance testing.

Key points for negotiation:
- Offer: 2-week test credits (we typically offer up to $10k in test credits for this stage) and a one-month free trial of increased retention for logs for debugging.
- SLA ask: they’ll push for 99.95% uptime for production; explain that Dedicated plans can reach 99.95 with reserved capacity, hosted plans are 99.9% with fallback policies.
- Failover proof: provide time-to-failover telemetry for two recent incidents (attach if needed).

Please make sure our SLO matrix clearly states: expected p99 at target load, failure modes covered, and the exact definition of "unavailable" (control-plane vs data-plane).

Vivek
VP Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments referenced internally: rwd_pricing-template.xlsx (xls)
From: Alex Russell <alexr@acme-analytics.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procure@bigcorp.com>
Date: 2026-03-15T14:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Thanks for the demo — questions on uptime, rate limits, failover

Thanks Logan — that checklist looks great and aligns with what we had in mind. A couple of clarifications before we lock the window:

- Failover: when you say "expected < 30s for standard failover policy", can you confirm what stages are excluded (e.g., DNS TTL propagation, client-side caching)? We want to be crystal clear in our scoring rubric.

title: Post-call: SLO checklist and test plan for Redwood vs MajorCloud
preview:
From: Alex Russell <alexr@acme-analytics.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Thanks for the demo — questions on uptime, rate limits, failover

Hi Logan / Marissa,

Thanks for the walkthrough earlier — appreciate the performance numbers you showed for the 8k-context model. We’re running a head-to-head evaluation across a couple of inference providers (MajorCloud, QuickServe, plus Redwood) and I want to make sure our next-step testing exercise captures the things that matter for production.

High-level asks from our side for the test plan:
- Clear uptime/SLA commitments and what’s covered vs edge cases (maintenance windows, regional disruptions)
- Token- and request-rate limits per API key / account and graceful behavior when limits are hit
- Observability hooks: latency percentiles, per-route error rates, and detailed error codes for retries
- Failover behavior: cross-region failover time and how session/KV cache is preserved (if at all)
- Any soft-limits or backoff behavior we should be aware of

Can you send a short checklist we can use to validate providers? Also, procurement (cc’d) will ask for an SLO matrix and a link to SOC/compliance docs. If you can provide a quick test window (we’re thinking 2 weeks) and the suggested test scenarios, we’ll schedule the load tests on our side.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Russell
Head of Platform Engineering
Acme Analytics

---
Attachments: MajorCloud_eval_notes.pdf (pdf)


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Alex Russell <alexr@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-15T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Thanks for the demo — questions on uptime, rate limits, failover

Alex — great to hear, and thanks for outlining the validation needs. Short recap from our call plus the checklist and proposed tests.

Quick call recap (2 lines): Redwood provides configurable SLOs per-tenant (uptime, p95/p99 latency targets) with documented failover policies that can route to a compatible quantized variant in another region. We also expose token-level billing and per-key QPS throttles with clear error codes.

Checklist / test plan (suggested, high priority first):
1) Uptime & failover
   - Verify control-plane and data-plane uptime: attempt regional failover during a simulated AZ/region outage and measure time-to-first-successful-request (expected < 30s for our standard failover policy).
   - Confirm retention of KV/prefix cache semantics: cache warm vs cold behavior post-failover.
2) Rate limits & throttling
   - Ramp single API key to peak sustained QPS for 30 minutes and measure error codes and retry headers (429/Retry-After behavior).
   - Multi-key burst test to surface global account quotas and per-route shaping.
3) Error budget & graceful degradation
   - Induce degraded CPU on a dedicated worker pool and validate automatic routing to lower-cost variant and per-token cost signals.
   - Observe how errors are surfaced (status codes, descriptive messages) for specific failure modes.
4) Observability & telemetry
   - Validate p50/p95/p99 latency reporting in Console for the test period and trace-level sample pulls for failed requests.
   - Confirm log retention window and ability to pull request-level transcripts for a 30-day window (we can provide extra retention if needed).

Deliverables we will attach: SLO comparison spreadsheet (rwd_slo-comparison-20260315.xlsx), short failover diagram (redwood-failover-architecture.png), link to SOC2 and penetration test summary.

Proposed schedule:
- Test window: 2026-03-20 to 2026-04-03 (2 weeks) — please confirm if that works for your team.
- Redwood will provide a small test account + API keys and a short runbook for each test.

Next steps (action items):
- Alex: confirm test window and list the critical workloads (model + avg context length) you want us to prioritize.
- Procurement: we’ll share the SLO matrix and links to compliance docs once NDA/standard engagement form is in place.
- Logan/Marissa/Vivek: prep the SLO matrix and runbook; I’ll schedule a pre-test sync for 03/19.

Thanks — I’ll attach the SLO matrix now.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-15T12:14:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: pricing/credits and SLA notes for Acme

Internal note — copy of what to be prepared to answer for procurement. Do not forward without review.

Summary: Acme will use inference for near-real-time analytics pipelines (high sustained QPS, sensitive to p99 latency). They will run parallel tests against MajorCloud; expect procurement to ask for committed tier pricing and a short credit for performance testing.

Key points for negotiation:
- Offer: 2-week test credits (we typically offer up to $10k in test credits for this stage) and a one-month free trial of increased retention for logs for debugging.
- SLA ask: they’ll push for 99.95% uptime for production; explain that Dedicated plans can reach 99.95 with reserved capacity, hosted plans are 99.9% with fallback policies.
- Failover proof: provide time-to-failover telemetry for two recent incidents (attach if needed).

Please make sure our SLO matrix clearly states: expected p99 at target load, failure modes covered, and the exact definition of "unavailable" (control-plane vs data-plane).

Vivek
VP Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference
