From: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@futurestage.co>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: contracts@futurestage.co
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 08:14:00 -0800
Subject: Platinum+ prospectus and speaking slot availability (DevScale West)

Hi Logan —

Thanks again for the call last week. Attaching the Platinum+ prospectus for DevScale West (early May). Key highlights that map to your asks: a 20'x20' lounge footprint (premium corner), keynote bloc (20 minute slot + 10 Q&A), two dedicated demo pods, and prominent stage backdrop logo placement.

We're holding two keynote slots for sponsors the week of Mar 9 — one is tentatively reserved for you pending contract and final speaker bio. I attached a draft SLA and the expo floor png for booth placement context.

Can you confirm the final decision timeline and whether Redwood intends to request exclusive demo hours? That impacts our AV staffing and load-in scheduling.

Attachments: FS-PlatinumPlus-Prospectus-2026.pdf; FS-Draft-SLA-Redlines.docx; expo-floor-BoothB4-v3.png

Thanks —
Samantha Reed
Partnerships Lead
FutureStage Events
Samantha.Reed@futurestage.co
+1 415-555-0198
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@futurestage.co>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Platinum+ prospectus and speaking slot availability (DevScale West)

Samantha —

Appreciate the prospectus. Quick answers from our side:
- Timeline: final sign-off by Friday Mar 12 (we have a downstream procurement review).
- Exclusive demo hours: likely yes for a 2-hour window per expo day, but need confirmation on exact start/end times before we commit.
- Speaker: we'll nominate our Head of Inference (Marissa) — can you confirm AV mic/backdrop specs so we can tailor the slides?

Cc'ing Marissa and Tessa to coordinate logistics and the contract redlines. I will also need to know the process and turnaround for DocuSign.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Director, Field Marketing
Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com | +1 650-555-0123
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Mar 2026 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Platinum+ prospectus and speaking slot availability (DevScale West)

Thanks Logan — noted. A few items for internal ops and product:
- Speaker brief: I'll draft a short abstract and bio by Thu Mar 5. Need AV notes: podium mic vs lav and screen resolution (projector or LED wall).
- Demo pods: confirm power (2x 20A circuits per pod), network (private VLAN for demo machines), and a 10' table footprint is tight — request 6' x 3' per pod instead.
- Branding: we should push for backdrop logo at center stage + flyaway logos on session slides. If FutureStage insists on smaller sizing, we need the deliverable proof before print.

Also tagging Stephanie for budget approval on a potential 10% booth install fee and any add-on ticket allotments beyond the included 8 passes.

Marissa Cole
Head of Developer Programs
Redwood Inference
From: contracts@futurestage.co <contracts@futurestage.co>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@futurestage.co>
Date: Wed, 4 Mar 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: FS-Contract Draft / redlines and exhibitor delivery windows

Logan / Marissa —

Please find the contract draft with our standard redlines. Two operational notes called out inside:
1) Load-in windows: earliest Monday May 4 08:00; heavy rigging needs to be scheduled through our production partner (contact on p.3).
2) Insurance: we require a Certificate of Insurance (COI) listing FutureStage as additional insured 30 days prior to load-in.

We can flex on the stage logo sizing (section 4.2) — normally center logo is 60% width; we can accept 50% with a proof before print.

DocuSign envelope created: https://app.docusign.com/envelope/status/ABC123FS
Attachments referenced: FS-Draft-SLA-Redlines.docx; FS-Exhibitor-Specs.pdf

Best,
FutureStage Events — Contracts Team
From: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Mar 2026 10:40:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: FS-Contract Draft / redlines and exhibitor delivery windows

Looping back with procurement notes from my end:
- COI: procurement can produce an interim COI but needs the full addl insured language — ask FutureStage to send sample verbiage to our broker (I'll send broker contact).
- Payment terms: standard is Net 30 after invoice; FutureStage's draft has Net 45 and a 50% deposit milestone. We can accept 50% deposit but push Net 30.
- Cancellation: push to 90-day refund window for force majeure tied to public health or travel restrictions.

Action items for team:
- Marissa: confirm exact pod footprint by Tue Mar 9.
- Logan: get final DocuSign link and confirm keynote slot hold time.
- Stephanie: quick approval on an estimated +10% install fee if required.

Thanks —
Tessa Morgan
Senior Events Program Manager
Redwood Inference
From: Aaron Li <aaron.li@devcon.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@futurestage.co>
Cc: contracts@futurestage.co
Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 17:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Platinum+ prospectus and speaking slot availability (DevScale West)

Hi all —

Confirming the keynote slot remains tentatively held for Redwood until Mar 12 23:59 PT. We can accept 50% deposit today to lock the Platinum+ package and will route the DocuSign once the redline items are resolved.

Per Logan's request: AV spec = podium mic + lav available, LED wall 3840x2160; backstage monitor for speaker notes available. Load-in for booth B4 is May 4 08:00 — heavy rigging must be arranged with Production contact (listed in the contract).

I'll follow up with the DocuSign status and a final logo proof deadline (Mar 18) for print production.

Best,
Aaron Li
Operations Director, DevScale (FutureStage Events)

Can you confirm the final decision timeline and whether Redwood intends to request exclusive demo hours? That impacts our AV staffing and load-in scheduling.

Attachments: FS-PlatinumPlus-Prospectus-2026.pdf; FS-Draft-SLA-Redlines.docx; expo-floor-BoothB4-v3.png

Thanks —
Samantha Reed
Partnerships Lead
FutureStage Events
Samantha.Reed@futurestage.co
+1 415-555-0198


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@futurestage.co>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Platinum+ prospectus and speaking slot availability (DevScale West)

Samantha —

Appreciate the prospectus. Quick answers from our side:
- Timeline: final sign-off by Friday Mar 12 (we have a downstream procurement review).
- Exclusive demo hours: likely yes for a 2-hour window per expo day, but need confirmation on exact start/end times before we commit.
- Speaker: we'll nominate our Head of Inference (Marissa) — can you confirm AV mic/backdrop specs so we can tailor the slides?

Cc'ing Marissa and Tessa to coordinate logistics and the contract redlines. I will also need to know the process and turnaround for DocuSign.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Director, Field Marketing
Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com | +1 650-555-0123


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Mar 2026 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Platinum+ prospectus and speaking slot availability (DevScale West)

Thanks Logan — noted. A few items for internal ops and product:
- Speaker brief: I'll draft a short abstract and bio by Thu Mar 5. Need AV notes: podium mic vs lav and screen resolution (projector or LED wall).
- Demo pods: confirm power (2x 20A circuits per pod), network (private VLAN for demo machines), and a 10' table footprint is tight — request 6' x 3' per pod instead.
- Branding: we should push for backdrop logo at center stage + flyaway logos on session slides. If FutureStage insists on smaller sizing, we need the deliverable proof before print.

Also tagging Stephanie for budget approval on a potential 10% booth install fee and any add-on ticket allotments beyond the included 8 passes.

Marissa Cole
Head of Developer Programs
Redwood Inference


From: contracts@futurestage.co <contracts@futurestage.co>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@futurestage.co>
Date: Wed, 4 Mar 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: FS-Contract Draft / redlines and exhibitor delivery windows

Logan / Marissa —

Please find the contract draft with our standard redlines. Two operational notes called out inside:
1) Load-in windows: earliest Monday May 4 08:00; heavy rigging needs to be scheduled through our production partner (contact on p.3).
2) Insurance: we require a Certificate of Insurance (COI) listing FutureStage as additional insured 30 days prior to load-in.

We can flex on the stage logo sizing (section 4.2) — normally center logo is 60% width; we can accept 50% with a proof before print.

DocuSign envelope created: https://app.docusign.com/envelope/status/ABC123FS
Attachments referenced: FS-Draft-SLA-Redlines.docx; FS-Exhibitor-Specs.pdf

Best,
FutureStage Events — Contracts Team


From: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Mar 2026 10:40:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: FS-Contract Draft / redlines and exhibitor delivery windows

Looping back with procurement notes from my end:
- COI: procurement can produce an interim COI but needs the full addl insured language — ask FutureStage to send sample verbiage to our broker (I'll send broker contact).
- Payment terms: standard is Net 30 after invoice; FutureStage's draft has Net 45 and a 50% deposit milestone. We can accept 50% deposit but push Net 30.
- Cancellation: push to 90-day refund window for force majeure tied to public health or travel restrictions.

Action items for team:
- Marissa: confirm exact pod footprint by Tue Mar 9.
- Logan: get final DocuSign link and confirm keynote slot hold time.
- Stephanie: quick approval on an estimated +10% install fee if required.

Thanks —
Tessa Morgan
Senior Events Program Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Aaron Li <aaron.li@devcon.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@futurestage.co>
Cc: contracts@futurestage.co
Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 17:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Platinum+ prospectus and speaking slot availability (DevScale West)

Hi all —

Confirming the keynote slot remains tentatively held for Redwood until Mar 12 23:59 PT. We can accept 50% deposit today to lock the Platinum+ package and will route the DocuSign once the redline items are resolved.

Per Logan's request: AV spec = podium mic + lav available, LED wall 3840x2160; backstage monitor for speaker notes available. Load-in for booth B4 is May 4 08:00 — heavy rigging must be arranged with Production contact (listed in the contract).

I'll follow up with the DocuSign status and a final logo proof deadline (Mar 18) for print production.

Best,
Aaron Li
Operations Director, DevScale (FutureStage Events)

source.subject: Post-call recap & proposed MAP: Riverton Health trial milestones
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@rivertonhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 06 Mar 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap & proposed MAP: Riverton Health trial milestones

Hi Sarah —

Thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap from our discovery so everyone has the same view and a proposed Mutual Action Plan (MAP) to get a pilot booked and resourced.

Key pain / goals you shared
- Current latency spikes on peak hours for triage assistant (avg tail latency ~1.2s, spikes to 4–5s) causing clinician frustration.
- High per-request cost on your current vendor when scaling to batch inference for daily summary reports.
- Data residency: patient data must remain in the US region with audit logging retained for 2 years.
- Success metric: reduce median latency to <=600ms and cost per generated page down by 40% for pilot workload.

Proposed MAP (milestones & owners)
1) Trial scope & infra sign-off — Mar 16, 2026 — Owner: Dr. Evan Morales (Riverton) / Logan (Redwood).
   - Deliverable: scope doc + dataset sample for bench.
2) Security checklist & SOC2 confirmation — Mar 23, 2026 — Owner: Naomi Feldman (Redwood) / Sarah (Riverton).
   - Deliverable: SOC2 summary + enclave configuration cert.
3) Pilot deploy (staging) — Apr 6, 2026 — Owner: Redwood infra + Riverton IT.
   - Deliverable: test harness, baseline latency/cost numbers.
4) Pilot review & go/no-go — Apr 20, 2026 — Owner: Evan + Logan + Monica.
   - Deliverable: comparison report vs current vendor and recommendation.

Stakeholders we should include on the MAP
- Riverton: Sarah Liu (product), Dr. Evan Morales (clinical lead), Procurement (procurement@rivertonhealth.com), IT (for network/VPC), Security.
- Redwood: Logan (AE), Monica Patel (CS lead), Ben Carter (solutions/infra), Naomi Feldman (security/compliance).

Next steps I propose
- I’ll attach a first-draft MAP spreadsheet and our SOC2 summary for quick review (attached).
- Can you confirm Evan and Procurement as approvers and whether Mar 16 works for scope sign-off?
- If procurement prefers, we can loop legal — Naomi is on this thread for any early redlines.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20-minute sync if useful.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Riverton-MAP-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), SOC2-summary.pdf (summary)
From: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@rivertonhealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dr. Evan Morales <evan.morales@rivertonhealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@rivertonhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 07 Mar 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed MAP: Riverton Health trial milestones

Hi Logan —

Appreciate the clear summary — this maps to our conversation. A few clarifications from our side:
- Evan can be the clinical approver; Mar 16 works for a scope-signing touchpoint but he’s out mornings this week — can we aim for a 11:00–12:00 PT window?
- Procurement: we’ve looped procurement in (procurement@rivertonhealth.com). Their initial ask: standard security attestation + draft SOW for cost estimates.
- Data residency: must be enforced at the VPC level; will need sample audit logs during the pilot.

Regarding cost: we’re still comparing a bundled vendor proposal — if your pilot can demonstrate 30–40% lower per-report token cost and stable tail latency under load, we can fast-track the procurement review.

I reviewed the attached MAP; two small edits noted on row 2 (security owner) — I’ll track comments in the spreadsheet and share.

Thanks — I’ll confirm Evan’s availability for Mar 16 by Monday EOD.

—Sarah
Sarah Liu
Sr. Product Manager, CareOps | Riverton Health
sarah.liu@rivertonhealth.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 08 Mar 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: procurement & trial cost sensitives (re: Riverton)

Internal note — quick checklist before we lock dates:
- Procurement likely needs an MSA + SOW estimate before Mar 23. Logan, can you confirm which SOW template (hourly vs consumption) you plan to send? I recommend consumption-based for their use case.
- Naomi: procurement may request an encryption-in-transit-and-at-rest clarification and KMS flow for VPC keys — can you prepare a one-page that references our SOC2 doc?
- Monica: to demonstrate the latency improvements, we should prepare a 5-day load script and a sample summary report (same schema Riverton will send). Can we allocate bench infra for Apr 6?

If we want to accelerate procurement, I can draft the initial SOW and send a stub to procurement@rivertonhealth.com after Evan confirms sign-off.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@rivertonhealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@rivertonhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Mar 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed MAP: Riverton Health trial milestones — updated MAP & next steps

Thanks all — quick update with next concrete actions and dates (attached updated MAP).

Updated milestones (agreed)
- Mar 16, 11:30 PT — Scope sign-off (Evan + Logan) — calendar invite sent.
- Mar 18 — SOW stub (consumption model) delivered to Procurement (Ben to draft).
- Mar 23 — Security check complete; Naomi to deliver KMS and SOC2 excerpt.
- Apr 06 — Staging deploy & 5-day load run starts.
- Apr 20 — Pilot review; decision to proceed to production.

Action items assigned
- Ben: SOW draft to procurement@rivertonhealth.com by Mar 18.
- Naomi: one-page KMS/audit flow by Mar 23.
- Monica: bench infra allocation confirmed by Mar 25; prepare load script.
- Sarah / Evan: provide 3 sample report payloads and confirm scope sign-off on Mar 16.

Procurement next steps
- Ben will share a stub SOW and a short commercial note. If procurement needs a quick call, happy to set one up this week.

DocuSign & approval: once scope is signed on Mar 16 I’ll send the SOW stub via DocuSign (link included in the MAP).

Thanks everyone — looking forward to getting this started.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments: Riverton-MAP-v1.xlsx (updated), SOC2-summary.pdf (same)

source.subject: Private VPC follow-up: edge termination, egress ownership & next steps
messages:
From: Alicia Torres <alicia@acadiahealth.io>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mike Brennan <mike@acadiahealth.io>
Date: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intro call recap — private VPC options

Thanks for the call earlier — good overview. Two quick follow-ups from netops:

- Can you confirm whether your PrivateLink setup terminates TLS at Redwood or at our VPC endpoint? We need to know if we must manage certs.
- For peering, we will likely need static egress IPs for outbound connections (audit & allowlist). Can you provide a short list of IPs or a CIDR we can hold in firewall rules?

Also attaching the rough VPC sketch we used on the call. Alicia

Attachment: acadia-vpc-sketch-20260225.png (image/png)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Torres <alicia@acadiahealth.io>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Mike Brennan <mike@acadiahealth.io>
Date: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 17:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Intro call recap — private VPC options

Alicia — thanks, and great to meet the Acadia team. Short answers up front, then proposed next steps: 

1) TLS termination: For PrivateLink we offer two deployment patterns —
   - "Customer-terminated TLS" (recommended for regulated deployments): TLS terminates in your VPC. You provision the cert on your endpoint; Redwood keeps backend-to-backend encryption.
   - "Redwood-terminated TLS": Redwood terminates at our private endpoint; we can manage certs via ACM/HSM integration when customers prefer that model.

   For onboarding speed we usually do Customer-terminated TLS for customers that require full cert control.

2) Egress IPs / allowlist: For Dedicated/Hosted we provide stable egress ranges. For Private deployments (peering or PrivateLink) we typically ask customers to allow either:
   - A short set of stable egress IPs (we can reserve 4 IPs per tenancy) OR
   - If you prefer not to rely on IP-based allowlist, we support mTLS between your proxy and our internal ingress endpoint (mutual TLS).

Proposed next steps (mutual action items):
- Redwood: produce a one-page comparison (Privatelink vs VPC peering) that includes who manages TLS, routing responsibilities, and expected egress behavior. (I attached a draft matrix.)
- Acadia: confirm whether you prefer cert custody (customer-managed) and identify the NetOps contact to run an ops workshop.

source.subject: Quick favor: short review + permission to feature HelioTech in a customer spotlight
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliotech.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick favor: short review + permission to feature HelioTech in a customer spotlight

Hi Alicia —

Hope you’re doing well. Quick ask: would HelioTech be open to two small things that would really help us and hopefully give you a little bump in visibility as well?

1) A short public review (1–3 sentences) on G2 or TrustRadius about how Redwood Inference has affected your product/team.
2) A quick yes/no permission to feature HelioTech in a short customer spotlight on our site (we’d use your logo and a single quote).

We’re happy to make it worth your time: we can send a Redwood swag pack (hoodie + stickers) and we also offer the option to direct a $100 donation to a charity of your choice instead of swag. No required promo content — just honest feedback.

I attached a short file with the one-click links (review-links.txt) and a PDF showing the swag options (swag-options.pdf). If you prefer, Monica from CS can hop on a 15–20 min call to capture a short on-record quote we can use verbatim.

Totally flexible on timing — if you can tell me whether you’d prefer G2 or TrustRadius first, I’ll send the exact link and next steps.

Thanks so much for considering — you’ve been an awesome partner to work with.

Best,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
-- Attachment: review-links.txt (text/plain, 1kb)
-- Attachment: swag-options.pdf (application/pdf, 120kb)

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliotech.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick favor: short review + permission to feature HelioTech in a customer spotlight

Hi Logan —

Thanks for the note and glad to hear from you. Happy to help — a few quick questions:

- We have to run anything with our comms/legal folks when a logo or quote is used publicly. Is there a simple release form or wording you use so I can forward?
- Can you confirm there are no NDAs or sensitive quote requests? We’ll only provide high-level product feedback.
- Is the donation option taxable/handled by Redwood, or do you send gift cards/swag directly? (We have internal rules about receiving vendor gifts.)

If you can share the release text and the direct review link I’ll route to comms. Otherwise I can submit a TrustRadius review this week and try to get G2 next week.

Appreciate it —
Alicia Gomez
Head of Product, HelioTech
-- Attachment: none

On Fri, Feb 20, 2026 at 09:12 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hope you’re doing well. Quick ask: would HelioTech be open to two small things that would really help us and hopefully give you a little bump in visibility as well?
> 
> 1) A short public review...
> 
> Best,
> Logan

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliotech.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Feb 2026 08:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick favor: short review + permission to feature HelioTech in a customer spotlight

Hi Alicia —

Thanks for the quick response and great questions. Short answers:

- We have a one-page media/release form for customer spotlights (attaching MA-approval-form.docx). It’s very standard: company name, short permission to use logo and a 1–2 sentence quote. You can have your legal team sign or we can accept an email approval from your comms lead.
- No NDAs; we only request high-level feedback and/or permission to use a short quote. We never publish private logs or PII.
- For swag/donations: we ship swag directly to the contact you specify. For donations we issue it from Redwood and can provide a receipt. If HelioTech has a policy restricting vendor swag, we’re happy to do the charity donation instead.

If it helps, I can take a 15–20 minute call to capture a short on-record quote for the website. Available windows next week: Tue 2/24 10–11am PT, Wed 2/25 2–4pm PT, Thu 2/26 9–11am PT. Which works for you?

Thanks again — happy to make this low overhead.

Cheers,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
-- Attachment: MA-approval-form.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, 18kb)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliotech.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Feb 2026 10:17:00 -0800
Subject: Follow-up + direct review links and optional draft blurb

Hi Alicia —

Following up on Monica’s note — really appreciate you routing this internally. To make it as easy as possible I pasted the direct links below and also wrote a one-sentence draft you can use or edit for G2/TrustRadius: no editing needed if you want to submit quickly.

G2 (one-click): https://g2.com/r/redwood-inference-review-helio
TrustRadius: https://www.trustradius.com/products/redwood-inference/reviews

Draft blurb suggestion: “Redwood Inference gave us predictable low-latency inference at scale; their support team moved quickly when we had questions.” — feel free to change any wording.

If you prefer a 10–15 minute recorded call rather than typing, Monica’s available on Thu 2/26 at 9:30am PT. If we don’t hear back I’ll assume TrustRadius first is fine and check back next week on G2.

Thanks again — and happy to route the swag or charitable donation once you confirm approval path.

Best,
Logan
-- Attachment: review-links.txt (text/plain, 1kb)

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia@heliotech.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick favor: short review + permission to feature HelioTech in a customer spotlight

Hi Logan / Monica —

Quick update: I submitted a TrustRadius review this morning (attached confirmation) and comms has signed off on a short quote and logo usage. We don’t need to sign a separate doc — emailed approval from our comms lead is fine for our records. I’ll try to post on G2 early next week.

Monica — the 2/26 9:30am PT time works for a short 15-min recording to capture the quote. If it’s easier to handle the media release via DocuSign link you sent, that’s fine too. For the swag vs donation: we’ll take the donation in our company name to the local STEM education charity you suggested.

Thanks for making this easy — Alicia

Alicia Gomez
Head of Product, HelioTech
-- Attachment: TrustRadius-confirmation.pdf (application/pdf, 2kb)

source.subject: Estimate & throughput options for BrightWave — next steps
messages:
From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@brightwave.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Feb 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Estimate & throughput options for BrightWave — next steps

Hi Elena —

Great to connect earlier today. Per our call I pulled together a short estimate and a technical appendix that show two capacity profiles so you can see the cost/latency tradeoffs:

- Standard: 2x inference nodes, targeted at steady throughput (monthly estimate included)
- Peak-Optimized: 4x nodes with burst capacity for peak traffic windows

Attachments: redwood_estimate_brightwave_v1.pdf (includes topline pricing + assumptions), redwood_tech_appendix_brightwave.pdf (networking, security, eviction/retention notes).

A couple of quick next steps I suggested on the call:
1) Confirm receipt of the deck/estimate — if anything is missing I can update immediately.
2) If helpful, I can run a 15-minute walkthrough next week to explain the assumptions (traffic profile, KV cache savings, routing options).
3) If you want, I can convert this into a short mutual action plan with milestones for procurement/SOW.

Proposed 15-min slots (PST): Tue 2/17 10:30am, Wed 2/18 11:00am. If those don't work tell me two times that do.

Also looping in Logan (solutions engineer) so he can join the walkthrough and answer any infra questions.

Thanks — enjoy the rest of your day.

Best,
Irene Choi
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
irene@redwood.com | (415) 555-0163

---
Attachment stubs: redwood_estimate_brightwave_v1.pdf (PDF, 9 pages); redwood_tech_appendix_brightwave.pdf (PDF, 6 pages).

From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@brightwave.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Feb 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Checking in on the BrightWave estimate — quick confirmation

Hi Elena —

Just following up to make sure the estimate and appendix landed in your inbox last week. I know things get busy — if it's easier I can walk through the key assumptions in 10–15 minutes and highlight where we can tighten numbers based on your actual traffic profile.

Two quick slots that work for me (PST):
- Wed 2/25 10:30am
- Thu 2/26 2:00pm

If neither fits, drop me two times that do or tell me if you'd rather get an updated version that collapses to a single recommended configuration (we did that for another customer to speed procurement).

Drive link for convenience: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2B3C-redwood-brightwave-estimate

If procurement/timing has shifted on your side I'd appreciate a quick note so we can adjust the estimate cadence.

Thanks —
Irene

---
Quoted from 2026-02-16 email: "Attachments: redwood_estimate_brightwave_v1.pdf ..."

From: Elena Morales <elena@brightwave.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Feb 2026 11:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Checking in on the BrightWave estimate — quick confirmation

Hi Irene —

Thanks for the nudge — I did see the estimate and tech appendix. Been heads-down this week with a product sprint. Tomorrow 2/25 at 11:00am PST works for a quick run-through. Can you send a calendar invite?

Quick question before the call: on the retention assumptions in the appendix, what does "7-day prefix cache" mean for our dataset size? Also, are we covered for SOC 2 evidence if we need to show audit logs?

Elena
VP Product | BrightWave
elena@brightwave.ai

On Mon, Feb 23, 2026 at 09:05 Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com> wrote:
> Just following up to make sure the estimate and appendix landed in your inbox last week.

---

From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@brightwave.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Feb 2026 12:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Checking in on the BrightWave estimate — quick confirmation

Perfect — I sent a calendar invite for Tue 2/25 11:00am PST with Logan included.

Quick answers up-front so you have context before we meet:
- "7-day prefix cache" in the appendix refers to our default prefix-level KV caching suggestion for sessions with repeat prompts — practically, with your projected active-user set it reduces token compute by ~18–26% in our estimate. We'll bring traffic-specific examples in the call and can update the model if your session characteristics differ.
- SOC 2: yes — our hosted offering provides audit logs and we can produce the required evidence. If you need SOC 2 artifacts ahead of procurement, I can share the checklist and a short export of the relevant log retention policy.

Thanks — looking forward to the chat.

Best,
Irene
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

---
Attachment stubs previously sent: redwood_estimate_brightwave_v1.pdf, redwood_tech_appendix_brightwave.pdf

Subject: Re: Discovery — next steps

Hi Logan,

Thanks for the call earlier. I wanted to follow up — I haven’t received a calendar invite for the technical deep dive we discussed (we had agreed to aim for next week). I checked my spam and calendar; nothing showed up.

Can you resend the invite or share a link? Also, we’re on PST at HQ, but Michael is on EST – just flagging in case that caused a time mismatch.

Best,
Emily
Emily Stone
Head of Data Platforms, Acme Analytics
emily.stone@acmeanalytics.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

To: Emily Stone <emily.stone@acmeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-02-20T10:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Discovery — next steps

Hi Emily — sorry about that, and thanks for flagging the timezone mismatch. Looks like the original invite was created in EST and didn’t translate for some recipients. My mistake.

I’m resending a fresh meeting link below and also listing a couple of alternative time blocks that should work across PST/EST — please pick whichever is easiest for your team and I’ll lock it in:

Proposed 45-min slots (all times listed with both PST / EST):
- Wed 25 Feb, 09:30–10:15 PST / 12:30–13:15 EST
- Wed 25 Feb, 15:00–15:45 PST / 18:00–18:45 EST
- Thu 26 Feb, 11:00–11:45 PST / 14:00–14:45 EST

Temporary Google Meet (I’ll replace with a calendar invite once you confirm): https://meet.google.com/new-link-xyz

Pre-demo asks: I’ve attached a short questionnaire that helps us focus the technical deep dive (data retention, VPC/private deployment needs, throughput targets). If you can share the filled form ahead of the session, that’ll help Dev and Marissa tailor the runbook.

Cc’ing Marissa (AE) and Dev (Solutions Eng) so they can attend the deep dive and review the questionnaire.

Thanks — I’ll wait to hear which slot you prefer and will send a formal calendar invite with the confirmed link.

Regards,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

source.subject: Telemetry sample: customer event chain verification
messages:
From: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@finnova.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Feb 2026 09:14:00 -08:00
Subject: Telemetry sample: missing events for txn batch 20260216

Hi Logan / Stephanie,

We noticed a gap in the telemetry for a handful of transactions originating on 2026-02-16 (internal batch id TXN-20260216-A). Our internal reconciliation shows the UI recorded the events, but your event stream appears to be missing ~120 event objects from that batch. This is causing a mismatch in reconciliation reports for our finance ops team.

Can you confirm whether Redwood has any indication of event loss or export issues for our tenant during that timeframe? At minimum we need a statement confirming whether any customer records were lost or could have been exported outside the platform.

Happy to hop on a quick call if useful.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Connor
Head of Platform Reliability | FinnovA
---
FinnovA Ops
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@finnova.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry sample: missing events for txn batch 20260216

Hi Sarah — thanks for flagging this and sorry for the disruption. We’ve opened support ticket #72619 and are prioritizing a reconstruction of the event path for TXN-20260216-A.

Initial notes: our telemetry ingestion shows elevated retry rates to the blobstore for a ~45 minute window starting 2026-02-16T14:10Z due to a transient storage throttling event on one of our write clusters. That triggered backpressure; however, our persistent write queue retained events and a background replay ran afterward. We will:

- Run a log-sampling sweep across the write queue and blobstore indices for the window in question.
- Compute cryptographic hashes for stored blobs and compare them to the inbound queue manifest.
- Provide a short evidence pack (sampling summary + manifest) showing chain-of-custody and whether any records are missing or exposed.

Marissa, can you run the hash reconciliation and attach the manifest + sampling summary to the ticket? Stephanie, please stand by for a one-page customer-facing statement once Marissa confirms results.

Sarah — I’ll follow up with timelines shortly; expect the evidence pack within 48 hours. If you want an interim update sooner, I can set one up.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@finnova.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Feb 2026 10:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry sample: missing events for txn batch 20260216

Sarah — investigative summary and evidence attached below. Short conclusion up front: we found no indication of data loss or external exposure. The ~120 events in question were delayed and later persisted; sampling and hash reconciliation confirm presence in our blobstore.

What we did (sampling & verification):
- Extracted the inbound queue manifest for TXN-20260216-A (manifest id: M-20260216-47).
- Sampled 400 message digests from the inbound queue and located corresponding blobstore objects via sequence IDs.
- Verified payload-level SHA256 hashes between queue entries and stored blobs. All sampled items matched.
- Replayed the write queue around the 14:10Z–14:55Z window; observed increased write retries due to transient blobstore throttle. Retries succeeded within an expected backoff window; no messages were truncated.

Findings:
- All sampled events matched their stored counterparts (SHA256 hashes match).
- No outbound streams were initiated that would send raw customer payloads to third parties during the incident window.
- The discrepancy in FinnovA reconciliation was caused by delayed acknowledgements to your webhook consumer; the consumer missed the short replay window. We have recommended a small buffering change on the webhook client side to accommodate transient retries.

Attached (evidence pack):
- telemetry_hash_manifest_20260219.csv (manifest: sequence_id, message_digest, blob_path)
- sampling_summary_20260219.pdf (sampling methodology, sample set, verification steps)
- kv-replay-log-snippet.txt (replay trace for sequence ids 1023400–1023605)

Customer-facing statement (feel free to copy):
"Redwood completed a targeted log and blobstore verification for FinnovA related to events generated on 2026-02-16. Our sampling and cryptographic verification found no missing customer data and no evidence of export or exfiltration. The observed discrepancy was caused by delayed webhook acknowledgements on FinnovA's consumer side during a short storage retry window. We retain full chain-of-custody records for the examined window and have provided the hash manifest and sampling summary to FinnovA for their audit."

Let me know if you want us to sign the manifest with a timestamped notarization (we can do an HMAC over the manifest) — happy to include that for compliance teams.

— Marissa
Marissa Cole
Incident Response Engineer | Redwood Inference
Attachments: telemetry_hash_manifest_20260219.csv (CSV), sampling_summary_20260219.pdf (PDF), kv-replay-log-snippet.txt (TXT)
From: Sarah Connor <sarah.connor@finnova.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Telemetry sample: missing events for txn batch 20260216

Thanks team — the evidence pack looks thorough and the one-line statement is perfect for our audit trail. We’ll implement the consumer buffer change you suggested and confirm after our next run.

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the clear chain-of-custody docs — that’s exactly what our security and compliance teams needed. Closing this on our side unless something else pops up.

Best,
Sarah
Head of Platform Reliability | FinnovA

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the clear chain-of-custody docs — that’s exactly what our security and compliance teams needed. Closing this on our side unless something else pops up.

Best,
Sarah
Head of Platform Reliability | FinnovA

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>

Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 16:03:00 +0000
Subject: Collaborative evaluation plan & quick alignment

Hi Priya —

Great to meet you on the call earlier. As discussed, I wanted to kick off a lightweight shared evaluation plan so we can keep decision-makers aligned and hit your target pilot start in mid‑March. Key goals I heard:

- Validate Redwood inference latency and cost on a 50‑agent use case
- Confirm data handling and audit log requirements for compliance
- Measure model quality on your 8 canonical prompt sets

Proposed 6‑week timeline (high level):
1) Week 0 (this week): Stakeholder roster + scope confirmed
2) Weeks 1–2: Pilot environment + baseline runs
3) Weeks 3–4: Iterations (batching, quantization checks)
4) Week 5: Final eval and success review

I attached a first‑draft collaborative plan (Healthsy_MAP_v1.xlsx) with roles and owners filled in for your convenience. Can you confirm the primary champion and executive approver for the pilot? Also please confirm whether Tom Anders should be included on technical and procurement threads.

Proposed next steps:
- Priya: confirm champion + approver, add any missing stakeholders (by EOD Thu)
- Marissa (CS): will propose the onboarding playbook and readiness checklist
- Priyom (SE): will share a short list of telemetry fields we’ll need from your side
- Logan (AE): schedule a 30‑min MAP handoff next week once roster is confirmed

Thanks — happy to be flexible on the timeline if you have constraints.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
logan_wright@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555‑0192

To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>

Thanks, Logan — this looks useful. Quick answers:

- Champion: Priya Sharma (me) — I’ll own day‑to‑day coordination.
- Executive approver: Erin Patel, VP Product (I’ll loop her in once we have the schedule)
- Please include Tom on technical prep and any procurement comms.

A couple notes from our side that should feed into the MAP:
- We need SOC 2 evidence and a short list of retention controls before we run synthetic samples

—Priya

Subject: Re: Collaborative evaluation plan & quick alignment

Hi Priya —

Thanks for confirming. I’m attaching a slightly expanded playbook and a one‑page onboarding checklist that maps to the timeline Logan proposed. Highlights:

- Compliance artifacts: I’ll attach the SOC 2 snapshot and a short template for evidence requests (we can redact customer names before sharing with your compliance team).
- Telemetry: Priyom will include the minimal telemetry schema (tokens, latency, error class, request_id) and suggested retention for the pilot.
- Access: We recommend a dedicated test bucket and a non‑production API key scoped to the pilot dataset.

Action items I’ll pick up:
- Upload SOC 2 and evidence template to the shared Drive link by EOD today.
- Prep onboarding checklist and a lightweight runbook for your infra team.

Priyom: tagging you for the telemetry details and a quick call request with Tom if needed.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this moving.

Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

Priya — once Erin is ready, we’ll drop a one‑page exec summary she can review in under 15 minutes.

Immediate next steps (today):

source.subject: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction (HealthGrid)
messages:
From: Elena Morris <elena@healthgrid.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthGrid Legal <legal@healthgrid.com>
Date: Sat, 24 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

Hi Logan,

Thanks for the quick onboarding call yesterday. Procurement/legal asked me to follow up with a few items we need in the DPA before we can route for signature:

- A current and complete subprocessor list (including region of processing).
- An explicit prohibition on using Customer Data for training or model improvement, with a short definition of what "training" means for the agreement.
- Confirmation on cross-border transfer mechanisms (we are EU-based but have US operations).

Timeline: procurement needs the executed DPA by Feb 5 to keep our pilot schedule. Can you send a redline that addresses the above?

Also: we prefer AdobeSign for execution if possible.

Best,
Elena
Director, Partnerships
HealthGrid

Attachments: none

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena@healthgrid.com>
Cc: HealthGrid Legal <legal@healthgrid.com>
Date: Sun, 25 Jan 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

Elena —

Appreciate the clear asks. I've attached a redline (HealthGrid_DPA_v3_redlines.pdf) and our latest subprocessor CSV. Key points in the draft:

1) Narrow definition: we propose defining "Model Training" as "the process of using Customer Data to iteratively update model weights or include Customer Data in training corpora in a manner that materially changes model parameters or embeddings used for inference."
2) Ban with carve: Redwood will not use Customer Data for Model Training or Model Improvement except to the extent necessary for (a) delivering the service (e.g., runtime caching/KV cache optimizations) or (b) aggregated telemetry to maintain performance; and in all cases, only with customer opt-in or when data is de-identified per the DPA.
3) Subprocessors: attached list includes processing region and last-audited date. We accept notification and objection flow.
4) Transfers: we include SCCs + our standard EU data flow controls.

If that looks reasonable, we can route this to AdobeSign and include an explicit page for signature of the DPA exhibit. Priyom (cc) can confirm schedule for procurement.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: HealthGrid_DPA_v3_redlines.pdf; Subprocessor_List_2026-01-24.csv

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Jan 2026 08:47:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

Neha / Priyom — forwarding HealthGrid's asks and the redline I prepared. Two legal questions for you: 

- Is the proposed "Model Training" definition enforceable / sufficiently narrow from a dispute perspective? We tried to avoid a blanket ban that would prevent routine runtime improvements.
- Should we add a short audit-rights callout tied to training restrictions (e.g., a right to verify de-identification/opt-in)?

Priyom — HealthGrid needs the executed DPA by Feb 5 to keep the pilot window. Can you confirm procurement cadence / whether they will accept AdobeSign?

I'll hold off on sending the AdobeSign envelope until we have legal sign-off.

— Logan

Quoted original thread below.

From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Jan 2026 10:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

On Mon, Jan 26, 2026 at 08:47 AM Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Neha / Priyom — forwarding HealthGrid's asks and the redline I prepared. Two legal questions for you: 
> 
> - Is the proposed "Model Training" definition enforceable / sufficiently narrow from a dispute perspective? We tried to avoid a blanket ban that would prevent routine runtime improvements.

Thanks for the context. The language is a good start but I recommend: 

1) Replace the phrase "Model Improvement" with clearer categories: (i) "Model Training" (as you defined), (ii) "Runtime Optimization" (cache/KV and other transient performance improvements that do not persist customer data in training corpora), and (iii) "Aggregated Metrics" (statistical metrics that cannot be traced to an individual customer).
2) Add a short audit right limited to verifying compliance with the "no training" prohibition (one inspection per 12 months, reasonable notice, redacted output acceptable).
3) Add a subprocessor flow paragraph: Redwood will provide the subprocessor list and notify customers of additions; customers may object within 15 days; objection remedies limited to termination for convenience if not resolved.
4) Confirm SCCs are attached — if HealthGrid insists on additional transfer mechanisms (e.g., BCRs), we'll need a transition plan.

I'll draft precise wording and propose the audit clause and the 15-day objection window. Once we have their review, we can execute via AdobeSign.

—Neha

Attachments: none

From: HealthGrid Legal <legal@healthgrid.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morris <elena@healthgrid.com>
Date: Sun, 01 Feb 2026 14:35:00 -0800
Subject: Re: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

Logan and team,

Thanks for the quick turnaround and for incorporating the proposed definition. We've reviewed Neha's suggested clarifications and are mostly aligned. Two small edits from our side: 

1) We prefer the objection remedy to be an escrow of data rather than immediate termination in the first instance — if good-faith discussions fail within 30 days, HealthGrid may request termination.
2) Please add the consolidated subprocessor list as an exhibit to the DPA (we will need an exhibit to reference in our procurement system).

We are ready to execute the revised DPA. Please send an AdobeSign envelope to legal@healthgrid.com and elena@healthgrid.com.

Elena and I will sign once the exhibit is attached.

Best,
HealthGrid Legal

Attachments: none

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: HealthGrid Legal <legal@healthgrid.com>, Elena Morris <elena@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Feb 2026 16:08:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction — execution

All —

Thanks, team. We attached the consolidated subprocessor exhibit and updated the remedy language to reflect a 30-day good-faith remediation period and the exhibit attachment. AdobeSign envelope sent: https://adobesign.example.com/envelope/A12345-HealthGrid-DPA (envelope sent to legal@healthgrid.com, elena@healthgrid.com).

Once signed, I'll upload the executed DPA to our contracts Drive and drop a copy here.

Logan
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

Attachments: Executed_DPA_HealthGrid_20260202.pdf (uploaded after signature)

text:
From: Elena Morris <elena@healthgrid.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthGrid Legal <legal@healthgrid.com>
Date: Sat, 24 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

Hi Logan,

Thanks for the quick onboarding call yesterday. Procurement/legal asked me to follow up with a few items we need in the DPA before we can route for signature:

- A current and complete subprocessor list (including region of processing).
- An explicit prohibition on using Customer Data for training or model improvement, with a short definition of what \"training\" means for the agreement.
- Confirmation on cross-border transfer mechanisms (we are EU-based but have US operations).

Timeline: procurement needs the executed DPA by Feb 5 to keep our pilot schedule. Can you send a redline that addresses the above?

Also: we prefer AdobeSign for execution if possible.

Best,
Elena
Director, Partnerships
HealthGrid

Attachments: none


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena@healthgrid.com>
Cc: HealthGrid Legal <legal@healthgrid.com>
Date: Sun, 25 Jan 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

Elena —

Appreciate the clear asks. I've attached a redline (HealthGrid_DPA_v3_redlines.pdf) and our latest subprocessor CSV. Key points in the draft:

1) Narrow definition: we propose defining \"Model Training\" as \"the process of using Customer Data to iteratively update model weights or include Customer Data in training corpora in a manner that materially changes model parameters or embeddings used for inference.\"
2) Ban with carve: Redwood will not use Customer Data for Model Training or Model Improvement except to the extent necessary for (a) delivering the service (e.g., runtime caching/KV cache optimizations) or (b) aggregated telemetry to maintain performance; and in all cases, only with customer opt-in or when data is de-identified per the DPA.
3) Subprocessors: attached list includes processing region and last-audited date. We accept notification and objection flow.
4) Transfers: we include SCCs + our standard EU data flow controls.

If that looks reasonable, we can route this to AdobeSign and include an explicit page for signature of the DPA exhibit. Priyom (cc) can confirm schedule for procurement.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: HealthGrid_DPA_v3_redlines.pdf; Subprocessor_List_2026-01-24.csv


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Jan 2026 08:47:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

Neha / Priyom — forwarding HealthGrid's asks and the redline I prepared. Two legal questions for you: 

- Is the proposed \"Model Training\" definition enforceable / sufficiently narrow from a dispute perspective? We tried to avoid a blanket ban that would prevent routine runtime improvements.
- Should we add a short audit-rights callout tied to training restrictions (e.g., a right to verify de-identification/opt-in)?

Priyom — HealthGrid needs the executed DPA by Feb 5 to keep the pilot window. Can you confirm procurement cadence / whether they will accept AdobeSign?

I'll hold off on sending the AdobeSign envelope until we have legal sign-off.

— Logan

Quoted original thread below.


From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Jan 2026 10:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: DPA: subprocessor list + explicit training restriction

On Mon, Jan 26, 2026 at 08:47 AM Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Neha / Priyom — forwarding HealthGrid's asks and the redline I prepared. Two legal questions for you: 
> 
> - Is the proposed \"Model Training\" definition enforceable / sufficiently narrow from a dispute perspective? We tried to avoid a blanket ban that would prevent routine runtime improvements.

Thanks for the context. The language is a good start but I recommend: 

1) Replace the phrase \"Model Improvement\" with clearer categories: (i) \"Model Training\" (as you defined), (ii) \"Runtime Optimization\" (cache/KV and other transient performance improvements that do not persist customer data in training corpora), and (iii) \"Aggregated Metrics\" (statistical metrics that cannot be traced to an individual customer).
2) Add a short audit right limited to verifying compliance with the \"no training\" prohibition (one inspection per 12 months, reasonable notice, redacted output acceptable).
3) Add a subprocessor flow paragraph: Redwood will provide the subprocessor list and notify customers of additions; customers may object within 15 days; objection remedies limited to termination for convenience if not resolved.
4) Confirm SCCs are attached — if HealthGrid insists on additional transfer mechanisms (e.g., BCRs), we'll need a transition plan.

I'll draft precise wording and propose the audit clause and the 15-day objection window. Once we have their review, we can execute via AdobeSign.

—Neha

source.subject: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)
source.body:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Team —

We’re trying to close Phase 0 requirements for shadow traffic telemetry (ENG-5404 / ENG-5410 / ENG-5411). I want to get to a concrete v1 event schema + defaults for retention and label/dimension policy, and make sure we can defend the ingestion cost.

Questions we need answers on this week:

1) Event model: do we emit *one* “shadow_pair” event per sampled request (with both control + shadow summary), or *two* events (control + shadow) with a join key?
   - My preference: a single “shadow_pair” to reduce joins and keep dimensions consistent, but concerned about payload size + partial availability for streaming.

2) Dimensions/cardinality: which fields are allowed as tags/labels (timeseries dims) vs only in logs/events (non-indexed / low-retention storage)?
   - Specifically: route, tenant, model_id/control_version, model_id/shadow_version, rubric_version, region, request_type (chat/embeddings/rerank), outcome.
   - I strongly believe prompt_id / request_fingerprint must NOT be a dimension.

3) Retention defaults:
   - Hosted: what’s the default retention for prompt/response artifacts vs aggregated metrics?
   - Dedicated: can we offer longer retention by default without exploding cost?
   - Private: do we require customer-controlled settings (and does “0 days” mean don’t store artifacts at all vs store only aggregates)?

4) Cost: can we get a back-of-napkin on ingestion/storage at 1% / 2% / 5% sampling for a large tenant (e.g. 5k RPS, average 1.2KB event payload after redaction)?

I’ve attached the current draft schema and a quick cost sheet from last week’s discussion. Would love each of you to weigh in from your area (SRE/observability, security/audit, telemetry infra).

Attachment(s):
- shadow-traffic-event-schema-draft.json (application/json)
- shadow-traffic-cost-model-sheet.json (application/json)

Thanks,
Logan

—
Logan Wright
Engineering Lead, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference


From: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 10:02 AM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

+1 on the “single shadow_pair event” bias for metrics sanity.

Observability POV:

- Strongly recommend we define a strict split between:
  (A) metrics-safe dimensions (low-ish cardinality, bounded enumerations)
  (B) event fields (stored, queryable, but not as Prom/TSDB labels)

Metrics-safe dims (proposal for v1):
- route_id (not route name; use internal stable ID)
- deployment_mode (hosted|dedicated|private)
- region
- request_type
- control_model_id
- shadow_target_id (if we allow multiple shadows, this is a bounded list per experiment)
- outcome (ok|timeout|error|circuit_open|dropped)
- rubric_bundle_version (bounded, pinned per experiment)

NOT safe dims:
- tenant_id/customer_id (unless we bucket/limit)
- request_fingerprint
- prompt_id
- user_id
- tool/function name

If PM needs tenant-level dashboards: we can do it at the Console query layer from event storage, or we do a controlled allowlist for “top-N tenants” emitted as a separate pipeline, but that’s a later thing.

Also calling out: we should *not* include free-form error_message as a dim (seen too many cardinality explosions). Keep it in event payload.

Action item I’d suggest: schedule a 30-min “cardinality review” with me + Logan + Nadia + Caleb this week and lock down the allowed label list in the ADR.

Allison

—
Allison Grant
Observability & Alerting Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 11:18 AM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Security/compliance POV (retention + artifacts):

- We should treat raw prompts/responses as potentially sensitive by default, even if “shadow” is opt-in. For Hosted, I’d recommend:
  - default artifact retention: 0 (do not persist raw prompt/response by default)
  - default derived/aggregated metrics retention: standard Console retention (whatever the existing default is; typically 30d+)

- If Product wants “sampled examples” drilldown in Console (and I think they do), we should store *redacted* snippets with:
  - truncation (e.g. first N chars/tokens)
  - structured allowlist (messages.role + messages.content, but content run through redaction)
  - strict TTL (7 days feels OK for Hosted; Dedicated could be configurable)

- For Dedicated:
  - default should still be short for artifacts (7–14 days), but make it an explicit tenant setting w/ audit trail.

- For Private:
  - must be configurable. “0 days” should mean: no prompt/response artifacts stored by Redwood services; aggregates only.
  - if we store artifacts locally in the customer environment, we need to make sure it’s covered by their retention controls and we can export audit events to their SIEM.

Also: do not put model output text in any place that is likely to end up in metrics labels or paging payloads.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 1:07 PM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

On event model + streaming / partials:

- For non-streaming requests, “single shadow_pair event” is simplest.
- For streaming, we have two reasonable choices:
  1) Emit a shadow_pair event at end-of-stream only (contains summaries + refs). Downside: if shadow path times out mid-stream we still want an event w/ outcome=timeout.
  2) Emit two events: shadow_control_summary and shadow_candidate_summary with same pair_id + a third optional shadow_pair_joined event later.

I think (1) still works if we define:
- control_summary always present
- shadow_summary optional
- outcome encodes shadow_missing_reason (timeout|dropped|circuit_open)
- attach “bytes_captured / tokens_captured” for streaming so we know partial.

Telemetry pipeline note:
- If we keep a single event but allow null shadow_summary, we need to be careful in downstream aggregations so “missing” doesn’t look like “zero quality score.”

Cost model quick math (ballpark, assuming 5k rps, 2% sampling => 100 events/sec):
- 1.2KB avg payload => 120KB/sec => ~10GB/day raw ingestion per tenant
- With overhead (envelope, indexing, replication), call it 2–3x => ~20–30GB/day
- At 5% sampling, that jumps to ~50–75GB/day

This argues hard for: small payloads, strict redaction/truncation, and default short TTL for artifacts.

Action items I can take:
- Update schema draft to make shadow_summary optional and add “shadow_missing_reason” + “captured_fraction.”
- Add a section spelling out “dims vs fields” and mark disallowed label candidates.

Nadia

—
Nadia Rahman
Telemetry Pipelines Owner
Redwood Inference


From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Jan 26, 2026 at 9:41 AM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Adding audit/controls angle:

- Any setting that changes artifact retention must produce an audit log entry (who/when/what). This includes:
  - enabling shadowing on a route
  - changing sampling rate
  - changing artifact retention window
  - changing redaction mode (if configurable)

- For Private: if customer wants “no artifacts,” we should still log *counts* of shadowed requests and high-level outcomes for compliance/reporting (e.g., N mirrored, N dropped, N timed out), but those can be purely aggregate.

- Data residency: please make sure the schema includes residency metadata (region / deployment) so we can enforce “store in-region” for Dedicated/Private.

If helpful, I can draft the audit event names and required fields (aligning with existing control-plane audit patterns).

Kevin

—
Kevin Osei
Audit/SIEM & Retention Controls
Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 4:52 PM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Thanks all — this is converging. Proposed decisions + concrete next steps below.

DECISIONS (v1 proposal):

A) Event model
- Single event type: shadow_pair.v1
- control_summary required; shadow_summary optional
- Add fields:
  - pair_id (UUID)
  - shadow_missing_reason (timeout|dropped|circuit_open|target_error|unknown)
  - captured_fraction (0.0–1.0) for streaming

B) Dimensions vs event fields
- We will publish an allowlist of metrics-safe dimensions (Allison’s list) and hard-reject anything else at ingestion.
- tenant_id/customer_id: NOT a timeseries label in v1.
  - We can still store tenant_id as an event field for Console filtering, but not in Prom/TSDB.
- request_fingerprint / prompt_id: event field only, and request_fingerprint must be HMAC’d / non-reversible.

C) Retention defaults (please sanity check)
- Hosted:
  - artifacts (redacted snippets): 7 days default, OFF by default unless customer explicitly enables “example capture” in Console
  - aggregates/metrics: follow existing Console retention (no change)
- Dedicated:
  - artifacts: 14 days default (configurable 0–30) w/ audit
  - aggregates: as above
- Private:
  - artifacts: default 0; configurable by customer admin; all storage in-environment

D) Cost framing to Product
- We’ll communicate the order-of-magnitude impact at 1%/2%/5% sampling and recommend 1–2% for beta. Anything above that should be explicit + warned.

ACTION ITEMS
1) Nadia: update schema draft with optional shadow_summary + streaming fields by EOD Thu.
2) Allison + Logan: 30-min cardinality review — I’ll send a calendar hold for Wed/Thu. Outcome: an explicit “allowed_dimensions.md” snippet for the ADR.
3) Marcus + Kevin: confirm Hosted default “example capture OFF” is acceptable from policy POV and draft the exact wording for the setting in Console (what it stores, for how long).
4) Caleb: can you sanity check our storage math against current ingestion costs (include 2–3x overhead assumption) and reply with updated numbers we can quote?

Separate note: SUP-28510 (cardinality alerts after shadow traffic) is a good cautionary tale; we should reference it in the ADR as motivation for strict dimension gates.

Logan

—
Logan Wright
Engineering Lead, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference
