Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 1:07 PM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

On event model + streaming / partials:

- For non-streaming requests, “single shadow_pair event” is simplest.
- For streaming, we have two reasonable choices:
  1) Emit a shadow_pair event at end-of-stream only (contains summaries + refs). Downside: if shadow path times out mid-stream we still want an event w/ outcome=timeout.
  2) Emit two events: shadow_control_summary and shadow_candidate_summary with same pair_id + a third optional shadow_pair_joined event later.

I think (1) still works if we define:
- control_summary always present
- shadow_summary optional
- outcome encodes shadow_missing_reason (timeout|dropped|circuit_open)
- attach “bytes_captured / tokens_captured” for streaming so we know partial.

Telemetry pipeline note:
- If we keep a single event but allow null shadow_summary, we need to be careful in downstream aggregations so “missing” doesn’t look like “zero quality score.”

Cost model quick math (ballpark, assuming 5k rps, 2% sampling => 100 events/sec):
- 1.2KB avg payload => 120KB/sec => ~10GB/day raw ingestion per tenant
- With overhead (envelope, indexing, replication), call it 2–3x => ~20–30GB/day
- At 5% sampling, that jumps to ~50–75GB/day

This argues hard for: small payloads, strict redaction/truncation, and default short TTL for artifacts.

Action items I can take:
- Update schema draft to make shadow_summary optional and add “shadow_missing_reason” + “captured_fraction.”
- Add a section spelling out “dims vs fields” and mark disallowed label candidates.

Nadia

—
Nadia Rahman
Telemetry Pipelines Owner
Redwood Inference


From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Jan 26, 2026 at 9:41 AM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Adding audit/controls angle:

- Any setting that changes artifact retention must produce an audit log entry (who/when/what). This includes:
  - enabling shadowing on a route
  - changing sampling rate
  - changing artifact retention window
  - changing redaction mode (if configurable)

- For Private: if customer wants “no artifacts,” we should still log *counts* of shadowed requests and high-level outcomes for compliance/reporting (e.g., N mirrored, N dropped, N timed out), but those can be purely aggregate.

- Data residency: please make sure the schema includes residency metadata (region / deployment) so we can enforce “store in-region” for Dedicated/Private.

If helpful, I can draft the audit event names and required fields (aligning with existing control-plane audit patterns).

Kevin

—
Kevin Osei
Audit/SIEM & Retention Controls
Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 4:52 PM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Thanks all — this is converging. Proposed decisions + concrete next steps below.

DECISIONS (v1 proposal):

A) Event model
- Single event type: shadow_pair.v1
- control_summary required; shadow_summary optional
- Add fields:
  - pair_id (UUID)
  - shadow_missing_reason (timeout|dropped|circuit_open|target_error|unknown)
  - captured_fraction (0.0–1.0) for streaming

B) Dimensions vs event fields
- We will publish an allowlist of metrics-safe dimensions (Allison’s list) and hard-reject anything else at ingestion.
- tenant_id/customer_id: NOT a timeseries label in v1.
  - We can still store tenant_id as an event field for Console filtering, but not in Prom/TSDB.
- request_fingerprint / prompt_id: event field only, and request_fingerprint must be HMAC’d / non-reversible.

C) Retention defaults (please sanity check)
- Hosted:
  - artifacts (redacted snippets): 7 days default, OFF by default unless customer explicitly enables “example capture” in Console
  - aggregates/metrics: follow existing Console retention (no change)
- Dedicated:
  - artifacts: 14 days default (configurable 0–30) w/ audit
  - aggregates: as above
- Private:
  - artifacts: default 0; configurable by customer admin; all storage in-environment

D) Cost framing to Product
- We’ll communicate the order-of-magnitude impact at 1%/2%/5% sampling and recommend 1–2% for beta. Anything above that should be explicit + warned.

ACTION ITEMS
1) Nadia: update schema draft with optional shadow_summary + streaming fields by EOD Thu.
2) Allison + Logan: 30-min cardinality review — I’ll send a calendar hold for Wed/Thu. Outcome: an explicit “allowed_dimensions.md” snippet for the ADR.
3) Marcus + Kevin: confirm Hosted default “example capture OFF” is acceptable from policy POV and draft the exact wording for the setting in Console (what it stores, for how long).
4) Caleb: can you sanity check our storage math against current ingestion costs (include 2–3x overhead assumption) and reply with updated numbers we can quote?

Separate note: SUP-28510 (cardinality alerts after shadow traffic) is a good cautionary tale; we should reference it in the ADR as motivation for strict dimension gates.

Logan

—
Logan Wright
Engineering Lead, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Team —

We’re trying to close Phase 0 requirements for shadow traffic telemetry (ENG-5404 / ENG-5410 / ENG-5411). I want to get to a concrete v1 event schema + defaults for retention and label/dimension policy, and make sure we can defend the ingestion cost.

Questions we need answers on this week:

1) Event model: do we emit *one* “shadow_pair” event per sampled request (with both control + shadow summary), or *two* events (control + shadow) with a join key?
   - My preference: a single “shadow_pair” to reduce joins and keep dimensions consistent, but concerned about payload size + partial availability for streaming.

2) Dimensions/cardinality: which fields are allowed as tags/labels (timeseries dims) vs only in logs/events (non-indexed / low-retention storage)?
   - Specifically: route, tenant, model_id/control_version, model_id/shadow_version, rubric_version, region, request_type (chat/embeddings/rerank), outcome.
   - I strongly believe prompt_id / request_fingerprint must NOT be a dimension.

3) Retention defaults:
   - Hosted: what’s the default retention for prompt/response artifacts vs aggregated metrics?
   - Dedicated: can we offer longer retention by default without exploding cost?
   - Private: do we require customer-controlled settings (and does “0 days” mean don’t store artifacts at all vs store only aggregates)?

4) Cost: can we get a back-of-napkin on ingestion/storage at 1% / 2% / 5% sampling for a large tenant (e.g. 5k RPS, average 1.2KB event payload after redaction)?

I’ve attached the current draft schema and a quick cost sheet from last week’s discussion. Would love each of you to weigh in from your area (SRE/observability, security/audit, telemetry infra).

Attachment(s):
- shadow-traffic-event-schema-draft.json (application/json)
- shadow-traffic-cost-model-sheet.json (application/json)

Thanks,
Logan

—
Logan Wright
Engineering Lead, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference


From: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 10:02 AM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

+1 on the “single shadow_pair event” bias for metrics sanity.

Observability POV:

- Strongly recommend we define a strict split between:
  (A) metrics-safe dimensions (low-ish cardinality, bounded enumerations)
  (B) event fields (stored, queryable, but not as Prom/TSDB labels)

Metrics-safe dims (proposal for v1):
- route_id (not route name; use internal stable ID)
- deployment_mode (hosted|dedicated|private)
- region
- request_type
- control_model_id
- shadow_target_id (if we allow multiple shadows, this is a bounded list per experiment)
- outcome (ok|timeout|error|circuit_open|dropped)
- rubric_bundle_version (bounded, pinned per experiment)

NOT safe dims:
- tenant_id/customer_id (unless we bucket/limit)
- request_fingerprint
- prompt_id
- user_id
- tool/function name

If PM needs tenant-level dashboards: we can do it at the Console query layer from event storage, or we do a controlled allowlist for “top-N tenants” emitted as a separate pipeline, but that’s a later thing.

Also calling out: we should *not* include free-form error_message as a dim (seen too many cardinality explosions). Keep it in event payload.

Action item I’d suggest: schedule a 30-min “cardinality review” with me + Logan + Nadia + Caleb this week and lock down the allowed label list in the ADR.

Allison

—
Allison Grant
Observability & Alerting Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Jan 25, 2026 at 11:18 AM
Subject: Re: Shadow telemetry schema + retention questions (need answers for Phase 0)

Security/compliance POV (retention + artifacts):

- We should treat raw prompts/responses as potentially sensitive by default, even if “shadow” is opt-in. For Hosted, I’d recommend:
  - default artifact retention: 0 (do not persist raw prompt/response by default)
  - default derived/aggregated metrics retention: standard Console retention (whatever the existing default is; typically 30d+)

- If Product wants “sampled examples” drilldown in Console (and I think they do), we should store *redacted* snippets with:
  - truncation (e.g. first N chars/tokens)
  - structured allowlist (messages.role + messages.content, but content run through redaction)
  - strict TTL (7 days feels OK for Hosted; Dedicated could be configurable)

- For Dedicated:
  - default should still be short for artifacts (7–14 days), but make it an explicit tenant setting w/ audit trail.

- For Private:
  - must be configurable. “0 days” should mean: no prompt/response artifacts stored by Redwood services; aggregates only.
  - if we store artifacts locally in the customer environment, we need to make sure it’s covered by their retention controls and we can export audit events to their SIEM.

Also: do not put model output text in any place that is likely to end up in metrics labels or paging payloads.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference

To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>

Telemetry pipeline note:
- If we keep a single event but allow null shadow_summary, we need to be careful in downstream aggregations so “missing” doesn’t look like “zero quality score.”

Cost model quick math (ballpark, assuming 5k rps, 2% sampling => 100 events/sec):
- 1.2KB avg payload => 120KB/sec => ~10GB/day raw ingestion per tenant
- With overhead (envelope, indexing, replication), call it 2–3x => ~20–30GB/day
- At 5% sampling, that jumps to ~50–75GB/day

This argues hard for: small payloads, strict redaction/truncation, and default short TTL for artifacts.

Action items I can take:
- Update schema draft to make shadow_summary optional and add “shadow_missing_reason” + “captured_fraction.”
- Add a section spelling out “dims vs fields” and mark disallowed label candidates.

Nadia

—
Nadia Rahman
Telemetry Pipelines Owner
Redwood Inference

To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>

messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Sara Malik <sara.malik@bridgepay.com>, Ethan Cole <ethan.cole@bridgepay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 16:02:00 -0800
Subject: Recap + Ask: Buying roster, decision criteria & security scope kickoff

Hi Sara / Ethan —

Thanks again for the demo earlier. Quick recap and concrete asks so we can get the security and procurement tracks moving: 

1) Decision & approval map (what we learned)
   - Economic buyer: Ethan Cole (VP Product) — cares about unit cost & SLA guarantees
   - Technical approvers: Amy Ruiz (Head of Infra) and Nikhil (your engineering lead) — will validate systems in scope and network boundaries
   - Procurement/legal: procurement@bridgepay.com (TBD contact) — will handle T&Cs and payment terms

2) Decision criteria you mentioned
   - Latency at 95th percentile under 120ms for core inference path
   - On-prem/private deployment option (for cardholder data flows)
   - Clear audit logs and key management integration

3) Security kickoff & ask
   - Attached: bridgepay-security-questionnaire-v1.docx (short intake we use to scope systems in/out). Can you share the initial answers and the list of systems where BridgePay would route data to Redwood (services, ingress points, network segments)? We primarily need: environment names, where PHI/PCI data may touch, and any air-gapped/VPC constraints.

4) Procurement
   - I attached a high-level draft of commercial terms we normally start with (procurement-terms-draft.pdf). If there are specific redlines, please share them and we’ll coordinate legal.

Proposed next steps / timeline
   - Please complete the questionnaire (or point us to your security contact who will). Goal: have a scoped security kickoff and questionnaire returned by end of next week (Jan 23).
   - Internal kickoff: I’ll schedule a 30-min intro with Priya (security lead) and Karthik (solutions) once we have the systems in-scope list.

If you prefer, we can do a short working session this week to complete the intake together — happy to schedule on Friday morning PT.

Thanks — looking forward to progressing.

Best,
Logan Wright
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0124

Attachments: bridgepay-security-questionnaire-v1.docx (17KB), procurement-terms-draft.pdf (42KB)
From: Sara Malik <sara.malik@bridgepay.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Ethan Cole <ethan.cole@bridgepay.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jan 2026 09:13:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Recap + Ask: Buying roster, decision criteria & security scope kickoff

Thanks Logan — appreciate the clear recap. Quick replies: 

- Confirmed roles: Ethan is the economic buyer and will be looped on commercial. Amy Ruiz and our infra lead Nikhil Sharma will be technical approvers. I’ll confirm the official procurement contact (we usually use procurement@bridgepay.com but I’ll add the person).

- Questionnaire: I can take a pass on the intake and return a first draft by Monday (Jan 19). If you send the doc as a Google doc it’ll be easier for edits — otherwise I’ll return the Word file.

- Systems in scope: high level — our public API layer (api.bridgepay.com), a queuing layer (kafka.bridgepay.internal), and an isolated worker fleet that processes card tokenization. Some of that touches PCI; we will flag specific flows in the questionnaire.

Friday working session: we’d prefer Monday morning PT for a working session with Amy and Nikhil — can you do 10:00 AM PT on Jan 19?

Also, given the PCI implications, can you confirm whether Redwood supports HSM-backed key storage in your private deployment?

--
Sara Malik
Head of Platform Operations | BridgePay
sara.malik@bridgepay.com | +1 (617) 555-0987
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jan 2026 11:20:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Recap + Ask: Buying roster, decision criteria & security scope kickoff

Team — forwarding BridgePay's note. Key points: they can return the questionnaire by Mon (Jan 19) and want a working session Mon 10:00 AM PT. They explicitly flagged PCI-touching flows and asked about HSM support for private deployment.

Asks: 
- Priya: can you lead the kickoff and confirm our standard HSM/KMS language for contracts (and whether we can surface an HSM-backed option in the private package)?
- Karthik: please be ready to join the working session and have a brief on how we collect network boundary info (suggest a 10–15 min demo of what we need).
- Ben: pls prepare a one-page on proposed timeline and SLO commitments we can share with Ethan (economic buyer) if they want to align commercial to SLA.

I scheduled the working session provisional at Mon Jan 19 10:00 AM PT — will send calendar once Priya confirms availability.

Thanks,
Logan

On Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 09:13 AM Sara Malik <sara.malik@bridgepay.com> wrote:
> Thanks Logan — appreciate the clear recap. Quick replies:
> ...
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Malik <sara.malik@bridgepay.com>, Ethan Cole <ethan.cole@bridgepay.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Jan 2026 10:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Recap + Ask: Buying roster, decision criteria & security scope kickoff

Hi all — thanks for the context, Logan and Sara. I’ll run the security kickoff. Quick plan and what I need from BridgePay to scope: 

1) Kickoff agenda (30-45 min)
   - Introductions & ownership
   - Systems in-scope review (we'll walk the questionnaire answers)
   - Data flows & PCI touchpoints 
   - Deployment topology options (Hosted / Dedicated / Private) and key-management options

2) What we need before the call (please attach to the questionnaire or email):
   - Inventory: short list of hostnames/services (api.bridgepay.com, kafka.*, worker-*), cloud provider and account IDs, and whether any infra is air-gapped
   - Example data flow for tokenization (sequence of services and where tokens are stored)
   - Primary procurement contact + any mandatory contract terms (payment timing, indemnities)

3) HSM / KMS note
   - We support customer-managed KMS (Cloud KMS/HSM) in Dedicated and Private. For Private, we can integrate with customer HSMs (via PKCS#11 or cloud HSM APIs) — that requires coordination on key rotation and a brief on the HSM vendor. I’ll attach standard KMS language after the call.

4) Next steps
   - Logan: confirm Monday 10:00 AM PT working session — I will hold 10:00–10:45 PT for the kickoff.
   - Sara: send the questionnaire answers + inventory ahead of the call.
   - Karthik: prepare a 10-min walkthrough of the intake UI and what fields matter for our scoping.

I’ll also create a shared drive folder and post the intake + checklist (link to follow). If procurement wants to start redlines, Ben, please coordinate with our legal ops and let me know which T&Cs we should prioritize.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Security & Compliance Lead | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0202

Attachments: (to be uploaded) security-kms-language.md

messages:
From: Mallory Reed <mallory@atlashealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@atlashealth.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 09:15:00 -0500
Subject: CAIQ intake - AtlasHealth HIPAA scope (initial)

Hi Logan,

Thanks for agreeing to help AtlasHealth with our CAIQ intake. We need the CAIQ answers and supporting evidence for a limited HIPAA-in-scope integration. To start, could you provide: 
- Completed CAIQ spreadsheet for applicable controls (we flagged ~20 controls)
- Network diagram showing VPC boundaries and any third-party ingress points
- Key rotation logs (last 12 months) for encryption-at-rest keys used in the service
- SOC 2 attestation PDF (if available)

We tried to attach our request (attached). Our procurement team also asked for a short mutual action plan showing delivery dates for artifacts so we can coordinate our audit timeline.

Can you confirm receipt and an ETA?

Thanks,
Mallory Reed
Security PM — AtlasHealth
mallory@atlashealth.com

Attachment: atlas_caiq_request.pdf (1 page — list of requested controls)

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Mallory Reed <mallory@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 10:03:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: CAIQ intake - AtlasHealth HIPAA scope (initial)

Mallory —

Thanks for the note and the attached request. Confirming receipt of atlas_caiq_request.pdf. We've scoped the ask to the 20 flagged CAIQ controls and will provide the completed mapping plus the artifacts you requested.

Next steps from our side:
- Marcus will assemble the CAIQ mapping spreadsheet and pull the network diagram (Marcus: can you attach the diagram version we maintain?)
- Priya will export the key-rotation history and include the KMS attestation pages
- I'll draft a brief mutual action plan with target delivery dates (we aim to have the first bundle to you by end of week).

If you have any restrictions on how artifacts are delivered (e.g., SFTP only, no public Drive links), please let us know. Otherwise we'll share via a secured Drive folder and send a DocuSign acknowledgement for any required NDA addendum.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Security Sales, Redwood Inference
logan@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: (none)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Mallory Reed <mallory@atlashealth.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 11:26:00 -05:00
Subject: CAIQ mapping draft and evidence list

Hi Mallory / Logan —

Attached is our initial CAIQ mapping draft (caiq_mapping_draft.xlsx) covering the 20 controls you flagged. Quick notes:
- Column "Response" contains our short answer and a cross-reference to the artifact in the "Evidence" column.
- Evidence items we can provide immediately: network-diagram-v3.pdf, key-rotation-log.csv, SOC2-2025-attestation.pdf (redacted).
- Open question on CCM_12 / Control: data-segmentation — can you confirm whether you need per-tenant logs or a high-level description? That will affect the artifacts we attach.

Delivery plan proposal (subject to your confirmation):
1) Day 0 (today): share Drive folder + mapping draft (attached)
2) Day 2: deliver network diagram + key rotation logs + SOC2 attestation stub
3) Day 4: provide the final completed CAIQ spreadsheet with any additional traceability notes

We have uploaded the draft and artifacts to a secured folder (link below). If you'd prefer SFTP, we can push to your endpoint — please send details.

Thanks — Marcus
Marcus Lin
Technical Solutions — Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.ai

Attachment: caiq_mapping_draft.xlsx
Related link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2B3C-Atlas-CAIQ

From: Mallory Reed <mallory@atlashealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@atlashealth.com, Nate Johnson <nate.j@clientsigns.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 14:03:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: CAIQ mapping draft and evidence list

Thanks Logan and Marcus — that plan works for us. A couple of clarifications from procurement: 
- Please include a short note in the Drive folder explaining any redactions in the SOC2 PDF.
- For CCM_12 (data segmentation), we need a per-tenant log example (pseudonymized is fine) or a clear statement that logs are not partitioned per tenant and the compensating controls.

Procurement also asked if we can get a DocuSign for the mutual action plan and a simple statement of data residency (US-only). Nate (cc'd) will coordinate the DocuSign envelope on his side.

Mallory
Mallory Reed
Security PM — AtlasHealth
mallory@atlashealth.com

> - Column "Response" contains our short answer and a cross-reference to the artifact in the "Evidence" column.

> - Open question on CCM_12 / Control: data-segmentation — can you confirm whether you need per-tenant logs or a high-level description? That will affect the artifacts we attach.
>
> Delivery plan proposal (subject to your confirmation):
> 1) Day 0 (today): share Drive folder + mapping draft (attached)
> 2) Day 2: deliver network diagram + key rotation logs + SOC2 attestation stub
> 3) Day 4: provide the final completed CAIQ spreadsheet with any additional traceability notes
>
> We have uploaded the draft and artifacts to a secured folder (link below). If you'd prefer SFTP, we can push to your endpoint — please send details.

Delivery plan proposal (subject to your confirmation):
1) Day 0 (today): share Drive folder + mapping draft (attached)
2) Day 2: deliver network diagram + key rotation logs + SOC2 attestation stub
3) Day 4: provide the final completed CAIQ spreadsheet with any additional traceability notes

We have uploaded the draft and artifacts to a secured folder (link below). If you'd prefer SFTP, we can push to your endpoint — please send details.

Thanks — Marcus
Marcus Lin
Technical Solutions — Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.ai

Attachment: caiq_mapping_draft.xlsx
Related link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2B3C-Atlas-CAIQ

Subject: Re: CAIQ mapping draft and evidence list

- For CCM_12 (data segmentation), we need a per-tenant log example (pseudonymized is fine) or a clear statement that logs are not partitioned per tenant and the compensating controls.

Procurement also asked if we can get a DocuSign for the mutual action plan and a simple statement of data residency (US-only). Nate (cc'd) will coordinate the DocuSign envelope on his side.

Mallory

source.subject: Oakridge Health: case study intake & interview confirmation
messages:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sophie Barnes <sophie.barnes@oakridgehealth.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 10:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Oakridge Health: case study intake & interview confirmation

Hi Sophie —

Thanks again for agreeing to speak with us about how Oakridge Health is using Redwood Inference. We want to keep this light and structured so it’s quick for your team. Proposed next steps: 

- 30–40 minute discovery interview (recorded, with your permission) to capture outcomes, metrics, and a short quote
- Draft case study for your review before publication
- Sign-off & media release (one-page) — we’ll send a DocuSign link

Proposed agenda (attached):
1) Quick intros / roles (5m)
2) Business context & goals (10m)
3) Implementation & integration highlights (10m)
4) Outcomes / metrics / quote (10m)
5) Next steps / permissions (5m)

Available windows this month: Jan 20–22 between 09:00–13:00 PT or Jan 27–29 in the afternoon PT. Do any of those work for you?

Please also confirm who should join from Oakridge (technical, product, or comms). We’ll include a short release for recording and use; if you’d prefer we can route through legal first.

Attaching the proposed agenda and a one-page intake checklist for your review.

Best,
Irene Choi
Customer Marketing Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_case_study_agenda_v1.md (text/md), oakridge_prelim_notes.docx (docx)
From: Sophie Barnes <sophie.barnes@oakridgehealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 09:42:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Oakridge Health: case study intake & interview confirmation

Hi Irene —

Thanks — this looks great. We’re happy to participate and to have the session recorded for internal note-taking and quote capture. A few quick answers: 

- We can do Jan 21 at 11:00 AM PT (that works best for both myself and Michael Chen, our Head of Platform).
- Suggested attendees from Oakridge: Michael Chen (Head of Platform), Priya Nair (Lead Data Engineer), and me (Product).
- Our legal team would appreciate a copy of the release before you publish; we’re fine signing after the draft is shared.

I don’t anticipate any major redlines, but we’ll review the release. For the interview, would you like any pre-reads from our side (metrics, slide deck)?

Sophie Barnes
Director, Product Marketing
Oakridge Health

(Please note: I’ve CC’d Michael on this thread.)
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sophie Barnes <sophie.barnes@oakridgehealth.com>, Michael Chen <michael.chen@oakridgehealth.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 08:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Oakridge Health: case study intake & interview confirmation

Sophie — perfect, thank you. Jan 21 at 11:00 AM PT is now penciled in. I’ll send a calendar invite after confirming with my team. A few quick notes / requests for the session: 

- Recording: we’ll record via Zoom for internal transcription and to help us accurately capture quotes. We will not publish audio without your explicit sign-off.
- Pre-reads: if you can share a 1–2 slide summary of the deployment and a short list of outcome metrics (latency improvements, cost savings, user-facing impacts), that will help us keep the interview focused.
- Internal roles: Ben will run the interview and capture technical details; Logan will take notes on adoption & business outcomes.

I’m attaching the draft media release (one page). It’s a simple permissions form — please feel free to send to legal for review. We’ll route the DocuSign for signature once you’ve had a chance to review.

I’ll follow up with a calendar invite and the Zoom link. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Irene
Customer Marketing Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments: case_study_release_v1.pdf (pdf)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sophie Barnes <sophie.barnes@oakridgehealth.com>, Michael Chen <michael.chen@oakridgehealth.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 09:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Oakridge Health: case study intake & interview confirmation

Great — Jan 21, 11:00 AM PT works for me. I’ve created a calendar invite and added a short pre-interview form for your team to fill out (it’s linked in the invite). Link to calendar & Zoom: https://cal.example.com/meet/rdw-oakridge-jan21

Irene: I’ll lead the session and capture the technical notes. Logan — can you confirm you’ll bring the adoption metrics and the customer success timeline?

Sophie / Michael — if it’s helpful we can pull anonymized token-level cost delta figures to illustrate savings; let me know.

Thanks all — looking forward to it.

Ben Carter
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Intermittent empty responses from /v1/generate — FastRetail
messages:
From: Samira Khan <samira@fastretail.co>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 09:20:00 -0800
Subject: Intermittent empty responses from /v1/generate

Hi Logan —

We've seen a handful of requests return an empty string body (200 OK but no text) from /v1/generate over the past 36 hours. This is hitting our checkout microservice and is causing brief feature regressions for a small percentage of users.

What we've collected so far:
- Examples: request IDs listed in attached api-logs-2026-01-12.txt (lines 332, 451, 716)
- One attached screenshot showing the response body blank ""
- No visible spike in latency; errors are not 4xx/5xx — just empty content with normal tokens count header

Can your team confirm whether this is a known issue, or provide next steps for triage? We're happy to provide any more traces.

Thanks,
Samira Khan
SRE, FastRetail
attachments: api-logs-2026-01-12.txt, screenshot-empty-response-01.png

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@fastretail.co>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 10:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intermittent empty responses from /v1/generate

Thanks for the report and the logs, Samira — looping in Marissa (CS) and Sean (SRE) to do an initial pass.

Quick ask to help us narrow scope: can you attach a full request payload and the precise request-id header for one of the examples you highlighted? The logs you sent show the request timestamp but not the outgoing X-Request-ID value that our runtime maps to traces. If you can't share the full payload, a redacted sample with the request-id will still let us tie it to infra traces.

Planned next steps from our side:
- Sean: try to reproduce against the same model and sequence lengths you used (we'll match sampling params)
- Marissa: validate whether this correlates with any rollout or model variant that might emit empty string for certain prompts

We'll update within 24 hours with findings. If this is customer-impacting you can escalate and we'll prioritize.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Manager, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@fastretail.co>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 08:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intermittent empty responses from /v1/generate

Hi Samira — quick update after an initial triage pass. Thanks for the extra log snippet you attached this morning (we were able to match request timestamp to one request-id: req_8f7a2b).

What we did:
- Replayed the request against the same model version (redwood/gptx-3.1) with identical sampling params from your logs; we ran 1500 replays across different sequence lengths and did not observe empty responses.
- Searched infra traces around req_8f7a2b and adjacent time windows. The serving runtime returned the response payload to the load balancer; the payload logged by the edge shows a body payload of "" for that request. However, kernel and runtime telemetry for the serving pod indicate successful token generation for the same call (non-empty).
- Verified there was no model rollout, quantization change, or autoscaler event at the time that could explain delta.

Preliminary conclusion: we do not have conclusive evidence that the model or runtime produced an empty output. The discrepancy appears between the serving runtime and the edge/proxy that returned the final HTTP payload. That points to an edge-level truncation or a transient proxy behavior.

Next steps suggested:
- We recommend FastRetail validate your edge/proxy logs for any middleware that might rewrite or truncate response bodies for certain content lengths (your logs show a correlation with responses under ~24 bytes).
- We will continue to monitor and run longer replay windows; Sean will add a temporary request sampling rule on our side to capture full raw responses (including binary body) for any requests matching your tenant for the next 48 hours.

Attachments: api-logs-2026-01-12.txt (the file you sent), extracted-trace-req_8f7a2b.txt (excerpt)

If you're comfortable, if you can confirm no proxy rewriting or share the edge middleware config we'll circle back and mark this as unresolved until we find an upstream cause. Otherwise, if you prefer to close now and re-open if it recurs, let us know.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Engineer
Redwood Inference

From: Samira Khan <samira@fastretail.co>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 10:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intermittent empty responses from /v1/generate

Thanks Marissa — that's helpful. We audited our proxy (Envoy) config and found no response body rewrite rules. We did see one transient CPU spike on our edge instances right when req_8f7a2b occurred, which looks like it overlapped with a non-blocking flush in our logging agent. Given your reproduction attempts came back negative and our audit didn't surface a middleware rule, we agree to mark this as "monitoring / no repro" for now.

Request: could Redwood add a short-lived webhook or a lightweight alert that posts to our slack if a 200 response arrives with an empty text body for our tenant in the next 72 hours? That would let us catch a recurrence quickly and share full traces.

If we see further hits we'll reopen INC-7482. Appreciate the quick triage and the extra trace extraction.

Best,
Samira
SRE, FastRetail

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 9:20 AM Samira Khan <samira@fastretail.co> wrote:
> Hi Logan —
> 
> We've seen a handful of requests return an empty string body (200 OK but no text) from /v1/generate over the past 36 hours. This is hitting our checkout microservice and is causing brief feature regressions for a small percentage of users.
> 
> What we've collected so far:
> - Examples: request IDs listed in attached api-logs-2026-01-12.txt (lines 332, 451, 716)
> - One attached screenshot showing the response body blank ""
> - No visible spike in latency; errors are not 4xx/5xx — just empty content with normal tokens count header
> 
> Can your team confirm whether this is a known issue, or provide next steps for triage? We're happy to provide any more traces.
> 
> Thanks,
> Samira Khan
> SRE, FastRetail
> attachments: api-logs-2026-01-12.txt, screenshot-empty-response-01.png

If you're comfortable, if you can confirm no proxy rewriting or share the edge middleware config we'll circle back and mark this as unresolved until we find an upstream cause. Otherwise, if you prefer to close now and re-open if it recurs, let us know.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Engineer
Redwood Inference

From: Samira Khan <samira@fastretail.co>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 10:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intermittent empty responses from /v1/generate

Thanks Marissa — that's helpful. We audited our proxy (Envoy) config and found no response body rewrite rules. We did see one transient CPU spike on our edge instances right when req_8f7a2b occurred, which looks like it overlapped with a non-blocking flush in our logging agent. Given your reproduction attempts came back negative and our audit didn't surface a middleware rule, we agree to mark this as \"monitoring / no repro\" for now.

Request: could Redwood add a short-lived webhook or a lightweight alert that posts to our slack if a 200 response arrives with an empty text body for our tenant in the next 72 hours? That would let us catch a recurrence quickly and share full traces.

If we see further hits we'll reopen INC-7482. Appreciate the quick triage and the extra trace extraction.

Best,
Samira

Samira Khan

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>

Thanks for the report and the logs, Samira — looping in Marissa (CS) and Sean (SRE) to do an initial pass.

Quick ask to help us narrow scope: can you attach a full request payload and the precise request-id header for one of the examples you highlighted? The logs you sent show the request timestamp but not the outgoing X-Request-ID value that our runtime maps to traces. If you can't share the full payload, a redacted sample with the request-id will still let us tie it to infra traces.

Planned next steps from our side:
- Sean: try to reproduce against the same model and sequence lengths you used (we'll match sampling params)
- Marissa: validate whether this correlates with any rollout or model variant that might emit empty string for certain prompts

We'll update within 24 hours with findings. If this is customer-impacting you can escalate and we'll prioritize.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Manager, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@fastretail.co>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 08:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intermittent empty responses from /v1/generate

Hi Samira — quick update after an initial triage pass. Thanks for the extra log snippet you attached this morning (we were able to match request timestamp to one request-id: req_8f7a2b).

What we did:
- Replayed the request against the same model version (redwood/gptx-3.1) with identical sampling params from your logs; we ran 1500 replays across different sequence lengths and did not observe empty responses.
- Searched infra traces around req_8f7a2b and adjacent time windows. The serving runtime returned the response payload to the load balancer; the payload logged by the edge shows a body payload of \"\" for that request. However, kernel and runtime telemetry for the serving pod indicate successful token generation for the same call (non-empty).
- Verified there was no model rollout, quantization change, or autoscaler event at the time that could explain delta.

Preliminary conclusion: we do not have conclusive evidence that the model or runtime produced an empty output. The discrepancy appears between the serving runtime and the edge/proxy that returned the final HTTP payload. That points to an edge-level truncation or a transient proxy behavior.

Next steps suggested:
- We recommend FastRetail validate your edge/proxy logs for any middleware that might rewrite or truncate response bodies for certain content lengths (your logs show a correlation with responses under ~24 bytes).
- We will continue to monitor and run longer replay windows; Sean will add a temporary request sampling rule on our side to capture full raw responses (including binary body) for any requests matching your tenant for the next 48 hours.

Attachments: api-logs-2026-01-12.txt (the file you sent), extracted-trace-req_8f7a2b.txt (excerpt)

source.subject: Ida Health - exception request for backup/DR retention & execution plan (DocuSign)
source.messages:
From: Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: legal@idahealth.com
Date: Mon, 5 Jan 2026 09:13:00 -0800
Subject: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Hi Logan —

Thanks for the call today. As discussed, Ida Health needs a limited carveout in Section 4 (Retention & Deletion) so that short-lived encrypted backups retained for disaster recovery can be kept for a defined period beyond active deletion timelines. We've attached our proposed redlines to the DPA and a simple DR flow diagram to show the narrow scope.

High-level ask: allow encrypted backup copies for DR for up to 120 days post-deletion, with access restricted to restore-only operations, logged and limited to named custodians. We also included a request to permit cross-border replication to our EU failover region (Ida Cloud) for resilience.

Attached: Ida_DPA_redlines_20260105.pdf; Ida_DR_flow_diagram.png

Can Redwood confirm whether you can accept a timeboxed backup carveout with: (1) encryption at rest/in transit, (2) strict restore-only access controls, (3) KMS key separation or equivalent, and (4) an audit log of any restore events? If acceptable, we'd like to get the DPA updated and executed via DocuSign this month.

Best,
Emma Carter
Senior Counsel, Ida Health

---

On Mon, Jan 5, 2026 at 10:02 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jan 2026 10:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Hi Emma —

Thanks for sending the redlines and the diagram, very helpful. High-level yes — we can support a narrowly scoped carveout for backups used solely for DR restores. Before we move to DocuSign, a couple of internal checks and small edits are needed so we capture safeguards and keep the exception limited.

Next steps from Redwood:
- Loop in Legal (Laura) for contract language tweaks and to confirm the precise placement of the exception (suggested: carveout as a defined-scope clause under "Retention & Deletion" rather than creating a blanket exception).
- Privacy (Marissa) to verify the search/restore process and ensure customer notification obligations are preserved.
- Security (Vivek) to confirm KMS/access controls and whether we can support a customer-managed key for Ida or require Redwood-managed keys with strict role separation.

If you can accept a 90-day maximum retention for DR snapshots (rather than 120) and the requirement that backups are encrypted and access-controlled for restore-only purposes with logged restores and quarterly audits, I think we can get to a signable version quickly. Laura — copying you for contract redlines.

Logan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 5, 2026 at 11:34 AM Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jan 2026 11:34:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Emma —

Appreciate the clear diagram and redlines. I reviewed the PDF and propose the following contract-level changes to keep the carveout tightly scoped: 
1) Language: add an explicit definition: "DR Snapshot" = offline, encrypted, immutable backup copy created exclusively for restoration following a service disruption.
2) Timebox: Redwood will retain DR Snapshots for no more than ninety (90) days from creation or thirty (30) days after Customer request for deletion, whichever is sooner.
3) Purpose limitation: Access to DR Snapshots is permitted solely to perform restoration to Customer-designated tenancy; no other processing is permitted.
4) Subprocessor / transfer constraints: Any cross-border replication permitted only to processors/subprocessors listed in Attachment A; Redwood will provide prior notice of adding subprocessor in accordance with Section 6.
5) Audit & access controls: Restores require two-person authorization and generate a tamper-evident audit record; restore events tied to a ticket id must be shared monthly with Customer security contact.

On the KMS matter — we can support customer-managed keys (bring-your-own-key) for DR snapshots where the customer provides a cloud KMS ARN. If Ida prefers Redwood-managed keys, we will bind key-access to a limited set of crypto-operators and log all unwrap operations.

If those edits look acceptable to Ida, we can fold them into the redline and route via DocuSign. I will mark the DPA redline PDF with tracked changes and re-share.

Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 6, 2026 at 08:22 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jan 2026 08:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Emma —

A few privacy checks from my side: 
- Please confirm whether the snapshot retention window needs to begin from snapshot creation or from the date of account deletion; Laura's suggested language covers both but Ida should confirm the trigger you prefer.
- For restores that may involve PII, we require a pre-approved restore plan (minimal scope, fields, and duration) and a requirement that restored data is expunged from any transient environments within 7 calendar days after restore completion.
- Recommend adding a sentence to DPA Section 3.2: "Customer will be notified within five (5) business days of any restore event involving Customer Personal Data."

From a compliance standpoint, limiting retention to 90 days and ensuring explicit restore logging + notification keeps the carveout defensible. Happy to iterate on wording.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Privacy Program Manager, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 15:45:00 -08:00 Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 15:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Emma / Ida team —

Security sign-off items: we can accept the 90-day timebox with the following operational controls added to the redline: 
- DR snapshots are encrypted with AES-256 at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit.
- KMS: customer-managed keys supported (BYOK) OR Redwood-managed keys with Hardware Security Module (HSM) key separation.
- Access: restores require two operators, documented ticket id, and multi-factor authentication for both individuals.
- Replication: cross-region replication allowed only to named failover regions; any transfer will be via encrypted channel and will be logged with IP and actor.
- Subprocessors: any third-party DR-site provider will be listed as a subprocessor and bound by the same retention and access constraints.

I will attach a short operations playbook snippet to the DPA as an Appendix (operational details, not contractual obligations) to clarify restore procedures. That should make audits straightforward.

If Ida agrees to the 90-day window and two-person restore control, Laura can update the DPA. When we have the final redline, Logan — please circulate the DocuSign envelope and I'll approve the Appendix.

Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Security, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 16:10:00 -08:00 Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com> wrote:
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, legal@idahealth.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 16:10:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Logan/Laura/Vivek/Marissa —

Thanks — Ida agrees to the 90-day retention cap and the two-person restore control. We accept the BYOK option for KMS and will provide a KMS ARN if needed. We also accept the Appendix as non-contractual operational detail, so long as the contractual DPA language includes the 90-day cap, purpose limitation, and restore notification within five business days.

We're ready to sign once Laura circulates the updated redline. Please send via DocuSign and include the HubSpot deal id in the envelope notes (IDA-2026-1192).

DocuSign link for execution: https://app.docusign.com/idahealth/ENVELOPE-6f3b2c (attached in Ida_DocuSign_Link.txt)

Best,
Emma Carter
Senior Counsel, Ida Health

[Attachments referenced: Ida_DPA_redlines_20260105.pdf (redlined DPA with tracked changes), Ida_DR_flow_diagram.png (diagram), Ida_DocuSign_Link.txt (shortlink to envelope)]

On Mon, Jan 5, 2026 at 10:02 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jan 2026 10:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Hi Emma —

Thanks for sending the redlines and the diagram, very helpful. High-level yes — we can support a narrowly scoped carveout for backups used solely for DR restores. Before we move to DocuSign, a couple of internal checks and small edits are needed so we capture safeguards and keep the exception limited.

Next steps from Redwood:
- Loop in Legal (Laura) for contract language tweaks and to confirm the precise placement of the exception (suggested: carveout as a defined-scope clause under "Retention & Deletion" rather than creating a blanket exception).
- Privacy (Marissa) to verify the search/restore process and ensure customer notification obligations are preserved.
- Security (Vivek) to confirm KMS/access controls and whether we can support a customer-managed key for Ida or require Redwood-managed keys with strict role separation.

If you can accept a 90-day maximum retention for DR snapshots (rather than 120) and the requirement that backups are encrypted and access-controlled for restore-only purposes with logged restores and quarterly audits, I think we can get to a signable version quickly. Laura — copying you for contract redlines.

Logan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 5, 2026 at 11:34 AM Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jan 2026 11:34:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Emma —

Appreciate the clear diagram and redlines. I reviewed the PDF and propose the following contract-level changes to keep the carveout tightly scoped: 
1) Language: add an explicit definition: "DR Snapshot" = offline, encrypted, immutable backup copy created exclusively for restoration following a service disruption.
2) Timebox: Redwood will retain DR Snapshots for no more than ninety (90) days from creation or thirty (30) days after Customer request for deletion, whichever is sooner.
3) Purpose limitation: Access to DR Snapshots is permitted solely to perform restoration to Customer-designated tenancy; no other processing is permitted.
4) Subprocessor / transfer constraints: Any cross-border replication permitted only to processors/subprocessors listed in Attachment A; Redwood will provide prior notice of adding subprocessor in accordance with Section 6.
5) Audit & access controls: Restores require two-person authorization and generate a tamper-evident audit record; restore events tied to a ticket id must be shared monthly with Customer security contact.

On the KMS matter — we can support customer-managed keys (bring-your-own-key) for DR snapshots where the customer provides a cloud KMS ARN. If Ida prefers Redwood-managed keys, we will bind key-access to a limited set of crypto-operators and log all unwrap operations.

If those edits look acceptable to Ida, we can fold them into the redline and route via DocuSign. I will mark the DPA redline PDF with tracked changes and re-share.

Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 6, 2026 at 08:22 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jan 2026 08:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Emma —

A few privacy checks from my side: 
- Please confirm whether the snapshot retention window needs to begin from snapshot creation or from the date of account deletion; Laura's suggested language covers both but Ida should confirm the trigger you prefer.
- For restores that may involve PII, we require a pre-approved restore plan (minimal scope, fields, and duration) and a requirement that restored data is expunged from any transient environments within 7 calendar days after restore completion.
- Recommend adding a sentence to DPA Section 3.2: "Customer will be notified within five (5) business days of any restore event involving Customer Personal Data."

From a compliance standpoint, limiting retention to 90 days and ensuring explicit restore logging + notification keeps the carveout defensible. Happy to iterate on wording.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Privacy Program Manager, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 15:45:00 -08:00 Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 15:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Emma / Ida team —

Security sign-off items: we can accept the 90-day timebox with the following operational controls added to the redline: 
- DR snapshots are encrypted with AES-256 at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit.
- KMS: customer-managed keys supported (BYOK) OR Redwood-managed keys with Hardware Security Module (HSM) key separation.
- Access: restores require two operators, documented ticket id, and multi-factor authentication for both individuals.
- Replication: cross-region replication allowed only to named failover regions; any transfer will be via encrypted channel and will be logged with IP and actor.
- Subprocessors: any third-party DR-site provider will be listed as a subprocessor and bound by the same retention and access constraints.

I will attach a short operations playbook snippet to the DPA as an Appendix (operational details, not contractual obligations) to clarify restore procedures. That should make audits straightforward.

If Ida agrees to the 90-day window and two-person restore control, Laura can update the DPA. When we have the final redline, Logan — please circulate the DocuSign envelope and I'll approve the Appendix.

Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Security, Redwood Inference

---

On Mon, Jan 12, 2026 at 16:10:00 -08:00 Emma Carter <emma.carter@idahealth.com> wrote:
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, legal@idahealth.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 16:10:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Backup/DR retention carveout - proposed DPA redlines attached

Logan/Laura/Vivek/Marissa —

Thanks — Ida agrees to the 90-day retention cap and the two-person restore control. We accept the BYOK option for KMS and will provide a KMS ARN if needed. We also accept the Appendix as non-contractual operational detail, so long as the contractual DPA language includes the 90-day cap, purpose limitation, and restore notification within five business days.

We're ready to sign once Laura circulates the updated redline. Please send via DocuSign and include the HubSpot deal id in the envelope notes (IDA-2026-1192).

DocuSign link for execution: https://app.docusign.com/idahealth/ENVELOPE-6f3b2c (attached in Ida_DocuSign_Link.txt)

Best,
Emma Carter
Senior Counsel, Ida Health

[Attachments referenced: Ida_DPA_redlines_20260105.pdf (redlined DPA with tracked changes), Ida_DR_flow_diagram.png (diagram), Ida_DocuSign_Link.txt (shortlink to envelope)]

source.subject: MSA + Order Form: DPA export-control mapping and technical evidence runbook
messages:
From: Amira Hassan <amira@novarelhealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Dec 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Initial DPA redlines & export-control mapping request

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo last week — we’re excited about moving forward. Legal has attached our redline of the DPA and a short note on where we think controller/processor roles sit for the pilot. Two asks from our side to keep procurement moving:

1) Can Redwood confirm whether you can accept the SCCs-based standard clause flow we proposed in section 3? We noted a few lines that reference EU->US export controls and wanted to see if you need a transfer impact addendum.

2) We need a short implementation timeline / evidence runbook for procurement to review (SOC2 evidence + subprocessor list + KMS cert). Procurement wants to see a 2-week evidence delivery plan if possible.

I’ve attached our redline (Novarel_DPA_redline_v3.docx) and a simple spreadsheet showing the SCCs mapping and proposed roles. Ben was on the call and copied.

Can you get back with a quick plan and who on your side will own each evidence item? We’re trying to hit a signing milestone by the end of December.

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call.

— Amira Hassan
Director, Procurement
Novarel Health
amira@novarelhealth.com
Attachment: Novarel_DPA_redline_v3.docx (docx, 145KB)
Attachment: SCCs_mapping_matrix.xlsx (xlsx, 32KB)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Amira Hassan <amira@novarelhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Dec 2025 11:07:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Initial DPA redlines & export-control mapping request

Hi Amira — thanks for the redline and the clear asks. Looping in Marissa (privacy & contracts) and Ben (solutions/ops) to scope the evidence list and confirm SCCs approach. High level next steps from Redwood:

- Marissa will review the DPA redlines and flag any absolute blockers on SCCs / roles by EOD tomorrow.
- Ben will map the evidence we can deliver in 10 business days and propose owners for each item.
- I’ll put together a brief transfer-impact note (if needed) and flag whether a formal transfer addendum is required for your jurisdiction.

Can you confirm whether Novarel needs a signed transfer addendum before DocuSign or if a post-signing evidence delivery plan is acceptable to procurement? That will change how aggressively we need to push the evidence sprint.

Propose a 30-minute sync tomorrow (Thu) at 9:30am PT / 17:30 CET to run through the matrix — Ben and Marissa, does that time work?

Thanks — we’ll review the files now.

— Logan
Head of Sales, EMEA
Redwood Inference
Attachment: Novarel_DPA_redline_v3.docx (referenced)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Dec 2025 09:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Draft evidence runbook & SCCs mapping (internal)

Logan / Marissa — following up from the call: I pulled together a quick draft evidence runbook and populated the SCCs mapping sheet with what we control vs. what we can commit to provide. High-level notes:

- SOC2 report (current) — available within 48 hours as redacted PDF. Owner: Ben Carter (ops).
- Subprocessor list and mapping — export-ready CSV, current as-of this morning. Owner: Platform Trust (cc’d internal).
- KMS attestations — we can provide KMS integration diagram and authority summary; full certs may require customer NDA route. Owner: Infra Eng (needs engineering contact).
- Transfer impact addendum — I believe we can avoid an addendum if we accept SCCs and provide the evidence bundle. If Novarel requires extra contractual wording, that’s a legal ask.

I attached: evidence_manifest_template.csv and an updated SCCs_mapping_matrix.xlsx (added column: "evidence_owner"). Proposed timeline to commit to procurement: 10 business days to assemble a redacted evidence pack if Novarel accepts post-signing delivery. If they insist on pre-signing delivery we need to accelerate and pull in Infra Eng lead — probable delivery in 6 business days with an engineering weekend sprint.

Questions for Marissa:
- Any DPA redlines that legally require a transfer addendum up front?
- Can we accept the delegation matrix as-is (we flagged a line in section 4 that implies controller obligations on Redwood)?

If all good, I’ll prepare a short email to Amira with the timeline and link to the Drive folder.

— Ben
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
Attachment: evidence_manifest_template.csv (csv, 2KB)
Attachment: SCCs_mapping_matrix.xlsx (xlsx, 38KB)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Dec 2025 16:43:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Draft evidence runbook & SCCs mapping (legal review)

Thanks Ben — helpful summary. Quick legal read:

1) SCCs approach: We can accept the SCCs flow proposed by Novarel with one change: add a short clause clarifying Redwood acts as processor with defined limited processing for the pilot (we should not take controller-level obligations). I’ve inserted tracked changes in Novarel_DPA_redline_v3.docx (see attachment).

2) Transfer addendum: Not required pre-signing if Novarel accepts a contractual commitment to provide evidence within an agreed timeline (we should capture the timeline in an Order Form addendum). If they insist on pre-signing proof of KMS certs, escalate — that’s an engineering deliverable and may delay signing.

3) Delegation / roles: I flagged section 4 where Novarel’s redline shifts liability to Redwood for data classification; we need to preserve customer responsibility there. I suggest we propose alternative language (two-line replacement) — included in the tracked doc.

4) Timeline: Legal is comfortable with the proposed 10 business day evidence pack post-signing. For procurement comfort, Ben please prepare a bulletized list of what will be in the pack (SOC2, subprocessors, KMS diagram, encryption-at-rest notes, audit log sample). We’ll include those bullets in an Order Form addendum to be signed alongside the MSA.

Next steps I recommend we send Amira: a single reply confirming (a) SCCs accepted with the small role clarification, (b) proposed evidence pack contents, (c) commitment to deliver in 10 business days post-signing, and (d) a DocuSign path for the Order Form addendum.

I’ll attach the tracked redline and the suggested Order Form addendum draft. If we get the green light from Logan, I’ll edit the doc and send to Amira.

— Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference
Attachment: Novarel_DPA_redline_v3_tracked.docx (docx, 158KB)
Attachment: OrderForm_DPA_addendum_draft.docx (docx, 12KB)

source.subject: Mid‑month capacity smoothing & one‑time true‑up amendment request
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Jared Klein <jared.k@brightlane.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-11-15 09:02 -0800
Subject: Mid‑month capacity smoothing & one‑time true‑up amendment request

Jared —

Thanks again for the quick sync this morning. Per our conversation, BrightLane is expecting a short, high‑volume window for the holiday test that will exceed your committed headroom for ~36 hours (predicted peak Nov 20‑21). To avoid service friction, we want to propose a one‑time, provisional quota increase + a temporary reduced overrun rate for that window and a small pro‑rata true‑up rather than full monthly overage billing.

Attached: a first‑pass amendment with the numbers we discussed and the usage extract I pulled (usage-report-Nov1-14.csv). Key asks from our side:

source.subject: Re: Greenline logistics — demo time coordination across regions
messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Sophia Burns <sophia.burns@greenline.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Nov 2025 08:42:17 -0500
Subject: Confirming Nov 12 demo (11:00 CET)

Hi Sophia —

Thanks again for the interest — we have 11:00 CET on the calendar for Wednesday, Nov 12 for a 30–45 minute walkthrough of Redwood Inference (agenda attached). Two quick notes: I will be traveling that week and my calendar currently shows a flight arriving Nov 12 at 10:45 CET, which makes the 11:00 CET slot pretty tight on my end. Mateo will be on the call as backup for technical questions.

Would you be open to moving the demo 45–60 minutes later on Nov 12 so I can join from the hotel, or alternatively we can shift to the morning of Nov 13 CET if that works better for the EU team?

Attaching the current agenda for reference.

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
e: evelyn.hart@redwood.com | m: +1 415 555 0192

Attachment: Greenline-demo-agenda-v2.pdf
From: Sophia Burns <sophia.burns@greenline.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Timothy Cho <timothy.cho@greenline.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Nov 2025 14:15:03 +0100
Subject: Re: Confirming Nov 12 demo (11:00 CET)

Hi Evelyn —

Thanks for the heads up. Nov 12 11:00 CET was the time we had agreed with our EU ops team, but there is some flexibility. A couple of constraints on our side: our EU ops lead needs it before lunch (preferably ending by 12:30 CET) because they have a customer site visit in the afternoon. Our APAC engineering lead is travelling but indicated they could dial in early Nov 13 SGT (morning for APAC); unfortunately that creates a stretch for our US procurement folks.

Options from our side:
- Move Nov 12 to 11:45–12:15 CET (slim but doable)
- Move to Nov 12 14:00 UTC (would be 15:00 CET, OK for EU, late for US)
- Move to Nov 13 09:30 SGT (good for APAC, early for EU)

We can also share a Calendly if that’s easier: https://calendly.com/greenline/demo-30m

—Sophia
Sophia Burns
Enterprise Programs | Greenline Logistics
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Sophia Burns <sophia.burns@greenline.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Nov 2025 09:30:55 -05:00
Subject: Re: Confirming Nov 12 demo (11:00 CET)

Sophia — thanks for the quick reply. Looping in Karthik who will run the technical portion and Logan for capacity/quoting questions.

Quick clarification on time conversions — we historically see confusion here because Europe recently moved back off DST (Nov 2) and some calendars still show CET/CET+1 inconsistently. To be explicit:
- 11:00 CET on Nov 12 is 10:00 UTC / 05:00 EST (US)
- 14:00 UTC on Nov 12 is 15:00 CET / 10:00 EST

If your team prefers to keep it Nov 12 and still include the APAC engineer remotely, I think 14:00 UTC (15:00 CET) is the cleanest cross-region window — it keeps the EU counterpart in afternoon, gives me time after arrival to join, and is not too late for US participants.

If that works, Karthik — can you run the demo at 14:00 UTC and focus the agenda on throughput/latency and our rollout options? I’ve attached my travel itinerary (.ics) so you can see my arrival time.

Thanks all,
Evelyn

Attachment: evelyn-travel-itinerary.ics
From: Sophia Burns <sophia.burns@greenline.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Timothy Cho <timothy.cho@greenline.com>
Date: Thu, 6 Nov 2025 09:20:11 +0100
Subject: Re: Confirming Nov 12 demo (11:00 CET)

Great, thanks for spelling out the conversions — that helps. 14:00 UTC on Nov 12 should be fine for our EU ops lead (they can stay until 16:00 CET), and it’s tolerable for US stakeholders if we keep the session to 30 minutes and share a recording. APAC will join remotely when possible.

Please use this Meet link for the session: https://meet.google.com/rwd-greenline-demo (we'll record). Also linking our HubSpot deal for reference: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/company/GL-3782

We’re good to confirm Nov 12, 14:00 UTC. Thanks everyone — see you then.

—Sophia

P.S. if anything changes last-minute (travel delays or flight reroute) we’ll post an update in this thread.
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Sophia Burns <sophia.burns@greenline.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 6 Nov 2025 10:03:00 +00:00
Subject: Re: Confirming Nov 12 demo (11:00 CET)

All — got it, Nov 12 14:00 UTC is locked on our side. I’ll lead the technical walkthrough and start with the quick latency/throughput benchmarks for a cross-region serving pattern, then demo rollout controls (canary, autoscaling, and routing). I’ll keep the live portion to 25 minutes and leave time at the end for Q&A.

Agenda snapshot:
- 0–5m: quick intros and goals
- 5–20m: live demo + perf highlights
- 20–25m: rollout and ops notes (dedicated/private)
- 25–30m: Q&A

If you have any specific workloads or sample prompts you want me to try, drop them here before EOD Nov 11.

Thanks — looking forward to it.
Karthik
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
