source.subject: Routing attestations and TIA scope for Zenith Health onboarding
messages:
From: Amelia Rosen <amelia.rosen@zenithhealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Zenith Legal <legal@zenithhealth.com>
Date: 2025-10-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Initial evidence request: SCCs / UK IDTA / TIA scoping

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. As we prepare to move toward a security review with our legal and privacy teams, they asked me to collect some concrete artifacts to map how data will flow and what transfer mechanisms we expect to rely on. In particular: 

- A simple CSV listing subprocessors and the locations where processing occurs (region-level is fine).
- Any draft or template language you intend to use for the UK Addendum / IDTA.
- A short note on whether you anticipate relying on SCCs only, or SCCs + UK IDTA + TIA/DTIA for cross-border processing.

We'd like to present a one-pager to our privacy committee by end of week; is there anything you can share today or tomorrow?

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call if that helps.

Best,
Amelia Rosen
Head of Privacy, Zenith Health
amelia.rosen@zenithhealth.com
attachments: none
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Amelia Rosen <amelia.rosen@zenithhealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-10-28T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Initial evidence request: SCCs / UK IDTA / TIA scoping

Amelia — thanks for the clear ask. I've looped in Kimberly (privacy ops) and Jonas (compliance engineering) to get the artifacts together. Quick answers up front: 

- Our standard approach for EU->outside-EU processing is to offer SCCs (EU standard contractual clauses) + an operational TIA for high-risk categories. For UK-resident data, we provide the UK IDTA / UK addendum path where requested.
- We can produce a region-level subprocessor map and a routing-attestation template.

I've attached a preliminary transfer_mapping.csv that lists processors and rough locations, and a redlined_idta_questions.pdf with the questions we normally expect back from customers (so your legal team can see our positions). Also attaching a blank routing_attestation_template.xlsx you can use to collect runbook-level info from teams.

Attachments: transfer_mapping.csv (CSV stub), redlined_idta_questions.pdf (PDF stub), routing_attestation_template.xlsx (XLSX stub)

Next steps proposed: 
1) Kimberly to confirm which safeguards we would include in a Zenith TIA tailored to your clinical data flows.
2) Jonas to provide a short note on data-at-rest and egress routing controls (logs, region pinning, subprocessor isolation).
3) If your legal team has specific redlines to the UK IDTA, send them over and we can surface internal signoff timelines.

I can also host a 30-minute working session tomorrow afternoon if that's helpful.

—Logan
Logan Wright
Director, Privacy Partnerships
Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Amelia Rosen <amelia.rosen@zenithhealth.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-10-29T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Initial evidence request: SCCs / UK IDTA / TIA scoping

Hi Amelia — quick technical clarifications from our side that will help scope the TIA: 

- Subprocessor tiers: we have three tiers — Redwood core (control plane, billing), inference runtime (dedicated GPU pools), and optional analytics/observability vendors. The CSV Logan shared tags each processor with a tier column.
- Routing patterns: by default requests from EU tenants are served from EU regions. If your integration uses our cross-region fallback we will capture that in the TIA as a conditional pathway; please confirm whether the product will use cross-region fallback for disaster recovery.
- Logs and retention: audit logs for inference requests are retained in-region by default for 30 days; longer retention can be provisioned under contract.

On the UK IDTA: our standard addendum is conservative around subprocessors and includes an attestation clause to be completed by the customer for any additional routing exceptions. If Zenith expects any on-prem or dedicated-hosted model variants, flag that now since it can change the scope of the technical assessment.

For the working session, I'm available 10/29 15:00-16:00 PT. Would that work to review the mapping and confirm which safeguards are required?

Best,
Kimberly Park
Senior Privacy Counsel, Redwood Inference
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
To: Amelia Rosen <amelia.rosen@zenithhealth.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-10-30T16:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Initial evidence request: SCCs / UK IDTA / TIA scoping

Amelia / team — thanks for the quick turnaround. Follow-up summary and concrete asks (so your privacy/legal committee has a one-page appendix):

1) Delivered artifacts (attached): transfer_mapping.csv, redlined_idta_questions.pdf, routing_attestation_template.xlsx
2) We propose a two-track approach for Zenith: 
   - Track A (default): SCCs + operational TIA. Covers EU data processed in EU infra with documented subprocessor map.
   - Track B (UK-specific): UK IDTA + localized TIA where UK-resident data is routed to UK-only hosts (if requested).
3) Action items for Zenith team by 2025-11-03: 
   - Confirm whether your integration will use cross-region fallback (yes/no).
   - Complete routing_attestation_template.xlsx for any environments that require non-EU processing.
   - Share any required redlines to the IDTA by 2025-11-05 so procurement can align.

If you'd like, we can convert this summary into a short memo for the committee. Please confirm preferred format (PDF or Google Doc).

Regards,
Jonas Weber
Compliance Engineer, Redwood Inference

On Tue, Oct 28, 2025 at 11:03 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Amelia — thanks for the clear ask. I've looped in Kimberly (privacy ops) and Jonas (compliance engineering) to get the artifacts together. Quick answers up front: 
> 
> - Our standard approach for EU->outside-EU processing is to offer SCCs (EU standard contractual clauses) + an operational TIA for high-risk categories. For UK-resident data, we provide the UK IDTA / UK addendum path where requested.
> - We can produce a region-level subprocessor map and a routing-attestation template.
> 
> I've attached a preliminary transfer_mapping.csv that lists processors and rough locations, and a redlined_idta_questions.pdf with the questions we normally expect back from customers (so your legal team can see our positions). Also attaching a blank routing_attestation_template.xlsx you can use to collect runbook-level info from teams.
> 
> —Logan

source.subject: OneTrust: scoped evidence request for Acme Payments onboarding
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Wed, 03 Sep 2025 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: OneTrust: scoped evidence request for Acme Payments onboarding

Priya / Marcus,

Acme Payments has asked us to populate a scoped evidence package in OneTrust so they can complete their procurement security review. Jordan from OneTrust copied below and said the portal record will accept a reduced set of artifacts for cloud-only SaaS vendors. Can you please: 

- Priya: pull a redacted SOC 2 Type II (audit date within last 12 months) and a network diagram that highlights customer-facing egress points.
- Marcus: export the current egress firewall config snippet and a short note on key management (KMS) usage for model artifacts.

We should aim to upload everything to the portal by EOD Friday so Acme can finish their vendor assessment next week. I started a Google Drive folder and put a checklist (link below).

Checklist link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/0AExampleSharedEvidenceLink

Thanks —
Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: onboarding-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, 48KB)]

From: Jordan Michaels <jordan.michaels@onetrust.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: security@acmepayments.com
Date: Thu, 04 Sep 2025 11:07:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: OneTrust: scoped evidence request for Acme Payments onboarding

Hi Logan —

Thanks for the heads up. I provisioned the portal record for Redwood (record id 123456) and set the profile to the "scoped evidence" template for SaaS vendors; that template only requires: redacted SOC 2 Type II, network diagram with boundary, and an inventory of encryption/KMS controls.

Upload steps: 
1) Sign in at https://app.onetrust.com/portal/company/ACME/inbound/record/123456 (use the temporary link).
2) Under "Evidence" choose "Upload Files" and tag them as SOC2 / Network Diagram / Encryption Controls.
3) For KMS notes, a short plain-text file is fine. If anything is sensitive please mark it as "Restricted" in the portal and we will keep it scoped to procurement reviewers.

I also attached the Acme evidence template and a helpful example of the minimum network diagram they accept. Let me know if you run into permission issues — I can open a support ticket.

Jordan Michaels
Vendor Onboarding, OneTrust Professional Services
[Attachment: acme-evidence-template.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, 21KB)]

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>
To: Jordan Michaels <jordan.michaels@onetrust.com>, security@acmepayments.com
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Sep 2025 13:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: OneTrust: scoped evidence request for Acme Payments onboarding

Forwarding the upload — attaching the files we plan to submit. I redacted internal contact emails and removed customer sample data from the SOC. Marcus included a short KMS note. Please confirm these meet the scoped template and I will proceed to upload.

[Attachment: soc2-type2-redacted.pdf (application/pdf, 2.1MB)]
[Attachment: network-diagram.pdf (application/pdf, 450KB)]
[Attachment: egress-firewall-config-snippet.txt (text/plain, 12KB)]

--Original Message--
On Thu, 04 Sep 2025 at 11:07 AM -04:00 Jordan Michaels <jordan.michaels@onetrust.com> wrote:
> Hi Logan —
> 
> Thanks for the heads up. I provisioned the portal record for Redwood (record id 123456) and set the profile to the "scoped evidence" template for SaaS vendors; that template only requires: redacted SOC 2 Type II, network diagram with boundary, and an inventory of encryption/KMS controls.
> 
> Upload steps: 
> 1) Sign in at https://app.onetrust.com/portal/company/ACME/inbound/record/123456 (use the temporary link).
> 2) Under "Evidence" choose "Upload Files" and tag them as SOC2 / Network Diagram / Encryption Controls.
> 3) For KMS notes, a short plain-text file is fine. If anything is sensitive please mark it as "Restricted" in the portal and we will keep it scoped to procurement reviewers.
> 
> I also attached the Acme evidence template and a helpful example of the minimum network diagram they accept. Let me know if you run into permission issues — I can open a support ticket.
> 
> Jordan Michaels
> Vendor Onboarding, OneTrust Professional Services

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.inference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Jordan Michaels <jordan.michaels@onetrust.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Sep 2025 16:11:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: OneTrust: scoped evidence request for Acme Payments onboarding

Thanks — uploaded the three items to the portal record and set the SOC file to "Restricted" per Jordan's guidance. Notes: 
- SOC 2 redaction: removed written contact lists and customer-specific artifacts.
- Network diagram: shows only vendor-facing ingress/egress and the CDN boundary.
- KMS note attached as text in the portal (short description of AWS KMS usage for model keys).

Portal upload link: https://app.onetrust.com/portal/company/ACME/inbound/record/123456/evidence

Jordan / Acme team, please confirm the artifacts look sufficient or advise on any additional items.

Priya
Priya Natarajan
Security & Compliance Manager, Redwood Inference

- Marcus: export the current egress firewall config snippet and a short note on key management (KMS) usage for model artifacts.

We should aim to upload everything to the portal by EOD Friday so Acme can finish their vendor assessment next week. I started a Google Drive folder and put a checklist (link below).

Checklist link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/0AExampleSharedEvidenceLink

Thanks —
Logan

Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference

Upload steps: 
1) Sign in at https://app.onetrust.com/portal/company/ACME/inbound/record/123456 (use the temporary link).
2) Under \"Evidence\" choose \"Upload Files\" and tag them as SOC2 / Network Diagram / Encryption Controls.
3) For KMS notes, a short plain-text file is fine. If anything is sensitive please mark it as \"Restricted\" in the portal and we will keep it scoped to procurement reviewers.

I also attached the Acme evidence template and a helpful example of the minimum network diagram they accept. Let me know if you run into permission issues — I can open a support ticket.

Jordan Michaels

source.subject: Foxline security review: pre-call agenda, handoff, and evidence follow-up
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Patel <ava.patel@foxline.com>, Foxline Security Team <security@foxline.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-09-03T08:14:00-07:00
Subject: Foxline security review: pre-call agenda, handoff, and evidence follow-up

Hi Ava (and team),

Thanks for agreeing to the security review call next week. Quick note to get us aligned before the meeting and to make the session as productive as possible. Attached: precall-agenda.md with proposed agenda and a rough SIG/CAIQ mapping we think will be useful.

Proposed agenda (30–40 min):
- Intros & scope (5m)
- SIG/CAIQ sections you care about (10m)
- Evidence handoff: what we can share now (10m)
- Open Q&A / next steps (5–15m)

Action items for us before the call:
- Share a redwood-network-diagram.pdf that shows isolation boundaries and VPC peering (attached).
- Fill and share the SIG/CAIQ spreadsheet with our answers for the sections we own (see Foxline_SIG_Q1-answers.xlsx).

Please confirm: 30 September, 10:00 AM PT works for you? If not, propose two alternate 30–45 minute slots. Also let me know if you want a deeper dive on KMS/encryption or a separate runbook review.

Thanks — looking forward.

—
Logan Wright
Enterprise Lead, Solutions
Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192
From: Ava Patel <ava.patel@foxline.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Foxline Security Team <security@foxline.com>
Date: 2025-09-03T09:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Foxline security review: pre-call agenda, handoff, and evidence follow-up

Logan — thanks for the clear agenda. 10/30 at 10:00 AM PT works for me. I'll invite Rajiv Menon (engineering) and our InfoSec lead.

Couple requests up front:
- Can you include a short section in the spreadsheet about data residency (which services store transients vs. persisted logs)?
- We typically prefer to review SOC 2 Type II summary in advance if available (redacted OK). Do you have a link or can you share the report?

Also, we'll want to confirm whether PKI/KMS keys are customer-managed or Redwood-managed for dedicated deployments — please indicate that in the SIG answers.

Ava
Ava Patel
Head of IT Security
Foxline Health
ava.patel@foxline.com
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Patel <ava.patel@foxline.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-09-03T11:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Foxline security review: pre-call agenda, handoff, and evidence follow-up

Adding the calendar invite: 2025-09-30 10:00 AM PT (30 min) — invite sent.

Notes for the Foxline team:
- We'll upload the SIG worksheet (Foxline_SIG_Q1-answers.xlsx) to the Drive folder by EOD 9/25 so you have time to review.
- Mina from our security team will own technical evidence (logs, IAM config screenshots, network diagram).
- I'll manage contract/PO and HIPAA paperwork follow-ups if those arise.

If anyone on your side needs a longer slot for a controls walkthrough, we can extend to 60 minutes — let me know.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com
From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Patel <ava.patel@foxline.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2025-09-03T17:50:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Foxline security review: pre-call agenda, handoff, and evidence follow-up

Hi Ava / team — quick prep notes from our side before the 9/30 call: 

1) Evidence owners & what we'll prepare (pre-call uploads):
- Network isolation diagram: redwood-network-diagram.pdf (attached) — shows VPCs, subnets, and peering.
- IAM role screenshots for the inference runtime and console access.
- Sample logs retention summary (we'll provide retention table and S3 bucket policies).
- CAIQ mapping: we'll mark items we can answer (our outputs) vs. items that require your config (customer-managed).

2) PKI/KMS note: for Redwood Hosted we manage keys; for Dedicated/Private we support customer-managed KMS (we'll flag which controls change in the SIG spreadsheet).

3) SOC 2: we can share an executive summary and selected pages relevant to logical access and encryption. Full reports require an NDA/attestation process.

4) Quick ask: can you confirm whether Foxline wants system logs exported to your SIEM (we support S3 pushes and streaming via Kinesis)? That affects retention evidence and proof of ingestion.

I'll upload the initial evidence set to the Drive link Logan shared by 9/25. Let me know if you'd prefer the files in a DocuSign or secure transfer instead.

Thanks,
Mina Haddad
Security Engineer
Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com
From: Ava Patel <ava.patel@foxline.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rajiv Menon <rajiv@foxline.com>
Date: 2025-09-04T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Foxline security review: pre-call agenda, handoff, and evidence follow-up

Thanks Mina — this is helpful. A few clarifications: 
- We'd like system logs streamed to our SIEM; please include a short tech note describing the streaming mechanism and retention lifecycle in the Drive folder.
- SOC 2 exec summary would be great — if you can attach the redacted PDF to the Drive link that would speed review.
- For KMS: confirm that for Dedicated deployments we can bring our own keys (BYOK) and whether key rotation is customer-driven.

Also, sharing the Drive folder with rajiv@foxline.com would be great.

Ava
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Patel <ava.patel@foxline.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rajiv Menon <rajiv@foxline.com>
Date: 2025-09-04T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Foxline security review: pre-call agenda, handoff, and evidence follow-up

Perfect — thanks all for the quick confirmations.

Next steps (recap):
- Mina: upload initial evidence (network diagram, IAM screenshots, retention table) and SOC 2 exec summary to https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1FxExample by 2025-09-25 (assigned).
- Marissa: coordinate any contract/PO questions and confirm extended meeting if needed.
- Foxline: confirm Rajiv + InfoSec lead as attendees and whether you prefer a 30 or 60 minute slot.

I'll also attach the HubSpot record here for reference: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/companies/9876

Please reply with final attendee list and preferred meeting length so I can update the invite.

Thanks — looking forward to the call.

—
Logan Wright
Enterprise Lead, Solutions
Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

source.subject: Pilot capacity — staggered hourly packs vs calendar extension (Orchid AI)
messages:
From: Sophie Lang <sophie@orchid.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 24 Jul 2025 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot: capacity top-up options — Orchid AI

Hi Logan,

Quick note from Orchid — our pilot next week is showing a spike in parallel inference as we move from daytime tests to a continuous ingest (bot-run) for 72 hours. Current allocation will likely exhaust the test window by day two.

We have three asks:
- Can we buy a short-term top-up so we keep consistent latency (priority access) during the window?
- If not, what does a calendar extension look like (add N more days to the trial) vs a prepaid hour bundle?
- Any hybrid approach (small top-up + extra days) you recommend?

Attached: Orchid_Pilot_Specs.pdf with expected token volume and concurrency.

Sophie
Sophie Lang
Head of ML Eng
Orchid AI

P.S. Procurement is ready to engage once we have pricing to review: contracts@orchid.ai
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Lang <sophie@orchid.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 24 Jul 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot: capacity top-up options — Orchid AI

Thanks Sophie — good flag. Two concise options below, plus a hybrid we sometimes use for bursty short pilots. Numbers are illustrative; I can send an updated line-item sheet after we align on preference.

Option A — Staggered Hour Packs (priority access)
- Prepay a block of GPU-hours (e.g., 1,000 hours) at $X/hr (10% off list for pilots).
- Pack is consumed from a priority queue (reduced preemption) so average latency is preserved during spikes.
- Unused hours valid for 30 days post-purchase.
- Example: 1,000 hours covers ~48 hours at your expected concurrency (per attached spec).

Option B — Calendar Extension (duration add-on)
- Add 14 calendar days to the current pilot, prorated at $Y/day (no priority guarantee).
- Simpler billing; lower up-front cost if you expect low average utilization but costlier under heavy load.

Option C — Hybrid Staggered Packs + Extension
- Smaller hour pack (200–400 hrs) + 7-day extension. Smooths cost while preserving burst protection for critical windows.

Next steps I suggest:
1) Tell me target runway (how many extra full test-days you want guaranteed).
2) If priority during spikes is must-have, choose Option A or C; I’ll generate a final quote and add a pilot-conversion credit line.

Attaching a quick price worksheet (redwood_proposal_v1.xlsx). Priya — including you for cache/optimization details (we can likely reduce required hours with prefix caching). Kimberly — FYI on discount approval for this pilot.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>, Sophie Lang <sophie@orchid.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 24 Jul 2025 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot: capacity top-up options — Orchid AI

On Thu, Jul 24, 2025 at 10:05 AM Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Sophie — good flag. Two concise options below...

Adding a quick technical lens from CS: based on Orchid_Pilot_Specs.pdf the single-biggest win is reducing repeated prefix compute — we can often cut effective hours by ~20-30% with agressive KV/prefix caching and a short-lived internal cache shard. If you can tolerate eventual consistency on a small subset of requests, that materially reduces required paid hours.

If Orchid wants strict tail-latency SLAs during the 72-hour window, the hour-pack (priority queue) is safest. For cost transparency, I’m attaching a quick estimate that runs your projected QPS through both: hour-pack vs straight extension (cache_impact_estimate.csv).

Question for Procurement: If Orchid prefers a blended rate (hour-pack discounted + pilot conversion credit on contract signature), we’ll need pricing sign-off from Kimberly. Kimberly — can we do a 8–12% blended concession for this early-stage PoC (deal ORC-2025-042)?

Technical note: For private deployment customers this week we can also offer a small reserved slice (burst-cap of 50 concurrent) but that requires a short engineering ops change (10–12 hours of runway). Let me know if you want me to scope that with devops.

— Priya
Priya Natarajan
Customer Solutions — Redwood Inference
From: Sophie Lang <sophie@orchid.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
Cc: contracts@orchid.ai
Date: Fri, 25 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot: capacity top-up options — Orchid AI

Thanks — that framing helps. Quick answers and ask:
- We want guaranteed tail-latency during the 72-hour run; Option C (hybrid) looks attractive if we can keep the overall budget under $9k.
- Procurement (cc’ed) would prefer a single SOW amendment + a 10% pilot-conversion credit that applies to the first committed quarter should we convert. That makes the decision fast on our side.
- Can you confirm unused hour-rollover period and whether hours are applied before extension days (i.e., precedence)?

If you can send a final quote with blended pricing and a short mutual action plan (milestones + sign-off contacts), we’ll circulate internally and try to sign by EOD Monday. DocuSign if possible — link to SOW will speed things up.

Best,
Sophie
Sophie Lang
Head of ML Eng, Orchid AI

Attachment referenced: Orchid_Pilot_Specs.pdf

source.subject: Discovery recap & request: pick a window for Private-hosted architecture workshop
messages:
From: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@avahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@avahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jul 2025 15:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick discovery follow-up

Thanks for the call earlier — that was really helpful. A couple of clarifying points as we think about next steps: 

- We definitely want a hands-on architecture review for our private deployment (VPC + on-prem connector).
- Security will need to validate network flow and key management; I can pull in our InfoSec lead if helpful.
- Ideal timing: sometime next week, 60–90 minutes.

Can you send a short agenda and a few time options? We are PT-based but can shift people if needed.

Best,
Sarah
CTO, AvaHealth

On Mon, Jul 7, 2025 at 12:05 PM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks again for the time today — quick recap and proposed next step below.
>
> On the call you mentioned:
> - Primary goal: verify network/topology, KMS approach, and a high-level ops runbook for private hosting.
> - Expected attendees: Sarah (CTO), Tom (Infra Eng), security lead (TBD), and 1–2 of your SREs.
> - Success criteria: alignment on required ports/VPN, an actionable checklist for the pilot, and a timeline to start an on-prem PoC.
>
> I’ll follow up with proposed windows and a short prep checklist for your team.
>
> — Logan
> Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
> 
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@avahealth.com>, Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@avahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Jul 2025 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Discovery recap & request: pick a window for Private-hosted architecture workshop

Hi Sarah / Tom —

Appreciate the quick follow-up. Per the call, we recommend a 75–90 minute architecture workshop where we:

1) Walk the proposed VPC + on-prem connector topology (30 minutes)
2) Review security primitives: KMS, audit logs, and access model (20 minutes)
3) Ops runbook & next steps for a pilot (20 minutes)
4) Q&A and action items (10 minutes)

To keep this productive, we also attach a short "Private Deploy Readiness" checklist and a lightweight network template for you to drop in current CIDR / firewall info before the call.

Proposed 90-minute windows (PT):
- Option A: Wed, Jul 9 — 09:00–10:30 PT
- Option B: Thu, Jul 10 — 13:00–14:30 PT
- Option C: Fri, Jul 11 — 11:00–12:30 PT

If you can pick one, we’ll send a calendar invite and include a short pre-call packet for your security reviewer. Also let us know if you prefer a shorter 60-minute session focused only on network/KMS and follow with an ops workshop later.

Attachments: priv_deploy_readiness_checklist.pdf, network_topology_template.xlsx

Thanks — Ben and Marissa are looped on the invite and will own the runbook and pilot timeline items.

Regards,
Logan Wright
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Cell: 415-555-0128

On Tue, Jul 8, 2025 at 07:21 AM Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@avahealth.com> wrote:
> Thanks — that agenda looks good. One ask: can you include a short snippet on where logs and audit events will live (we use Splunk)?
>
> Also please confirm whether KV/secret rotation will be supported via our existing KMS provider (AWS KMS) or if Redwood expects a different flow.
>
> - Sarah
From: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@avahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@avahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Jul 2025 09:01:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discovery recap & request: pick a window for Private-hosted architecture workshop

Logan —

Option B (Thu, Jul 10 13:00–14:30 PT) works best for our infra folks. Please include Sarah and our security lead (I’ll confirm name shortly).

Two quick items for prep:
- We will share our current VPC CIDR and existing peering config — I’ll populate the network_topology_template.xlsx before EOD today.
- For KMS: we prefer to use AWS KMS; if Redwood supports envelope encryption with customer-managed CMKs, that would be ideal. If not, we’ll want to understand the alternative.

Thanks — looking forward to the workshop.

Tom
Sr. Infrastructure Engineer, AvaHealth

On Tue, Jul 8, 2025 at 08:14 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (see proposed windows + checklist attached)
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@avahealth.com>, Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@avahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Jul 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Confirmed: Private-hosting workshop (Thu Jul 10 13:00–14:30 PT) + pre-call notes

Thanks Tom — great, I’ve penciled in Option B and sent a calendar invite: https://calendar.redwood.ai/event?eid=abc123

Pre-call asks (short):
- Please complete the attached network_topology_template.xlsx with CIDRs, peering, and any NAT/gateway details.
- If available, add a one-line contact for your security reviewer (name + email).
- Confirm whether you want a dedicated segment for logs (Splunk) or prefer an agent-based push — we’ll prepare both patterns.

Agenda will follow in a separate doc (we’ll include specific slide references and a one-page runbook draft). Ben will cover ops/runbook and Marissa will cover pilot timeline and support SLAs.

If anything changes, ping me directly. Looking forward to it.

— Logan
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: priv_deploy_readiness_checklist.pdf, network_topology_template.xlsx

source.subject: Mutual action plan: success signature lanes & evaluation playbook for Clearline
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire@clearlinehealth.com>, Theo Ramirez <theo@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Mutual action plan: success signature lanes & evaluation playbook for Clearline

Claire / Theo —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. As promised, I've sketched a compact MAP that ties the pilot timeline to discrete "success signature" lanes and explicit stop conditions. The goal is to make acceptance binary for the pilot handshake so your internal committee can sign off without ambiguity.

Attached: Clearline_MAP_v1.xlsx (high-level lanes), sample-acceptance-tests.md (concrete tests), and the security Q&A you requested.

High-level summary (please confirm):
- Week 0 (kickoff): data ingest + synthetic smoke tests (dev env)
- Week 1–2 (integration): API routing + latency smoke; 1K test tokens/day, 95th p95 latency < 250ms on key route
- Week 3 (quality validation): accuracy checks vs. 200 labeled prompts; target F1 >= 0.78 on primary intents OR manual review rate <= 10%
- Week 4 (go/no-go): production slice run with 10K tokens; exit gates described below

Exit/stop conditions (if any of these trigger, we pause and remediate):
- Data connectivity errors > 3 consecutive runs
- Latency p95 > 400ms for three measurement windows
- Regression: primary-intent F1 drops > 5% vs baseline
- Security redline: inability to confirm encryption-at-rest for the pilot dataset

Decisions we need from you this week:
1) Confirm the evaluation prompt set (attach or give reviewer names).
2) Approve the 4-week cadence or propose tightening.
3) Who will be the formal acceptance signer on your side? (name + email)

If that all looks good I’ll lock the MAP in the Drive, spin up the sandbox, and share a HubSpot milestone with the runbook and who-owns-what.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
logan@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0124

From: Claire Huang <claire@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Cc: Theo Ramirez <theo@clearlinehealth.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Jul 2025 11:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Mutual action plan: success signature lanes & evaluation playbook for Clearline

Thanks Logan — this is helpful and much clearer than our internal drafts. A few quick notes from our side: 

- Acceptance signer will be me (Claire Huang) unless a compliance question requires C-level review.
- We can provide the labeled prompt set by Monday (Jul 7). Will be ~250 prompts covering clinical instructions and payer queries.
- For latency: can you confirm whether the p95 target is measured from our east-coast region? We need regional numbers.
- Security: our security team flagged the encryption-at-rest statement; they need the KMS vendor and whether keys are customer-controlled. Can you cc in Redwood security or provide the artifact?

Also, curious if you can add a short regression analysis plan to the MAP: how will we surface drifts during the pilot?

Claire
VP Product, Clearline Health
claire@clearlinehealth.com
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire@clearlinehealth.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Theo Ramirez <theo@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Jul 2025 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Mutual action plan: success signature lanes & evaluation playbook for Clearline

Claire — quick answers inline. Adding Monica to coordinate security and our regression plan.

- Latency region: we'll measure p95 from your primary region (us-east-1) and include cross-region samples. We'll annotate the runbook with region-specific baselines.
- KMS: Redwood supports customer-managed KMS in Private and Dedicated modes. For Hosted pilot, we can provide encryption-at-rest with AWS KMS (Redwood-managed) or bring-your-own key (BYOK) via a partner integration. Monica will follow up with the required artifact (KMS vendor + controls).
- Regression: we'll run daily concordance checks against the labeled set and maintain a rolling 7-day delta; if the primary-intent F1 drops by >3% during the run, an automated alert (PagerDuty) will trigger and we'll quarantine the sample set for triage. We'll add a small script that snapshots scoring and stores it in Drive.

Action items from Redwood:
- Karthik: add region-labeled latency page to the MAP (ETA Fri 4pm PT)
- Monica: security artifact (ETA EOD Fri)
- Logan: finalize HubSpot milestone and schedule the kickoff (ETA Tue morning)

Karthik
Head of Product, Redwood Inference
From: Claire Huang <claire@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Jul 2025 15:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Mutual action plan: success signature lanes & evaluation playbook for Clearline

Appreciate the fast turnaround — the plan looks actionable. Two confirmations:

1) We'll upload the labeled prompts to the Drive folder you shared by Monday 07/07.
2) I'll be the acceptance signer for the pilot unless a compliance item forces escalation.

Please add the following to the MAP before kickoff:
- A tiny runbook section titled "graduation evidence" listing the exact queries and expected outcomes (we'll reference this during sign-off).
- A single-line rollback play: if regression >5% during the go/no-go run, pause production cutover and revert to control routing.

Once those are in, consider this tentatively approved and we'll confirm after the first integration smoke test.

Claire
VP Product, Clearline Health

source.subject: Technical handoff & sync window — next steps
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bennet <alex.bennet@atlasgen.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jun 2025 14:12:00 -0400
Subject: Technical handoff & sync window — next steps

Hi Alex —

Great to meet earlier — thanks for walking through AtlasGen's inference needs and the production constraints you flagged (SLA 95th pct latency, on-prem model isolation, and regulatory retention requirements). Quick recap and a proposed next step:

Recap (short):
- Primary goal: move from prototype -> production serving for the internal recommendation model, prioritizing low-latency 1-2s p95 for short sequences.
- Key blockers: infra capacity sizing, KV cache behavior for long-tail prompts, and network egress constraints in your VPC.

Next step: schedule a 60–90 minute technical handoff / deep-dive with our ML infra engineers + your infra/ML leads. That session is intended to:
- Walk through your current model stack (weights, tokenizer, sequence lengths)
- Run through serving runtime tradeoffs (batching, quantization, caching) specific to your workload
- Identify any network/KMS/retention constraints and agree on a rollout plan

Would you be able to share 3 windows that work for your team next week? To make scheduling easier, options we have available are:
- Wed 18 Jun: 14:00–16:00 ET
- Thu 19 Jun: 15:00–17:00 ET
- Fri 20 Jun: 10:00–12:00 ET

Please confirm:
1) Which slot you prefer (we can do 60 or 90 minutes)

source.subject: Private Offer request: multi-sku tiering & partner margin for CortexAI pilot
messages:
From: Timothy Lopez <tim.lopez@cortexai.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
Cc: CortexAI Procurement <procurement@cortexai.com>
Date: Sat, 7 Jun 2025 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot

Hi Logan,

We're about to kick off a 3-month pilot (internal infra + dev seats) and would like to use Redwood in the loop. Can you share:
- A proposed private-offer price for a multi-sku bundle (inference node hours + reserved throughput) for a 90-day pilot
- Any partner/reseller margin expectations if we route through our systems integrator (NorthStar Partners)
- Typical procurement lead time and PO flow (we sometimes need DocuSign + PO)

We're treating this as a small paid pilot that could expand to a longer reserved capacity commitment if results look good.

Thanks,
Tim
Timothy Lopez
Head of ML Ops | CortexAI
---
Attachment: CortexAI_RFP_v1.pdf (pdf stub)

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Timothy Lopez <tim.lopez@cortexai.com>
Cc: CortexAI Procurement <procurement@cortexai.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Jun 2025 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot

Tim — thanks for the note and excited to help. Quick answers up front, and attaching a starter pricing matrix: RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx.

High level:
- We can do a timeboxed 90-day private offer that combines a small reserved pool (for predictable latency) + metered overage billing for bursts.
- For reseller routing (NorthStar Partners) we typically do a pass-through commission or a carved-out reseller margin on the private offer. We can support either: 10% reseller pass-through or a $/node-hour rebate model.
- Procurement: typical timeline is 1–2 weeks for PO + standard SOW/PO signature; if you need faster, we can send a pre-signed DocuSign and start immediately upon countersign.

A couple asks so we can make the quote tight:
1) Approx weekly peak concurrent requests / node-hour estimate for pilot (or % of traffic you expect to route through Redwood).
2) Whether NorthStar will be billing you directly (they'd expect a reseller margin) or you want Redwood to issue invoice and pass-back to NorthStar.

Next step: if you can share preferred routing and a target committed node-hour for the 90 days I’ll build a private-offer draft (PO + DocuSign) and loop in finance.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx (excel stub)

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Jun 2025 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot — need channel margin guidance

Team — forwarding CortexAI's ask. They want a short 90-day paid pilot with potential to convert. Key constraints: NorthStar Partners (SI) may sit in the middle. Options we can offer:
- Option A: Reseller pass-through (we give NorthStar 10% off list on the private offer; Cortex pays NorthStar)
- Option B: Redwood invoices Cortex; we provide a rebate to NorthStar post-invoice (flat $/node-hour or tiered rebate)

Questions:
- Margin: can we standardize on 10% pass-through for pilots under $75k? If not, what's the minimum acceptable?
- Compliance: any additional terms needed if NorthStar requires evidence of SOC2/audit logs? (Cortex likely will ask)

I’ve attached the starter matrix. I want to propose a single private offer with three tiers (pilot, scale, committed) and mark a channel pass-through line for NorthStar. Marcus — finance will need a recommended net price band and approved partner margin. Ben — any ops constraints for short reserved pools and immediate start?

Thanks,
Logan

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Jun 2025 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot — need channel margin guidance

Logan — quick guidance from finance/partner ops:
- Standard pilot reseller pass-through: 10% on list for pilots <$75k. That aligns with previous small pilots (see deal RDW-2024-2981).
- If Cortex prefers Redwood to invoice, offer a tiered rebate to NorthStar: $0.05 / node-hour for first 5k hours, $0.03 thereafter for the 90d pilot (keeps incentives aligned).
- For conversion to committed capacity (if they move to Reserved 6–12 month), we can offer a backfill credit that effectively increases partner take to ~12% NRR on net new committed ARR. Finance can approve this conditional on minimum 6-month commit.
- Compliance: SOC2 evidence + audit-log export can be included in the partner pack; we usually provide the SOC2 letter + a scoped evidence bundle when requested (no additional charge).

Ben — ops: confirm we can spin up a small reserved pool (2–4 p3-equivalents) on short notice and allow burst to hosted for metered traffic. Also confirm KV cache warm-up expectations (they likely will need low latency).

If ops greenlights, I recommend we propose Option A (10% pass-through) as the baseline and Option B (rebate) as fallback. That keeps invoicing simple for Cortex and keeps NorthStar happy.

— Marcus

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Timothy Lopez <tim.lopez@cortexai.com>
Cc: CortexAI Procurement <procurement@cortexai.com>, NorthStar Partners <partner@nstar.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Jun 2025 14:23:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private Offer request: multi-sku tiering & partner margin for CortexAI pilot

Tim — thanks for the details. Per our internal review, here is a concise proposed structure (attached: CortexAI_PO_Draft.pdf):

Proposed private offer (90-day pilot):
- Reserved pool: 3 x p3-equivalent nodes reserved for guaranteed latency (billed at private-offer net price)
- Metered burst: metered node-hours at a tiered overage rate
- Pricing: see attached RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx (summary below)
   * Pilot net (after 10% NorthStar pass-through): $X.XX / reserved-node-hour equivalent (line-item in sheet)
   * Overage: $Y.YY / node-hour (metered)
- Channel flow: NorthStar Partners will receive a 10% pass-through on the private-offer list price. CortexAI can opt to have NorthStar bill you directly (recommended to simplify invoicing).
- Compliance & evidence: we will attach SOC2 type II letter and scoped audit artifacts to the partner packet.
- Procurement: if you can confirm routing (NorthStar invoice vs. Redwood invoice) I’ll send a DocuSign-ready PO + SOW. Typical sign timeline is < 7 business days if no custom legal redlines.

Next steps:
1) Confirm if NorthStar will invoice CortexAI (recommended) or whether you prefer Redwood to invoice and handle pass-back.
2) Approve the attached PO draft or suggest edits.
3) Share estimated weekly peak so we can finalize the reserved sizing in the PO.

If this looks good I’ll loop in finance and legal and get DocuSign out by end of the week.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
---
Attachments referenced: RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx, CortexAI_PO_Draft.pdf

Subject: Re: Private Offer request: multi-sku tiering & partner margin for CortexAI pilot

Tim — thanks for the details. Per our internal review, here is a concise proposed structure (attached: CortexAI_PO_Draft.pdf):

Proposed private offer (90-day pilot):
- Reserved pool: 3 x p3-equivalent nodes reserved for guaranteed latency (billed at private-offer net price)
- Metered burst: metered node-hours at a tiered overage rate
- Pricing: see attached RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx (summary below)
   * Pilot net (after 10% NorthStar pass-through): $X.XX / reserved-node-hour equivalent (line-item in sheet)
   * Overage: $Y.YY / node-hour (metered)
- Channel flow: NorthStar Partners will receive a 10% pass-through on the private-offer list price. CortexAI can opt to have NorthStar bill you directly (recommended to simplify invoicing).
- Compliance & evidence: we will attach SOC2 type II letter and scoped audit artifacts to the partner packet.
- Procurement: if you can confirm routing (NorthStar invoice vs. Redwood invoice) I’ll send a DocuSign-ready PO + SOW. Typical sign timeline is < 7 business days if no custom legal redlines.

Next steps:
1) Confirm if NorthStar will invoice CortexAI (recommended) or whether you prefer Redwood to invoice and handle pass-back.
2) Approve the attached PO draft or suggest edits.
3) Share estimated weekly peak so we can finalize the reserved sizing in the PO.

If this looks good I’ll loop in finance and legal and get DocuSign out by end of the week.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
---
Attachments referenced: RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx, CortexAI_PO_Draft.pdf
text:
From: Timothy Lopez <tim.lopez@cortexai.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
Cc: CortexAI Procurement <procurement@cortexai.com>
Date: Sat, 7 Jun 2025 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot

Hi Logan,

We're about to kick off a 3-month pilot (internal infra + dev seats) and would like to use Redwood in the loop. Can you share:
- A proposed private-offer price for a multi-sku bundle (inference node hours + reserved throughput) for a 90-day pilot
- Any partner/reseller margin expectations if we route through our systems integrator (NorthStar Partners)
- Typical procurement lead time and PO flow (we sometimes need DocuSign + PO)

We're treating this as a small paid pilot that could expand to a longer reserved capacity commitment if results look good.

Thanks,
Tim
Timothy Lopez
Head of ML Ops | CortexAI
---
Attachment: CortexAI_RFP_v1.pdf (pdf stub)

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Timothy Lopez <tim.lopez@cortexai.com>
Cc: CortexAI Procurement <procurement@cortexai.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Jun 2025 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot

Tim — thanks for the note and excited to help. Quick answers up front, and attaching a starter pricing matrix: RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx.

High level:
- We can do a timeboxed 90-day private offer that combines a small reserved pool (for predictable latency) + metered overage billing for bursts.
- For reseller routing (NorthStar Partners) we typically do a pass-through commission or a carved-out reseller margin on the private offer. We can support either: 10% reseller pass-through or a $/node-hour rebate model.
- Procurement: typical timeline is 1–2 weeks for PO + standard SOW/PO signature; if you need faster, we can send a pre-signed DocuSign and start immediately upon countersign.

A couple asks so we can make the quote tight:
1) Approx weekly peak concurrent requests / node-hour estimate for pilot (or % of traffic you expect to route through Redwood).
2) Whether NorthStar will be billing you directly (they'd expect a reseller margin) or you want Redwood to issue invoice and pass-back to NorthStar.

Next step: if you can share preferred routing and a target committed node-hour for the 90 days I’ll build a private-offer draft (PO + DocuSign) and loop in finance.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: RDW_pricing_matrix_CortexAI.xlsx (excel stub)

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Jun 2025 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot — need channel margin guidance

Team — forwarding CortexAI's ask. They want a short 90-day paid pilot with potential to convert. Key constraints: NorthStar Partners (SI) may sit in the middle. Options we can offer:
- Option A: Reseller pass-through (we give NorthStar 10% off list on the private offer; Cortex pays NorthStar)
- Option B: Redwood invoices Cortex; we provide a rebate to NorthStar post-invoice (flat $/node-hour or tiered rebate)

Questions:
- Margin: can we standardize on 10% pass-through for pilots under $75k? If not, what's the minimum acceptable?
- Compliance: any additional terms needed if NorthStar requires evidence of SOC2/audit logs? (Cortex likely will ask)

I’ve attached the starter matrix. I want to propose a single private offer with three tiers (pilot, scale, committed) and mark a channel pass-through line for NorthStar. Marcus — finance will need a recommended net price band and approved partner margin. Ben — any ops constraints for short reserved pools and immediate start?

Thanks,
Logan

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Jun 2025 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Private Offer inquiry for CortexAI pilot — need channel margin guidance

Logan — quick guidance from finance/partner ops:
- Standard pilot reseller pass-through: 10% on list for pilots <$75k. That aligns with previous small pilots (see deal RDW-2024-2981).
- If Cortex prefers Redwood to invoice, offer a tiered rebate to NorthStar: $0.05 / node-hour for first 5k hours, $0.03 thereafter for the 90d pilot (keeps incentives aligned).
- For conversion to committed capacity (if they move to Reserved 6–12 month), we can offer a backfill credit that effectively increases partner take to ~12% NRR on net new committed ARR. Finance can approve this conditional on minimum 6-month commit.
- Compliance: SOC2 evidence + audit-log export can be included in the partner pack; we usually provide the SOC2 letter + a scoped evidence bundle when requested (no additional charge).

Ben — ops: confirm we can spin up a small reserved pool (2–4 p3-equivalents) on short notice and allow burst to hosted for metered traffic. Also confirm KV cache warm-up expectations (they likely will need low latency).

If ops greenlights, I recommend we propose Option A (10% pass-through) as the baseline and Option B (rebate) as fallback. That keeps invoicing simple for Cortex and keeps NorthStar happy.

— Marcus

source.subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access
source.body:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>
Cc: QuantaLedger Legal <legal@quantaledger.example>
Date: Sun, Jun 1, 2025 at 8:22 AM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Maya  confirming we can keep serving/storage in the US region for the Private deployment.

On your question about the EU-based rollout subcontractor: we can approve a *time-bounded exception* that allows that individual to access deployment logs and operational metadata during rollout. This does *not* include access to prompts/responses or any payload-level content. Access will be least-privilege and fully audited.

If you confirm the identity + SSO details, well document the exception and proceed.

Logan


From: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: QuantaLedger Legal <legal@quantaledger.example>
Date: Mon, Jun 2, 2025 at 9:10 AM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Thanks. Please clarify in writing: scope (logs/metadata only), duration, and that the contractor cannot access prompts/responses.


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: QuantaLedger Legal <legal@quantaledger.example>
Cc: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 11:52 AM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Confirmed. The exception covers out-of-region human access to operational metadata only (deployment logs, request ids/timestamps, admin audit logs) for 2025-06-05 through 2025-07-31. Prompts/responses are excluded. Access is via least-privilege role mapping and audited.

Aisha Rahman
Head of Security, Redwood Inference


From: Maya Ellis <maya.ellis@quantaledger.example>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 4, 2025 at 12:10 PM
Subject: Re: QuantaLedger - US data residency + EU rollout support access

Accepted. Please proceed with the RBAC setup and send us the final exception record reference.

source.subject: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)
source.body:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)
Attachments: PLG-to-Sales-Signal-Routing_Kickoff-Notes_2025-05-01.docx; Signal-taxonomy-and-thresholds_working-notes_v0.3.xlsx

Team —

Kicking off the “PLG-to-sales signal routing + playbook” project. We’re seeing more expansions initiate from self-serve usage, but we’re too inconsistent about catching the right moments (or we catch too many and spam).

Outcome we want: a privacy-aware, reliable signal pipeline that routes *sales-worthy* signals into HubSpot with the right owner + the right next step, and a playbook so AEs/SEs/CSMs follow up consistently.

Phase 0 (next 1–2 weeks): align on signal definitions + success metrics + privacy guardrails.

Goals (MVP):
- Define canonical signals: threshold (scale/reliability) + intent (Dedicated/Private/security).
- Daily/weekly window aggregation + dedupe + scoring into a “PLG Signal” object.
- Route qualified signals into HubSpot as company timeline notes + tasks.
- Deterministic ownership rules: preserve existing owner; else region/tier mapping; else round-robin.
- Pilot NA team with a limited cohort.
- Instrument outcomes: contacted → meeting → eval started → opp created (Dedicated/Private).

Non-goals for initial phase:
- No fully automated outbound without human review for early cohorts.
- Not building an in-house sequencing tool (start with HubSpot tasks/templates).

Asks (owners + dates):
1) Signal definitions + cut lines (Product/Growth/Sales):
   - Owner: Nikhil + Ben + Stephanie
   - Due: Fri 5/9
   - Output: “v1 signal taxonomy + thresholds” with expected weekly volume (and suppression rules).

2) Data pipeline MVP (Eng):
   - Owner: Logan + Priya
   - Due: MVP plan by Wed 5/7; implementation estimate after schema is final
   - Requirements: idempotent writes, dedupe keys, backfills, and audit logs.

3) HubSpot object + routing workflows (BizOps/SalesOps/MarketingOps):
   - Owner: David + Isabelle + Tyler
   - Due: initial spec by Fri 5/9

4) Privacy + data handling (Security/Legal):
   - Owner: Aisha
   - Due: guardrails + review checklist by Fri 5/9

5) Playbook + cadence (Sales/SE/CS):
   - Owner: Stephanie + Markus + Jasmine (Ben for PLG messaging)
   - Due: draft by Fri 5/16 (so it’s ready for pilot)

Target pilot start: week of Mon 5/19.

Please reply-all with (a) confirmation you can own the above, (b) any blockers, and (c) “what would make this fail” risks we should address in Phase 0.

Thanks,
Jordan

Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference


---

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed on Sales owner for NA pilot. Avery (pilot AE rep) and I can commit time for weekly quality review.

Two items I’d like us to nail early:
1) Noise controls — AEs will ignore this if we generate “busywork tasks.” We should set an explicit cap per AE per week for the pilot (even if we miss some).
2) What counts as “reliability signal” — repeated 429s and sustained latency spikes should probably be treated differently. For 429s, we need to know if they’re self-inflicted (bad retry loops) vs true capacity limits.

Nikhil/Ben: can we add a “confidence” + “why this triggered” snippet directly on the HubSpot task?

-Stephanie

Stephanie Nguyen
VP Sales, North America
Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 10:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

+1 to suppression/noise control.

Marketing ask: we need explicit rules for when an AE task suppresses (or pauses) lifecycle emails, so we don’t double-touch and confuse customers.

Proposal for pilot:
- If an account is routed to AE task: pause “Dedicated/Private nurture” for 14 days.
- If Marketing has already sent a nurture touch in the last 3 business days: delay AE task creation by 2 days (unless it’s a compliance/security intent signal).

Tyler/I can own the workflow side if David confirms what fields / lists we’ll have available.

Camila

Camila Reyes
VP Marketing
Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed from Eng sponsorship side.

A couple constraints to factor into Phase 0:
- We can reliably compute daily aggregates (usage/spend/error rates) from the analytics dataset with ~12–24h latency. Anything “real-time” will require additional infra and oncall.
- HubSpot API rate limits will become a problem if we do per-event writes. We should route only aggregated “qualified signals” and batch updates.

Logan and I will draft the MVP plan by Wed 5/7 as requested. Key design points we’ll cover:
- idempotency + dedupe key (account_id + signal_type + window_start)
- backfill job + replays for late telemetry
- audit log for what was sent to HubSpot (payload hash + timestamp)

Priya

Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed: David + Isabelle + Tyler will own HubSpot setup.

For the spec by 5/9, we’ll propose:
- Company properties:
  - last_plg_signal_type
  - last_plg_signal_at
  - plg_signal_score (0–100)
  - plg_signal_summary (short text, e.g., “7d spend +62%, 11x 429s, viewed Dedicated docs”)
  - plg_signal_suppressed_until
  - plg_signal_owner_rule (existing_owner/region/round_robin)
- Timeline note template: standardized “Why this triggered” block.
- Task template(s): “PLG signal follow-up (pilot)” with fields for persona + recommended next step.

One question for Product/Eng: do we want a custom object (“PLG Signal”) or store as properties + timeline notes? Custom object is cleaner but adds complexity + permission surface area. For pilot, I’d prefer properties + timeline note + task.

David

David Kim
Business Systems (HubSpot)
Redwood Inference


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 1:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed on security/privacy guardrails.

Baseline requirements for Phase 0 approval:
- PII minimization: no end-user content, prompts, completions, or raw request payloads in HubSpot. No IP addresses.
- Aggregate-only metrics are OK (token volume, spend, error counts, doc page view categories), but keep “doc view” signals to coarse categories (e.g., “Security FAQ viewed” vs exact URL query params).
- Retention: define retention in HubSpot for signal fields and timeline notes (and how to support deletion requests).
- Access control: only GTM roles should see signal fields; we should avoid exposing to broad portal users.

Also: any “security intent signal” needs careful messaging. The playbook should avoid implying we’re monitoring anything beyond product usage + public docs interactions.

I’ll send a checklist by 5/9; looping in Marcus Lin for security engineering review if we end up doing custom objects.

-Aisha

Dr. Aisha Rahman
Security & Privacy
Redwood Inference


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, May 2, 2025 at 8:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Thanks all — quick recap and decisions so we can move.

Decisions (pilot):
- We will start with *aggregated qualified signals only* (daily/weekly windows) and avoid per-event HubSpot writes.
- For HubSpot, we’ll do properties + timeline note + task for pilot (no custom object). Revisit custom object after we validate signal volume + usefulness.
- Noise controls are a requirement, not a “nice to have.” We will set a pilot cap per AE/week and suppression windows (Sales + Marketing).

Owner confirmations:
- Signal definitions + thresholds: Nikhil / Ben / Stephanie (due 5/9)
- Eng MVP plan: Priya / Logan (plan due 5/7)
- HubSpot spec: David / Isabelle / Tyler (due 5/9)
- Privacy guardrails: Aisha (due 5/9)
- Playbook draft: Stephanie / Markus / Jasmine / Ben (due 5/16)

Open questions to close in Phase 0:
1) Confidence + “why this triggered” copy — agree on a standard snippet AEs can trust.
2) 429 interpretation — distinguish customer misconfiguration vs platform capacity; route differently.
3) Marketing suppression rules — finalize the exact timing + exceptions (Camila’s proposal is a good starting point).

Next meeting: 30 min working session Tue 5/6 @ 11:00am PT (calendar invite coming) to finalize the v1 list of signals and expected weekly volumes.

Jordan

Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference

title: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)
Attachments: PLG-to-Sales-Signal-Routing_Kickoff-Notes_2025-05-01.docx; Signal-taxonomy-and-thresholds_working-notes_v0.3.xlsx

Team —

Kicking off the “PLG-to-sales signal routing + playbook” project. We’re seeing more expansions initiate from self-serve usage, but we’re too inconsistent about catching the right moments (or we catch too many and spam).

Outcome we want: a privacy-aware, reliable signal pipeline that routes *sales-worthy* signals into HubSpot with the right owner + the right next step, and a playbook so AEs/SEs/CSMs follow up consistently.

Phase 0 (next 1–2 weeks): align on signal definitions + success metrics + privacy guardrails.

Goals (MVP):
- Define canonical signals: threshold (scale/reliability) + intent (Dedicated/Private/security).
- Daily/weekly window aggregation + dedupe + scoring into a “PLG Signal” object.
- Route qualified signals into HubSpot as company timeline notes + tasks.
- Deterministic ownership rules: preserve existing owner; else region/tier mapping; else round-robin.
- Pilot NA team with a limited cohort.
- Instrument outcomes: contacted → meeting → eval started → opp created (Dedicated/Private).

Non-goals for initial phase:
- No fully automated outbound without human review for early cohorts.
- Not building an in-house sequencing tool (start with HubSpot tasks/templates).

Asks (owners + dates):
1) Signal definitions + cut lines (Product/Growth/Sales):
   - Owner: Nikhil + Ben + Stephanie
   - Due: Fri 5/9
   - Output: “v1 signal taxonomy + thresholds” with expected weekly volume (and suppression rules).

2) Data pipeline MVP (Eng):
   - Owner: Logan + Priya
   - Due: MVP plan by Wed 5/7; implementation estimate after schema is final
   - Requirements: idempotent writes, dedupe keys, backfills, and audit logs.

3) HubSpot object + routing workflows (BizOps/SalesOps/MarketingOps):
   - Owner: David + Isabelle + Tyler
   - Due: initial spec by Fri 5/9

4) Privacy + data handling (Security/Legal):
   - Owner: Aisha
   - Due: guardrails + review checklist by Fri 5/9

5) Playbook + cadence (Sales/SE/CS):
   - Owner: Stephanie + Markus + Jasmine (Ben for PLG messaging)
   - Due: draft by Fri 5/16 (so it’s ready for pilot)

Target pilot start: week of Mon 5/19.

Please reply-all with (a) confirmation you can own the above, (b) any blockers, and (c) “what would make this fail” risks we should address in Phase 0.

Thanks,
Jordan

Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed on Sales owner for NA pilot. Avery (pilot AE rep) and I can commit time for weekly quality review.

Two items I’d like us to nail early:
1) Noise controls — AEs will ignore this if we generate “busywork tasks.” We should set an explicit cap per AE per week for the pilot (even if we miss some).
2) What counts as “reliability signal” — repeated 429s and sustained latency spikes should probably be treated differently. For 429s, we need to know if they’re self-inflicted (bad retry loops) vs true capacity limits.

Nikhil/Ben: can we add a “confidence” + “why this triggered” snippet directly on the HubSpot task?

-Stephanie

Stephanie Nguyen
VP Sales, North America
Redwood Inference

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 10:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

+1 to suppression/noise control.

Marketing ask: we need explicit rules for when an AE task suppresses (or pauses) lifecycle emails, so we don’t double-touch and confuse customers.

Proposal for pilot:
- If an account is routed to AE task: pause “Dedicated/Private nurture” for 14 days.
- If Marketing has already sent a nurture touch in the last 3 business days: delay AE task creation by 2 days (unless it’s a compliance/security intent signal).

Tyler/I can own the workflow side if David confirms what fields / lists we’ll have available.

Camila

Camila Reyes
VP Marketing
Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed from Eng sponsorship side.

A couple constraints to factor into Phase 0:
- We can reliably compute daily aggregates (usage/spend/error rates) from the analytics dataset with ~12–24h latency. Anything “real-time” will require additional infra and oncall.
- HubSpot API rate limits will become a problem if we do per-event writes. We should route only aggregated “qualified signals” and batch updates.

Logan and I will draft the MVP plan by Wed 5/7 as requested. Key design points we’ll cover:
- idempotency + dedupe key (account_id + signal_type + window_start)
- backfill job + replays for late telemetry
- audit log for what was sent to HubSpot (payload hash + timestamp)

Priya

Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed: David + Isabelle + Tyler will own HubSpot setup.

For the spec by 5/9, we’ll propose:
- Company properties:
  - last_plg_signal_type
  - last_plg_signal_at
  - plg_signal_score (0–100)
  - plg_signal_summary (short text, e.g., “7d spend +62%, 11x 429s, viewed Dedicated docs”)
  - plg_signal_suppressed_until
  - plg_signal_owner_rule (existing_owner/region/round_robin)
- Timeline note template: standardized “Why this triggered” block.
- Task template(s): “PLG signal follow-up (pilot)” with fields for persona + recommended next step.

One question for Product/Eng: do we want a custom object (“PLG Signal”) or store as properties + timeline notes? Custom object is cleaner but adds complexity + permission surface area. For pilot, I’d prefer properties + timeline note + task.

David

David Kim
Business Systems (HubSpot)
Redwood Inference

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, May 1, 2025 at 1:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Confirmed on security/privacy guardrails.

Baseline requirements for Phase 0 approval:
- PII minimization: no end-user content, prompts, completions, or raw request payloads in HubSpot. No IP addresses.
- Aggregate-only metrics are OK (token volume, spend, error counts, doc page view categories), but keep “doc view” signals to coarse categories (e.g., “Security FAQ viewed” vs exact URL query params).
- Retention: define retention in HubSpot for signal fields and timeline notes (and how to support deletion requests).
- Access control: only GTM roles should see signal fields; we should avoid exposing to broad portal users.

Also: any “security intent signal” needs careful messaging. The playbook should avoid implying we’re monitoring anything beyond product usage + public docs interactions.

I’ll send a checklist by 5/9; looping in Marcus Lin for security engineering review if we end up doing custom objects.

-Aisha

Dr. Aisha Rahman
Security & Privacy
Redwood Inference
