From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, May 2, 2025 at 8:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: PLG → Sales signal routing kickoff (Phase 0)

Thanks all — quick recap and decisions so we can move.

Decisions (pilot):
- We will start with *aggregated qualified signals only* (daily/weekly windows) and avoid per-event HubSpot writes.
- For HubSpot, we’ll do properties + timeline note + task for pilot (no custom object). Revisit custom object after we validate signal volume + usefulness.
- Noise controls are a requirement, not a “nice to have.” We will set a pilot cap per AE/week and suppression windows (Sales + Marketing).

Owner confirmations:
- Signal definitions + thresholds: Nikhil / Ben / Stephanie (due 5/9)
- Eng MVP plan: Priya / Logan (plan due 5/7)
- HubSpot spec: David / Isabelle / Tyler (due 5/9)
- Privacy guardrails: Aisha (due 5/9)
- Playbook draft: Stephanie / Markus / Jasmine / Ben (due 5/16)

Open questions to close in Phase 0:
1) Confidence + “why this triggered” copy — agree on a standard snippet AEs can trust.
2) 429 interpretation — distinguish customer misconfiguration vs platform capacity; route differently.
3) Marketing suppression rules — finalize the exact timing + exceptions (Camila’s proposal is a good starting point).

Next meeting: 30 min working session Tue 5/6 @ 11:00am PT (calendar invite coming) to finalize the v1 list of signals and expected weekly volumes.

Jordan

Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)
source.body:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 9:12 AM PDT
Subject: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Team — pulling forward a cross-functional readout because it’s now showing up in Console beta feedback + SUP-24910 (“results not updating / numbers don’t match”). This is specifically about experiment attribution completeness and the rollup migration for the results dashboard.

TL;DR
- We have measurable data quality gaps in attribution (experiment_id/variant_id/route_id normalization) that can skew deltas, especially for streaming + retries.
- We can fix forward quickly (most is already in flight), but we need an explicit backfill plan + comms/UX guardrail for “partial data” during the migration window.

Current issues (measured in staging + early beta)
1) Attribution drop rate (missing experiment_id and/or variant_id)
   - Ranges 3–6% for non-streaming.
   - Spikes to 8–12% on streaming paths with retries.
   - Primary cause: context loss across retry boundary for stream resume; secondary: older SDKs not passing stickiness key leads to “default bucket” behavior.

2) route_id inconsistencies between router and analytics rollups
   - Router emits normalized template route_id, but some spans still carry raw path (high-cardinality). Our mitigation (dimension limiting/sampling) means those can get dropped in aggregation.
   - Outcome: a subset of traffic lands in “unknown route” bucket and doesn’t show up in Console route drilldown.

3) Rollup schema migration gap
   - Dashboard expects rollup_v2 keyed by {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region}.
   - We have partial rollup_v2 coverage starting Mar 18; earlier data is still rollup_v1 (no experiment dims).

Artifacts
- Grafana (guardrails): https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/route-exp-guardrails/route-experiments-guardrails
- Grafana (data quality): https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/route-exp-data-quality/route-experiments-data-quality
- Looker DQ view (rollup_v2): https://looker.redwoodinference.com/looks/8921-experiment-results-rollup-v2-dq
- Attachment: experiment-attribution-drop-rate-2025-03-26.csv

Fix forward (already in flight)
- PR 28622 (streaming retries attribution): https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/28622
- PR 28741 (reduce metrics cardinality; keep required dims in rollups): https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/28741

Proposal: backfill + migration plan (needs sign-off)
A) Ship fixes forward (Mar 28–Apr 1)
   - Merge 28622 to prod behind route_experiments flag first, then broader.
   - Ensure router emits *only* normalized route_id to attribution pipeline for experiment traffic.

B) Backfill rollup_v2 (limited window)
   - Backfill from request log / billing events where we have stable experiment_id + variant_id (not raw traces).
   - Window: last 14 days at GA (initially), then extend to 30 days if costs are acceptable.
   - Ownership: Telemetry/Analytics to implement (ENG-9034), with SRE review for load.

C) Console UX/Comms
   - Add banner/tooltips in results dashboard when attribution drop rate > threshold or during backfill window: “Results are partial; attribution coverage X%.”
   - This helps us avoid “numbers don’t match” escalations while we repair.

Asks (today)
1) Dylan/Marissa: OK to add “partial data” banner + temporarily suppress % deltas when coverage < 95%? (We can still show raw metrics with a warning.)
2) Sean/Priya: Can we proceed with backfill job for 14d window with a hard QPS/warehouse cap? I’ll share estimated load/cost once we agree on window.
3) Evelyn: FYI — this is the gating item for claiming “results dashboard is reliable” in GA messaging; we can still launch with explicit caveats if needed.

Logan

—

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 9:36 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Thanks. Confirming from Console side: we’re seeing the symptom as “flat lines / stale charts” because the results API is querying rollup_v2 only (post-28658). When attribution drops or route_id doesn’t join, we show zeros.

+1 to a banner and to a “coverage” metric. If we can get a numeric coverage per {experiment_id, variant_id} it’s also usable as a guardrail in UI.

Question: can we backfill in a way that preserves config versioning? If an experiment changed splits mid-flight, we need to avoid rewriting history incorrectly.

Dylan

—

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 10:02 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

SRE concerns / questions before we bless a backfill:
- What system are we backfilling into (warehouse tables vs online store used by results API)?
- Do we have a clear throttle + abort plan? (We already had a staging incident from tag changes; don’t want a repeat in prod.)
- Can we do this per-account allowlist first (beta accounts only) to validate cost/impact?

I’m ok in principle with a 14d backfill if we cap it and schedule off-peak.

Sean

—

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 10:21 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Agree we need to put an explicit “partial data” message in Console.

Two product questions:
1) What’s the definition of “coverage”? Missing attribution fields at emit time, or missing after aggregation/join? We should pick one and stick to it.
2) Do we expect any customer-visible changes to their metrics after backfill (i.e., their historical p95/cost deltas shift)? If yes, we need to warn beta accounts.

Also: can we tie the warning to a discrete state so Support can point to it instead of writing custom explanations per ticket?

Marissa

—

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 11:07 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Adding from the risk review angle:
- Please make sure any backfill does not reintroduce high-cardinality route tags into metrics.
- For the “coverage” number, I’d rather we base it on the rollup output (what Console will actually use) vs raw ingest. Otherwise we’ll end up with a “coverage says 99% but UI is still missing rows” situation.

If you share the load/cost estimate + throttle settings, I can sign off today.

Priya

—

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 11:48 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Thanks for surfacing early.

Please send:
- Backfill window recommendation (14d vs 30d) with cost delta.
- The GA narrative: what we claim, what we don’t (e.g., “results are directional; guardrails are authoritative”).

I want a crisp decision by EOD so Connor can lock the release train notes.

Evelyn

—

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 1:24 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Answers + proposal tweaks.

Dylan (config versioning):
- Backfill will NOT attempt to re-derive variant assignment; it only fills missing dims where the request logs already captured experiment_id/variant_id at request time.
- We’ll include experiment_config_version as a column where available (post-28692); for earlier we’ll set "unknown" and keep the row but mark it low-confidence.

Marissa (coverage definition):
- Agree with Priya: define coverage as “% of requests in rollup output that land in a valid {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} tuple”.
- Concretely: coverage = 1 - (unknown_tuple_count / total_request_count) over the same time window used by results charts.
- We can show it as a small badge on the experiment results page and use it to gate deltas.

Customer-visible changes:
- Yes, backfill will change historical charts (they’ll go up, not down, because we’re recovering previously dropped rows).
- For beta: we should message “results have improved accuracy; historical charts may update as we backfill the last N days.”

Backfill details (for SRE)
- Target: warehouse rollup table only; the results API reads from that (not an online store).
- Throttle: cap to 15% of normal aggregation job throughput; abort if warehouse queue depth > threshold or if results query latency p95 increases > 20%.
- Rollout: allowlist by account_id (beta accounts) first; then global.

Cost estimate
- Attached: backfill-cost-estimate-2025-03-27.xlsx
- Summary: 14d backfill is “comfortable” (fits into existing nightly windows). 30d becomes materially more expensive and risks extending into business hours if we have retries.

Recommendation:
- Commit to 14d backfill for GA.
- Add an option to extend to 30d post-GA if we see demand + have capacity.

Logan

—

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 2:03 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Looks sane. Two conditions:
1) Start with allowlist-only (beta accounts) and do a 24h soak with explicit monitoring checkpoints.
2) Create an on-call playbook snippet: “if backfill causes query latency spike, flip X flag / pause job / revert to rollup_v1 view.” (Even if we don’t show v1 in Console, SRE needs a kill path.)

If you add those, I’m supportive.

Sean

—

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 4:41 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Decision + plan (so Connor can lock notes):

1) GA backfill window: 14 days
- Run allowlist-only first (beta accounts) starting Sat night.
- Expand after 24h soak if dashboards are clean.

2) Console behavior until coverage stabilizes
- Add "Data completeness" badge (coverage %) on results page.
- If coverage < 95%: hide variant % deltas (or show with warning), continue showing raw metrics.
- Add a persistent banner during backfill window: “Historical charts may update as data is backfilled.”

3) Engineering actions (owners)
- Logan: implement coverage metric in rollup output + wire to results API.
- Dylan/Console: display badge/banner + gating logic.
- Sean/Priya: sign off on throttle + kill switch; add runbook snippet and alert threshold.

Tracking
- Backfill: ENG-9034
- Aggregation: ENG-9027

Evelyn: GA narrative suggestion (for release notes / sales enablement)
- “Route experiment results provide directional comparisons across variants (latency, cost, error). Guardrails and health indicators are authoritative. During the first GA weeks, historical charts may update as we improve attribution coverage.”

Logan

Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 4:41 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Decision + plan (so Connor can lock notes):

1) GA backfill window: 14 days
- Run allowlist-only first (beta accounts) starting Sat night.
- Expand after 24h soak if dashboards are clean.

2) Console behavior until coverage stabilizes
- Add "Data completeness" badge (coverage %) on results page.
- If coverage < 95%: hide variant % deltas (or show with warning), continue showing raw metrics.
- Add a persistent banner during backfill window: “Historical charts may update as data is backfilled.”

3) Engineering actions (owners)
- Logan: implement coverage metric in rollup output + wire to results API.
- Dylan/Console: display badge/banner + gating logic.
- Sean/Priya: sign off on throttle + kill switch; add runbook snippet and alert threshold.

Tracking
- Backfill: ENG-9034
- Aggregation: ENG-9027

Evelyn: GA narrative suggestion (for release notes / sales enablement)
- “Route experiment results provide directional comparisons across variants (latency, cost, error). Guardrails and health indicators are authoritative. During the first GA weeks, historical charts may update as we improve attribution coverage.”

Logan
text:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 9:12 AM PDT
Subject: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Team — pulling forward a cross-functional readout because it’s now showing up in Console beta feedback + SUP-24910 (“results not updating / numbers don’t match”). This is specifically about experiment attribution completeness and the rollup migration for the results dashboard.

TL;DR
- We have measurable data quality gaps in attribution (experiment_id/variant_id/route_id normalization) that can skew deltas, especially for streaming + retries.
- We can fix forward quickly (most is already in flight), but we need an explicit backfill plan + comms/UX guardrail for “partial data” during the migration window.

Current issues (measured in staging + early beta)
1) Attribution drop rate (missing experiment_id and/or variant_id)
   - Ranges 3–6% for non-streaming.
   - Spikes to 8–12% on streaming paths with retries.
   - Primary cause: context loss across retry boundary for stream resume; secondary: older SDKs not passing stickiness key leads to “default bucket” behavior.

2) route_id inconsistencies between router and analytics rollups
   - Router emits normalized template route_id, but some spans still carry raw path (high-cardinality). Our mitigation (dimension limiting/sampling) means those can get dropped in aggregation.
   - Outcome: a subset of traffic lands in “unknown route” bucket and doesn’t show up in Console route drilldown.

3) Rollup schema migration gap
   - Dashboard expects rollup_v2 keyed by {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, model_id, region}.
   - We have partial rollup_v2 coverage starting Mar 18; earlier data is still rollup_v1 (no experiment dims).

Artifacts
- Grafana (guardrails): https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/route-exp-guardrails/route-experiments-guardrails
- Grafana (data quality): https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/route-exp-data-quality/route-experiments-data-quality
- Looker DQ view (rollup_v2): https://looker.redwoodinference.com/looks/8921-experiment-results-rollup-v2-dq
- Attachment: experiment-attribution-drop-rate-2025-03-26.csv

Fix forward (already in flight)
- PR 28622 (streaming retries attribution): https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/28622
- PR 28741 (reduce metrics cardinality; keep required dims in rollups): https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/28741

Proposal: backfill + migration plan (needs sign-off)
A) Ship fixes forward (Mar 28–Apr 1)
   - Merge 28622 to prod behind route_experiments flag first, then broader.
   - Ensure router emits *only* normalized route_id to attribution pipeline for experiment traffic.

B) Backfill rollup_v2 (limited window)
   - Backfill from request log / billing events where we have stable experiment_id + variant_id (not raw traces).
   - Window: last 14 days at GA (initially), then extend to 30 days if costs are acceptable.
   - Ownership: Telemetry/Analytics to implement (ENG-9034), with SRE review for load.

C) Console UX/Comms
   - Add banner/tooltips in results dashboard when attribution drop rate > threshold or during backfill window: “Results are partial; attribution coverage X%.”
   - This helps us avoid “numbers don’t match” escalations while we repair.

Asks (today)
1) Dylan/Marissa: OK to add “partial data” banner + temporarily suppress % deltas when coverage < 95%? (We can still show raw metrics with a warning.)
2) Sean/Priya: Can we proceed with backfill job for 14d window with a hard QPS/warehouse cap? I’ll share estimated load/cost once we agree on window.
3) Evelyn: FYI — this is the gating item for claiming “results dashboard is reliable” in GA messaging; we can still launch with explicit caveats if needed.

Logan

—

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 9:36 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Thanks. Confirming from Console side: we’re seeing the symptom as “flat lines / stale charts” because the results API is querying rollup_v2 only (post-28658). When attribution drops or route_id doesn’t join, we show zeros.

+1 to a banner and to a “coverage” metric. If we can get a numeric coverage per {experiment_id, variant_id} it’s also usable as a guardrail in UI.

Question: can we backfill in a way that preserves config versioning? If an experiment changed splits mid-flight, we need to avoid rewriting history incorrectly.

Dylan

—

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 10:02 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

SRE concerns / questions before we bless a backfill:
- What system are we backfilling into (warehouse tables vs online store used by results API)?
- Do we have a clear throttle + abort plan? (We already had a staging incident from tag changes; don’t want a repeat in prod.)
- Can we do this per-account allowlist first (beta accounts only) to validate cost/impact?

I’m ok in principle with a 14d backfill if we cap it and schedule off-peak.

Sean

—

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 10:21 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Agree we need to put an explicit “partial data” message in Console.

Two product questions:
1) What’s the definition of “coverage”? Missing attribution fields at emit time, or missing after aggregation/join? We should pick one and stick to it.
2) Do we expect any customer-visible changes to their metrics after backfill (i.e., their historical p95/cost deltas shift)? If yes, we need to warn beta accounts.

Also: can we tie the warning to a discrete state so Support can point to it instead of writing custom explanations per ticket?

Marissa

—

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 11:07 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Adding from the risk review angle:
- Please make sure any backfill does not reintroduce high-cardinality route tags into metrics.
- For the “coverage” number, I’d rather we base it on the rollup output (what Console will actually use) vs raw ingest. Otherwise we’ll end up with a “coverage says 99% but UI is still missing rows” situation.

If you share the load/cost estimate + throttle settings, I can sign off today.

Priya

—

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 11:48 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Thanks for surfacing early.

Please send:
- Backfill window recommendation (14d vs 30d) with cost delta.
- The GA narrative: what we claim, what we don’t (e.g., “results are directional; guardrails are authoritative”).

I want a crisp decision by EOD so Connor can lock the release train notes.

Evelyn

—

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 1:24 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Answers + proposal tweaks.

Dylan (config versioning):
- Backfill will NOT attempt to re-derive variant assignment; it only fills missing dims where the request logs already captured experiment_id/variant_id at request time.
- We’ll include experiment_config_version as a column where available (post-28692); for earlier we’ll set "unknown" and keep the row but mark it low-confidence.

Marissa (coverage definition):
- Agree with Priya: define coverage as “% of requests in rollup output that land in a valid {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} tuple”.
- Concretely: coverage = 1 - (unknown_tuple_count / total_request_count) over the same time window used by results charts.
- We can show it as a small badge on the experiment results page and use it to gate deltas.

Customer-visible changes:
- Yes, backfill will change historical charts (they’ll go up, not down, because we’re recovering previously dropped rows).
- For beta: we should message “results have improved accuracy; historical charts may update as we backfill the last N days.”

Backfill details (for SRE)
- Target: warehouse rollup table only; the results API reads from that (not an online store).
- Throttle: cap to 15% of normal aggregation job throughput; abort if warehouse queue depth > threshold or if results query latency p95 increases > 20%.
- Rollout: allowlist by account_id (beta accounts) first; then global.

Cost estimate
- Attached: backfill-cost-estimate-2025-03-27.xlsx
- Summary: 14d backfill is “comfortable” (fits into existing nightly windows). 30d becomes materially more expensive and risks extending into business hours if we have retries.

Recommendation:
- Commit to 14d backfill for GA.
- Add an option to extend to 30d post-GA if we see demand + have capacity.

Logan

—

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 2:03 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Looks sane. Two conditions:
1) Start with allowlist-only (beta accounts) and do a 24h soak with explicit monitoring checkpoints.
2) Create an on-call playbook snippet: “if backfill causes query latency spike, flip X flag / pause job / revert to rollup_v1 view.” (Even if we don’t show v1 in Console, SRE needs a kill path.)

If you add those, I’m supportive.

Sean

—

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 27, 2025 at 4:41 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Results dashboard: data quality gaps + backfill/migration plan (Route Experiments)

Decision + plan (so Connor can lock notes):

1) GA backfill window: 14 days
- Run allowlist-only first (beta accounts) starting Sat night.
- Expand after 24h soak if dashboards are clean.

2) Console behavior until coverage stabilizes
- Add "Data completeness" badge (coverage %) on results page.
- If coverage < 95%: hide variant % deltas (or show with warning), continue showing raw metrics.
- Add a persistent banner during backfill window: “Historical charts may update as data is backfilled.”

3) Engineering actions (owners)
- Logan: implement coverage metric in rollup output + wire to results API.
- Dylan/Console: display badge/banner + gating logic.
- Sean/Priya: sign off on throttle + kill switch; add runbook snippet and alert threshold.

Tracking
- Backfill: ENG-9034
- Aggregation: ENG-9027

Evelyn: GA narrative suggestion (for release notes / sales enablement)
- “Route experiment results provide directional comparisons across variants (latency, cost, error). Guardrails and health indicators are authoritative. During the first GA weeks, historical charts may update as we improve attribution coverage.”

source.subject: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)
related_links: Linear: ENG-5866 (fix checklist state race condition)
source.body:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Leila Farouk <leila_farouk@redwoodinference.com>, Kenji Nakamura <kenji_nakamura@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)
Attachments: dogfood-notes-2025-03-20.txt, screenshots-checklist-step-drawer.zip

Team — quick summary from dogfood (Console checklist + progress service) and what I think we need for MVP launch.

Top feedback themes (from ~14 internal users across DevEx + a couple folks from SRE):

1) Step state regressions / flicker (P0)
- Several saw steps go Complete → In progress after a refresh.
- Correlates with out-of-order events / delayed ingestion. This lines up with ENG-5866 / PR-28510 work.
- This is the main “trust killer” — we should not ship if step state can regress.

2) Confusing deep links / missing context (P1)
- “Set budgets/quotas” link currently drops you into the general Usage page with no obvious affordance.
- Users wanted a short “what am I looking for” sentence + direct link to the exact section.

3) Checklist entry point + empty state confusion (P1)
- People didn’t notice the checklist on first login if they landed on API Keys.
- When no events exist yet, the empty state reads like an error (“we couldn’t load your progress”).

4) Copy length / step naming consistency (P2)
- Step drawer copy is too long; people skim and miss the acceptance criteria.
- “First successful request” vs “First 2xx” wording caused debate.

5) Loading + error states (P2)
- Some spinners exceed ~3s on the checklist load; no retry button.
- If progress service 5xxs, UI shows blank list (looks broken).

My proposed MVP bar for cohort rollout:
- No regressions in step state (monotonic transitions). If we can’t guarantee ordering/idempotency, we don’t roll.
- Checklist always renders with a safe empty state (“No activity yet — complete Step 1 to begin”) even if there are no events.
- Deep links land users on the right Console section (or we provide a clear callout in the drawer).
- Support can interpret state reliably (Dev — pls sanity check against what your team needs).

Defer / follow-on (not required for MVP):
- Full guided tour overlays
- Automated remediation (“we detected a 401, click here to fix”) — great, but not now
- Admin override tooling (unless Support says this is required for MVP; I think it can wait if state is reliable)

Asks:
- Chloe/Leila: can you confirm timeline for PR-28510 landing + any remaining gaps for ordering/idempotency?
- Kenji/Tessa: can you propose the smallest copy + IA tweaks that address the above (esp. empty state + deep link context) by Monday?
- Logan: any telemetry/data freshness issues from dogfood that would block funnel reporting?

Let’s align on “go/no-go” inputs by EOD today so Marissa/Talia can schedule next week’s cohort rollout review.

— Dylan
Dylan Brooks
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference



From: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Leila Farouk <leila_farouk@redwoodinference.com>, Kenji Nakamura <kenji_nakamura@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 9:36 AM
Subject: Re: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)

Thanks Dylan.

I’ll set up a 30-min “MVP go/no-go” checkpoint for Tues morning (Mar 25) with Marissa + the DRIs. Goal is to exit with: (1) must-fix list, (2) owners, (3) rollout date or slip decision.

Proposed criteria (draft):
- P0s cleared: state monotonicity; safe empty/error states (no blank screens)
- QA signoff on happy path + top failure modes (401, 429, streaming disconnect) across at least 2 SDKs
- Feature flag kill switch validated (toggle off returns Console to normal)

Leila — can you sanity check whether your E2E plan covers the above? If not, let’s add the minimum tests.

Also: please keep admin override out of the MVP unless Dev/Support says it’s required to unblock customers.

— Talia
Talia Benson
Engineering Program Manager
Redwood Inference



From: Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Leila Farouk <leila_farouk@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 10:18 AM
Subject: Re: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)

On the P0 (monotonic step transitions):

- PR-28510 addresses the root issue we saw in dogfood: out-of-order event arrival causing step state “rewind.”
- Approach: idempotency keys + per-step monotonic guard (we only allow forward transitions; duplicates ignored; older timestamps ignored).
- Remaining risk: clock skew across emitters. We’re using server-side emitted timestamps where available; client hints aren’t authoritative.

Timeline:
- PR is up, in review. I expect merge today if we get final signoff from Jada + Logan (schema alignment) by ~2pm.
- After merge, we need ~1 day for staging soak + verify we no longer see regressions on refresh.

Empty state + 5xx fallback:
- I can add an explicit “no events yet” response in the progress API (today) so the UI can render a friendly state.
- For progress service 5xx, we should return last known state (if present) + a warning flag, rather than empty list. I can implement that Monday.

— Chloe
Chloe Martin
Engineering Manager, Console Backend
Redwood Inference



From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Leila Farouk <leila_farouk@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 11:02 AM
Subject: Re: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)

Telemetry notes from dogfood:

Blocking-ish:
- We saw a handful of events missing tenant_id (coming from one gateway path that still uses the legacy auth middleware). That’s a hard fail for funnel attribution.
- I don’t think this is widespread, but we should patch before cohort rollout. I’ll sync with Amara/Brian today.

Non-blocking but should address:
- Data freshness: dashboards will lag up to ~10–15 min due to current batching. That’s fine, but the Console copy shouldn’t imply “real-time.”
- Cardinality: please avoid attaching raw model name strings in events (use Redwood model_id). I saw one dogfood session using full display name.

On PR-28510: +1 to Chloe’s approach. If we’re ignoring older events, I’d like to ensure we log a counter metric for “out_of_order_event_dropped” so we can monitor if it’s still happening.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Telemetry Platform Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Kenji Nakamura <kenji_nakamura@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 1:27 PM
Subject: Re: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)

Proposed “smallest useful” UX/copy adjustments for MVP:

1) Empty state
- Replace current “We couldn’t load your progress” with:
  Title: “Get ready for your first production launch”
  Body: “Complete the first step to start tracking progress. We’ll update your checklist as you make requests.”
  CTA: “Create an API key” (deep link)

2) Step drawer “What / Why / Done when” pattern
- Move acceptance criteria to the top as a single bullet (“Done when: …”).
- Keep the rest under a collapsible “Why this matters” section (defaults collapsed).

3) Deep link context
- In steps that land on generic pages (Usage/Quotas), add a one-line callout: “On this page, look for: [Budgets] → [Create budget alert].”

4) Entry point visibility (P1)
- If user lands on API Keys and checklist not started, show a lightweight banner linking to the checklist.

Tessa and I can produce final copy for the above by Monday noon.

— Kenji
Kenji Nakamura
Product Designer
Redwood Inference



From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya_shah@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 3:48 PM
Subject: Re: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)

From Support POV, agree with the MVP bar Dylan outlined.

Two notes:
1) We don’t need admin override for the first cohort *if* step state is reliable and we have a clear way to tell whether a customer is blocked on auth (401/403) vs quota (429) vs networking/streaming.
- If the checklist can capture “first 401” / “first 429” events, that will save us time in triage.

2) Please avoid language that implies the usage dashboard is instant.
- A common ticket is “I made requests but usage is zero.” If copy can set expectation (10–15 min) that will reduce churn and tickets.

Priya and I can draft a short internal macro: “How to read checklist state during onboarding troubleshooting” once we have final step names.

— Dev
Dev Patel
Support Enablement
Redwood Inference

(adding Priya Shah to keep aligned on top onboarding ticket patterns)

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Leila Farouk <leila_farouk@redwoodinference.com>, Kenji Nakamura <kenji_nakamura@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Checklist dogfood feedback + bug triage (MVP go/no-go inputs)
Attachments: dogfood-notes-2025-03-20.txt, screenshots-checklist-step-drawer.zip

Team — quick summary from dogfood (Console checklist + progress service) and what I think we need for MVP launch.

Top feedback themes (from ~14 internal users across DevEx + a couple folks from SRE):

1) Step state regressions / flicker (P0)
- Several saw steps go Complete → In progress after a refresh.
- Correlates with out-of-order events / delayed ingestion. This lines up with ENG-5866 / PR-28510 work.
- This is the main “trust killer” — we should not ship if step state can regress.

2) Confusing deep links / missing context (P1)
- “Set budgets/quotas” link currently drops you into the general Usage page with no obvious affordance.
- Users wanted a short “what am I looking for” sentence + direct link to the exact section.

3) Checklist entry point + empty state confusion (P1)
- People didn’t notice the checklist on first login if they landed on API Keys.
- When no events exist yet, the empty state reads like an error (“we couldn’t load your progress”).

4) Copy length / step naming consistency (P2)
- Step drawer copy is too long; people skim and miss the acceptance criteria.
- “First successful request” vs “First 2xx” wording caused debate.

5) Loading + error states (P2)
- Some spinners exceed ~3s on the checklist load; no retry button.
- If progress service 5xxs, UI shows blank list (looks broken).

My proposed MVP bar for cohort rollout:
- No regressions in step state (monotonic transitions). If we can’t guarantee ordering/idempotency, we don’t roll.
- Checklist always renders with a safe empty state (“No activity yet — complete Step 1 to begin”) even if there are no events.
- Deep links land users on the right Console section (or we provide a clear callout in the drawer).
- Support can interpret state reliably (Dev — pls sanity check against what your team needs).

Defer / follow-on (not required for MVP):
- Full guided tour overlays
- Automated remediation (“we detected a 401, click here to fix”) — great, but not now
- Admin override tooling (unless Support says this is required for MVP; I think it can wait if state is reliable)

Asks:
- Chloe/Leila: can you confirm timeline for PR-28510 landing + any remaining gaps for ordering/idempotency?
- Kenji/Tessa: can you propose the smallest copy + IA tweaks that address the above (esp. empty state + deep link context) by Monday?
- Logan: any telemetry/data freshness issues from dogfood that would block funnel reporting?

Let’s align on “go/no-go” inputs by EOD today so Marissa/Talia can schedule next week’s cohort rollout review.

— Dylan
Dylan Brooks
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference

source.subject: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
source.body:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
Attachments: Route_Experiments_GA_Scope_2025-03-20.pdf

Team — confirming GA scope + timeline for Route-level Experiments (“route A/B testing”) in Console Rollouts.

TL;DR
- Target: GA in April release train (Console 2025.04), with region-by-region enablement behind feature flag for the first 1–2 weeks.
- GA focus: safe operations + trustworthy metrics attribution + clear UX.
- Explicit non-goals for GA: full statistical significance engine, customer-defined custom metrics ingestion, cross-region population rebalancing.

GA scope (in)
1) Control plane + config model
- Experiment CRUD + state machine (draft → running → stopping/stopped), server-side validation.
- Deterministic assignment + eligibility rules per ADR-047.
- Stickiness key precedence finalized (SDK/header/customer_id fallbacks) + warnings when key entropy is low.

2) Data plane routing behavior
- Experiment selection applied at route policy evaluation, before fallback/canary resolution.
- Safe defaults if config unavailable (fail closed to control variant, do not “spray” traffic).

3) Telemetry + attribution
- Canonical attribution schema per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region; model_id gated/sampled as discussed).
- Guardrails: attribution drop-rate monitoring + alerting.

4) Console
- Create/manage workflow (wizard), list/detail views.
- Results view with basic deltas (latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput, cost/token) and guardrail status.
- Audit/change history view in Console.

5) Safety / compliance / ops
- RBAC enforced for mutations (create/update/start/stop/delete) + “break-glass” admin documented.
- Audit events for all lifecycle actions with required export fields for enterprise.
- Guardrails: soft-stop + notifications as default GA behavior.
- Optional hard-stop remains behind kill switch and disabled by default at GA (we can enable per-account later).

GA non-goals (explicit)
- No full statistical significance engine in-product (we can show deltas and guardrail thresholds only).
- No customer-managed custom metric ingestion (Redwood-defined metrics + Redwood eval signals only).
- No multi-region cross-population rebalancing beyond deterministic bucketing.

Open risks / required resourcing (asks)
- SRE: I need a committed block for alert review + game day + on-call readiness signoff (week of 3/31).
- Telemetry/data: we need attribution drop-rate consistently <0.5% in staging across streaming + retries; and a clear backfill plan for schema changes.
- Metrics cardinality: final SRE/Obs signoff on tagging restrictions before widening beta.

Requested confirmations by EOD tomorrow (Fri 3/21)
- Dylan: confirm eng cut line for April train + any scope cuts needed to hit quality bar.
- Sean: confirm SRE coverage for (1) alerts/runbooks, (2) game day, (3) GA go/no-go.
- Priya: confirm cardinality constraints + any “must haves” before we GA-enable by default.
- Logan: confirm data quality/backfill plan and target numbers for attribution drop + propagation lag.
- Marissa: confirm GA messaging (esp. “no stat-sig”) + beta→GA comms plan.

Thanks — attaching the one-pager we’ll use for exec staff sync so we’re aligned.

Evelyn Hart
VP Product, Redwood Inference


---

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on scope as written.

Eng cut line proposal (Console 2025.04)
- Keep: results API basic deltas + guardrail banners; audit trail view; RBAC gates on all mutations.
- Keep: assignment determinism + “missing/low-entropy sticky key” warnings (we saw too much skew without this).
- Keep: query cost controls (aggregation-backed charts only; no raw high-cardinality drilldowns at GA).

Scope we should explicitly de-prioritize to protect schedule
- Anything resembling in-Console statistical significance. We should close the loop on the exploratory branch and ensure no UI hooks imply it.
- “Quality” UX beyond showing the eval score stream if present (no deep analysis). If we’re tight, we can display quality as a single normalized score trend per variant and ship richer breakdown later.

Risk callouts
- Results query load: we need the aggregation jobs stable + cached endpoints. If we miss that, Console page performance will regress under larger beta.
- Attribution completeness for streaming + retries: we’re dependent on the last fixes + validation in staging.

If you want a hard GA gate, I’d suggest: (1) attribution drop <0.5%, (2) propagation lag p99 within SLO, (3) no P1 alerts for 7 days during beta expansion.

-Dylan
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Aligned.

SRE coverage / asks
- We can staff a 2-hour alert/runbook review early next week + 90 min game day slot the week of 3/31.
- For GA go/no-go: we’ll need (a) propagation lag alerting enabled, (b) attribution drop-rate alert tuned (the earlier version was noisy), (c) the hard-stop kill switch verified end-to-end.

Strong +1 to keeping hard-stop disabled by default at GA. False positives here would be high-severity, so we should treat enabling it as a per-account opt-in until we have more history.

If product wants a single “break glass” story, we should document:
- who can stop experiments in emergency,
- how quickly config propagates,
- what customers will see when we soft-stop.

Sean
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed from Observability.

For GA, I’m signing off with these constraints (non-negotiable unless we re-review):
- Required metric dimensions: route_id (normalized template), experiment_id, variant_id.
- region allowed; model_id only where already present + sampled expansion (no “always-on” model_id tagging on all metrics).
- No user_id/customer_id in metrics labels. Stickiness keys must never be emitted as labels.
- Traces can include experiment_id/variant_id as span attrs, but the “export set” needs to stay consistent with our existing OTel conventions.

If we do the above, I’m comfortable widening beta and moving to GA enable-by-default after the initial region-by-region ramp.

Priya
Observability, Redwood Inference


---

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 2:09 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed.

Data quality plan (GA gating)
- Attribution drop-rate: target <0.5% overall and <1.0% for streaming traffic specifically. We’ll report both.
- The streaming/retry attribution bug is addressed; we’re validating in staging with synthetic replay this week.

Backfill
- We can backfill 7 days of rollups post-schema change. Beyond that, retention + cost make it not worth it for GA; we’ll document the cutoff clearly in Console (e.g., “variant metrics available from <date>”).

Propagation lag
- We’re instrumenting p50/p95/p99 propagation lag by region; Sean’s team will help set final alert thresholds after game day.

I’ll send a short note to Marissa/Dylan with the exact wording for the Console tooltip on “Why are my results missing?” so Support has something consistent.

Logan
Telemetry & Analytics Lead, Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 3:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on product side.

Comms / expectations we’ll set for GA
- “Experiment Results” = operational deltas + guardrails; not a statistical decision engine.
- We’ll add explicit copy in the results view: “No significance calculation. Use deltas + your own analysis.”
- We’ll call out the backfill cutoff/date in docs + in-product.

Beta → GA plan
- Week 1: GA flag on for existing beta accounts + 2 additional design partners; region ramp with daily check-ins.
- Week 2: widen to self-serve orgs with admin role + no custom SLA constraints.
- Then: enable by default once we have 7 clean days on the key gates Sean/Dylan listed.

Also: I’m aligning with Security on audit export wording (what’s included, retention expectations). That’s the #1 thing Helio/Northbridge keep asking about in the security reviews.

Marissa
Product, Console & Rollouts

title: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
preview:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
Attachments: Route_Experiments_GA_Scope_2025-03-20.pdf

Team — confirming GA scope + timeline for Route-level Experiments (“route A/B testing”) in Console Rollouts.

TL;DR
- Target: GA in April release train (Console 2025.04), with region-by-region enablement behind feature flag for the first 1–2 weeks.
- GA focus: safe operations + trustworthy metrics attribution + clear UX.
- Explicit non-goals for GA: full statistical significance engine, customer-defined custom metrics ingestion, cross-region population rebalancing.

GA scope (in)
1) Control plane + config model
- Experiment CRUD + state machine (draft → running → stopping/stopped), server-side validation.
- Deterministic assignment + eligibility rules per ADR-047.
- Stickiness key precedence finalized (SDK/header/customer_id fallbacks) + warnings when key entropy is low.

2) Data plane routing behavior
- Experiment selection applied at route policy evaluation, before fallback/canary resolution.
- Safe defaults if config unavailable (fail closed to control variant, do not “spray” traffic).

3) Telemetry + attribution
- Canonical attribution schema per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region; model_id gated/sampled as discussed).
- Guardrails: attribution drop-rate monitoring + alerting.

4) Console
- Create/manage workflow (wizard), list/detail views.
- Results view with basic deltas (latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput, cost/token) and guardrail status.
- Audit/change history view in Console.

5) Safety / compliance / ops
- RBAC enforced for mutations (create/update/start/stop/delete) + “break-glass” admin documented.
- Audit events for all lifecycle actions with required export fields for enterprise.
- Guardrails: soft-stop + notifications as default GA behavior.
- Optional hard-stop remains behind kill switch and disabled by default at GA (we can enable per-account later).

GA non-goals (explicit)
- No full statistical significance engine in-product (we can show deltas and guardrail thresholds only).
- No customer-managed custom metric ingestion (Redwood-defined metrics + Redwood eval signals only).
- No multi-region cross-population rebalancing beyond deterministic bucketing.

Open risks / required resourcing (asks)
- SRE: I need a committed block for alert review + game day + on-call readiness signoff (week of 3/31).
- Telemetry/data: we need attribution drop-rate consistently <0.5% in staging across streaming + retries; and a clear backfill plan for schema changes.
- Metrics cardinality: final SRE/Obs signoff on tagging restrictions before widening beta.

Requested confirmations by EOD tomorrow (Fri 3/21)
- Dylan: confirm eng cut line for April train + any scope cuts needed to hit quality bar.
- Sean: confirm SRE coverage for (1) alerts/runbooks, (2) game day, (3) GA go/no-go.
- Priya: confirm cardinality constraints + any “must haves” before we GA-enable by default.
- Logan: confirm data quality/backfill plan and target numbers for attribution drop + propagation lag.
- Marissa: confirm GA messaging (esp. “no stat-sig”) + beta→GA comms plan.

Thanks — attaching the one-pager we’ll use for exec staff sync so we’re aligned.

Evelyn Hart
VP Product, Redwood Inference


---

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on scope as written.

Eng cut line proposal (Console 2025.04)
- Keep: results API basic deltas + guardrail banners; audit trail view; RBAC gates on all mutations.
- Keep: assignment determinism + “missing/low-entropy sticky key” warnings (we saw too much skew without this).
- Keep: query cost controls (aggregation-backed charts only; no raw high-cardinality drilldowns at GA).

Scope we should explicitly de-prioritize to protect schedule
- Anything resembling in-Console statistical significance. We should close the loop on the exploratory branch and ensure no UI hooks imply it.
- “Quality” UX beyond showing the eval score stream if present (no deep analysis). If we’re tight, we can display quality as a single normalized score trend per variant and ship richer breakdown later.

Risk callouts
- Results query load: we need the aggregation jobs stable + cached endpoints. If we miss that, Console page performance will regress under larger beta.
- Attribution completeness for streaming + retries: we’re dependent on the last fixes + validation in staging.

If you want a hard GA gate, I’d suggest: (1) attribution drop <0.5%, (2) propagation lag p99 within SLO, (3) no P1 alerts for 7 days during beta expansion.

-Dylan
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Aligned.

SRE coverage / asks
- We can staff a 2-hour alert/runbook review early next week + 90 min game day slot the week of 3/31.
- For GA go/no-go: we’ll need (a) propagation lag alerting enabled, (b) attribution drop-rate alert tuned (the earlier version was noisy), (c) the hard-stop kill switch verified end-to-end.

Strong +1 to keeping hard-stop disabled by default at GA. False positives here would be high-severity, so we should treat enabling it as a per-account opt-in until we have more history.

If product wants a single “break glass” story, we should document:
- who can stop experiments in emergency,
- how quickly config propagates,
- what customers will see when we soft-stop.

Sean
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed from Observability.

For GA, I’m signing off with these constraints (non-negotiable unless we re-review):
- Required metric dimensions: route_id (normalized template), experiment_id, variant_id.
- region allowed; model_id only where already present + sampled expansion (no “always-on” model_id tagging on all metrics).
- No user_id/customer_id in metrics labels. Stickiness keys must never be emitted as labels.
- Traces can include experiment_id/variant_id as span attrs, but the “export set” needs to stay consistent with our existing OTel conventions.

If we do the above, I’m comfortable widening beta and moving to GA enable-by-default after the initial region-by-region ramp.

Priya
Observability, Redwood Inference


---

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 2:09 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed.

Data quality plan (GA gating)
- Attribution drop-rate: target <0.5% overall and <1.0% for streaming traffic specifically. We’ll report both.
- The streaming/retry attribution bug is addressed; we’re validating in staging with synthetic replay this week.

Backfill
- We can backfill 7 days of rollups post-schema change. Beyond that, retention + cost make it not worth it for GA; we’ll document the cutoff clearly in Console (e.g., “variant metrics available from <date>”).

Propagation lag
- We’re instrumenting p50/p95/p99 propagation lag by region; Sean’s team will help set final alert thresholds after game day.

I’ll send a short note to Marissa/Dylan with the exact wording for the Console tooltip on “Why are my results missing?” so Support has something consistent.

Logan
Telemetry & Analytics Lead, Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 3:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train

Confirmed on product side.

Comms / expectations we’ll set for GA
- “Experiment Results” = operational deltas + guardrails; not a statistical decision engine.
- We’ll add explicit copy in the results view: “No significance calculation. Use deltas + your own analysis.”
- We’ll call out the backfill cutoff/date in docs + in-product.

Beta → GA plan
- Week 1: GA flag on for existing beta accounts + 2 additional design partners; region ramp with daily check-ins.
- Week 2: widen to self-serve orgs with admin role + no custom SLA constraints.
- Then: enable by default once we have 7 clean days on the key gates Sean/Dylan listed.

Also: I’m aligning with Security on audit export wording (what’s included, retention expectations). That’s the #1 thing Helio/Northbridge keep asking about in the security reviews.

Marissa
Product, Console & Rollouts

source.subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Runtime.

Hard constraints from our side:
- Instrumentation MUST be behind config flags, default on only where we’ve validated overhead (staging/canary first).
- Streaming retries + continuous batching semantics have to be correct (no double counting). We’ve been burned here.
- Miss reason taxonomy needs to be stable; don’t iterate labels every other day or SRE dashboards will churn.

Omar is set as lead; Noah will review the final metric definitions vs the ADR before we flip anything beyond canary.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Runtime


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Platform/Telemetry with two conditions:

1) Cardinality policy is non-negotiable:
   - allowlist labels only; no “free-form route name”, no tenant_id, no request_id
   - route_id/model_version_id must be normalized (single source of truth from control plane)
   - prompt-length/token-count must be bucketed (per standards)

2) Ingestion needs an explicit “drop detection” signal.
   We need to know when guards are dropping series so dashboards don’t show false “0 hits”. Please make that part of ENG-4823/4825 acceptance.

Logan/Nadia will own schema enforcement; Caleb will implement the guardrails.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:29 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

title: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Runtime.

Hard constraints from our side:
- Instrumentation MUST be behind config flags, default on only where we’ve validated overhead (staging/canary first).
- Streaming retries + continuous batching semantics have to be correct (no double counting). We’ve been burned here.
- Miss reason taxonomy needs to be stable; don’t iterate labels every other day or SRE dashboards will churn.

Omar is set as lead; Noah will review the final metric definitions vs the ADR before we flip anything beyond canary.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Runtime
