Cc: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 2:06 PM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Approved — thank you.

Decisions captured:
- Proceed with the sprint starting tomorrow.
- Must ship behind flags; staging -> canary -> prod only with gates.
- Cardinality policy enforced via allowlists + ingestion guards; add explicit drop-detection metrics.
- Perf gates: p95 <0.5% regression at default sampling; p99 >1% at canary blocks unless Runtime+SRE approve.
- Alerts start ticket-level; page only after baseline stabilization.
- Runbook + telemetry pipeline health indicator are required deliverables.

Talia: please set up a daily 15-min leadership checkpoint this week only (Mon–Fri) to keep decision latency low.

Connor/Allison/Logan/Omar: please reply-all by EOD with the concrete rollout timeline (staging date, canary start, target prod date) + explicit rollback owner.

Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Edge attachment & egress matrix — private VPC reference review
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@novacloud.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Feb 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Edge attachment & egress matrix — private VPC reference review

Hi Sara —

Following up from our kickoff: attached is an initial matrix that maps our control-plane/data-plane attachment points, expected egress destinations, and suggested firewall/NAT patterns for the private VPC deployment. This is v1 (vpc-egress-attachment-matrix-v1.xlsx).

High level:
- Control plane (Redwood managed) will terminate in the customer VPC via a dedicated control-plane endpoint in the management subnet.
- Data-plane inference traffic will stay in-customer (tenant subnets) and only contact Redwood endpoints for model metadata/rollouts and telemetry when allowed.
- We propose three egress classes: local-only (no internet), proxied (through customer HTTP/S proxy), and shared egress (via a dedicated NAT with strict ACLs).

Questions for the review:
1) Any constraints on accepting TLS-terminating proxies vs TCP passthrough for our telemetry channels?
2) Preferred source IP preservation for audit logs (do you need client IPs preserved)?
3) Any corporate-managed egress scanning (DLP) that would require changes to our packaging?

Can we do a 30-min readout today or Monday to converge? I copied Irene and Hannah to capture control-plane specifics and operational runbook items.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Sr. Solutions Architect
Redwood Inference
Attachments: vpc-egress-attachment-matrix-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet, 120KB)

From: Sara Patel <sara@novacloud.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: NovaCloud Networking Team <netops@novacloud.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Feb 2025 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Edge attachment & egress matrix — private VPC reference review

Thanks Logan —

Appreciate the matrix. A few quick clarifications from our side before a call:
- We prefer proxy termination for outbound HTTP/S (company policy). TLS passthrough is limited to approved services only.
- For audit, preserving the original source IP is important for our SIEM correlation. If you can support X-Forwarded-For or keep TCP orig source when possible, that helps.
- We do have per-tenant DLP scanning on egress; anything routed through our shared NAT will be scanned.

Specific ask: can you mark which control-plane endpoints need origin IP preservation vs which ones can use a proxied/e2e TLS pattern? Also call out ports (443 vs custom) — our egress rules are port-strict.

I'm free 2–4pm PST today or 9–11am Monday for the readout.

Sara Patel
Director, CloudOps
NovaCloud

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@novacloud.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Feb 2025 13:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Edge attachment & egress matrix — private VPC reference review

Sara — good notes. I updated the matrix and attached v2 (vpc-egress-attachment-matrix-v2.xlsx) with the following changes:
- Added column "IP_preservation_required" and flagged control-plane heartbeat, audit-log ingestion, and metrics ingestion as "yes" (these flows expect source IP or X-Forwarded-For).
- Marked model artifact downloads and large payload checkpoints as "proxied" (HTTPS over 443) by default — can be routed via your outbound proxy.
- Added port matrix: 443 (HTTPS) for most flows; 8443 for internal admin endpoints (optional and can be tunneled through 443 if needed).
- Included a suggested ACL snippet for the shared NAT: allow outbound to s3-compatible endpoints, telemetry.ingest.redwood.ai on 443, and control-plane.redwood.ai on 443/8443.

Notes on implementation:
- We can optionally wrap control-plane in a TLS passthrough if you cannot terminate at the proxy, but that will limit our ability to insert X-Forwarded-For headers.
- If source IP preservation is mandatory, we recommend a short-lived host route (BGP OR static) for telemetry subnets to keep orig IPs, or NAT with 1:1 mapping. That has operational overhead on your side.

I'm happy to walk through the v2 spreadsheet on a call. 2:30pm PST today works for me.

Irene Choi
Principal Architect, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference
Attachments: vpc-egress-attachment-matrix-v2.xlsx (spreadsheet, 180KB)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@novacloud.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Feb 2025 16:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Edge attachment & egress matrix — private VPC reference review

Sara — great, thanks for the details and quick turnaround. 2:30pm PST today is confirmed on our end (calendar invite sent).

A couple of confirmations we'll cover in the readout so the runbook and cutover plan are accurate:
- Telemetry & audit: require IP preservation — we'll outline a short BGP path or 1:1 NAT option and the tradeoffs.
- Artifact downloads: we'll default to proxied 443 and include a checksum verification step in the client to satisfy DLP scanning integrity requirements.
- Admin ports: we'll recommend tunneling 8443 over 443 if your proxy blocks custom ports — plan includes health-check endpoints on 443 to avoid false positives in monitoring.

I'll bring the deployment cutover timeline and the canary plan (how we slowly enable routing and verify logs/metrics). Irene will walk through the matrix specifics and Hannah will take notes for the runbook.

Thanks — see you at 2:30.

Logan Wright
Sr. Solutions Architect
Redwood Inference

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Sara Patel <sara@novacloud.com>
Date: Sat, 15 Feb 2025 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Edge attachment & egress matrix — private VPC reference review

Thanks everyone for the call and alignment. Writing up the readout / action items below (copied into the shared drive and jira ticket).

Decisions made:
- Control-plane heartbeat + audit ingestion: preserve source IP. NovaCloud will provide a short-term host route for telemetry subnets; fallback is 1:1 NAT if host route unavailable.
- Artifact downloads: proxied via corporate HTTP/S proxy on 443. Redwood will add checksum verification and retry/backoff behaviors.
- Admin endpoints: tunnel 8443 over 443 where blocked; implement health-check endpoints on 443.

Action items (owners + ETA):
- NovaCloud: provide egress ACL export and confirm proxy behavior (owner: Sara) — ETA Tue 18 Feb
- Redwood (Irene): produce final v3 matrix with ACL snippets and runbook excerpt — ETA Wed 19 Feb
- Redwood (Logan): draft canary cutover schedule and rollback playbook — ETA Fri 21 Feb
- Ops (Hannah): create JIRA NETOPS-7842 with acceptance criteria and verification checklist — DONE (link in related_links)

Links: drive readout and artifacts: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3ExampleDriveLink
JIRA: NETOPS-7842

If anything needs correction, flag in the drive doc. Otherwise I'll proceed to publish the runbook draft by Wed.

Hannah Schmitt
Director, Platform Operations (Redwood Inference)

source.subject: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks
source.content:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 10, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks

Team — we’re pulling together the quarterly KPI pack and I want to make sure we’re set up to use the Unit Economics dashboard as the source of truth for:

- Blended $/1M output tokens (overall + by model/region/tier)
- QTD / quarterly comparisons (and being able to explain deltas)

Two asks:

1) Readiness: When will the dashboard be “exec-safe” for quarterly reporting? (definition locked, reconciliation story, and expected freshness)

2) Risk assessment: I need a crisp list of known data quality risks + mitigations (including what could cause a misleading KPI readout). Please include what we’ll do if a metric looks off during close.

If there are caveats we should message (e.g., confidence indicators, known gaps by dimension), I’d rather include them explicitly than discover them at readout.

Can someone send me a short status + risk summary by EOD Wed?

— Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Chief Product Officer
Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 10, 2025 at 10:02 AM
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks

Thanks Evelyn. Proposed framing:

What we can make exec-safe for the KPI pack:
- Metric definition + methodology: pending final Finance sign-off (PM-483 checklist). We’re close; main remaining alignment is idle capacity + overhead treatment, and rate card versioning.
- Freshness: hourly aggregation is in progress; for Finance we’re proposing a “finance view” that updates hourly but is pinned to a specific rate card version + day-boundary convention.
- Drilldowns: model / region / tier in v1; model version drilldown is likely v1.1 (we can provide a “top model versions” table via ad hoc query if needed).

Known caveats (likely to call out explicitly):
- Streaming output token parity: we had undercounting in a subset of streaming paths; fix is landing (ENG-8421) and we’ll backfill affected windows.
- Tier mapping: pilots/credits and a handful of resellers have edge cases; we’re moving to an authoritative derivation and adding regression tests.
- Timezone/day-boundary: Finance reporting uses a fixed day boundary; our pipeline is hourly UTC — we need to ensure the finance view matches FP&A expectations for QTD.

Logan/Sean can provide the risk/SLO angle; Laura/Rishi can provide what’s required for quarterly pack acceptance.

Marissa


---

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 10, 2025 at 11:37 AM
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks

Adding an initial risk assessment (Telemetry/Analytics). We’ll send a tighter one-pager Wed, but here are the high-risk items + mitigations:

A) Output token completeness (highest impact)
- Failure mode: missing/undercounted output tokens (especially streaming) -> KPI inflates ($/1M appears worse) and drilldowns skew toward non-streaming workloads.
- Status: identified + fix in flight; we’re adding a canary + regression tests and a completeness check by streaming_flag.
- Mitigation: alert on sudden token drops + missing streaming coverage; backfill tooling to re-run affected hours; dashboard will show a “coverage/confidence” indicator when completeness < threshold.

B) Dimension mapping correctness (model / region / tier)
- Failure mode: rows dropped due to unknown region mapping; tier misclassification moves spend/tokens across segments.
- Status: region normalization is mostly done; tier derivation still has a few edge cases (pilots/credits/resellers).
- Mitigation: enforce “unknown” bucket instead of dropping; alerts for rising unknowns; reconciliation view will include unknown share.

C) Cost attribution inputs (rate card + allocation)
- Failure mode: rate card change causes discontinuity; allocation changes move $ without real infra changes; idle allocation changes can swing blended metric.
- Mitigation: pin dashboard to a rate card version for the KPI pack; add “methodology_version” to all reporting extracts; reconciliation report vs billing totals so FP&A can sanity check.

D) Freshness / lag
- Failure mode: missing hours or delayed ingestion yields partial QTD (trendline looks better/worse than reality).
- Mitigation: SLO on hourly completeness; alert if freshness > X hours; show last-updated timestamp + % hours present in selected window.

Proposed “exec-safe” gating criteria (what we’d like to be true before quarterly pack):
- 99%+ hourly completeness in last 14 days (tokens)
- Unknown dimension share < 0.5% for region + tier (or visibly flagged)
- Streaming/non-streaming output token parity check passing (within agreed tolerance)
- Reconciliation report within Finance tolerance band (Rishi/Laura to define)

Sean/Allison are aligning alert routes / oncall for the pipeline SLOs.

Logan


---

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 11, 2025 at 8:19 AM
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks

From FP&A/Finance perspective, “exec-safe” for quarterly KPI means:

- Consistent methodology: a clearly stated definition (incl/excl) and a versioned rate card with effective dates.
- Reconciliation: we don’t need perfect matching to billing, but we do need a stable, explainable delta with a documented checklist for quarter close (e.g., which costs are in/out; how Dedicated/Private are treated).
- Day boundary: QTD should match our reporting calendar. If the pipeline is hourly UTC, the finance view must align to the reporting day boundary we use for close.

I’m supportive of including a confidence/coverage indicator in the exec view, as long as we keep it simple and avoid implying false precision.

Rishi is drafting the tolerance band proposal for reconciliation (how tight we expect it to be by v1).

Laura


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 11, 2025 at 12:56 PM
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks

On the operational side, we can make this dependable for exec reporting, but only if we treat it like a pipeline with SLOs + clear owners.

Plan (aligned with unit-economics-pipeline-slo):
- Freshness SLO: hourly aggregates available within 2 hours of hour-close (target); page after 4 hours.
- Completeness SLO: >= 99% of expected hourly partitions present for the last 24h; alert on missing partitions by key dimensions (tier/region/streaming_flag).
- Drift checks: alert on token volume drops > threshold (global and per-tier) and spikes in unknown mappings.
- Runbook: backfill steps + verification queries; explicit step for “when to loop Finance” (if reconciliation delta changes materially).

We’ll route alerts to the Eng oncall for pipeline health; Finance will not be paged. The runbook will include a single “KPI pack status” check (green/yellow/red) so it’s easy to answer “can I use this number today?”

Sean


---

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 4:41 PM
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks

This is helpful. Here’s what I need by Friday morning so we can confidently reference this in the quarterly KPI narrative:

1) One-page “Exec-safe readiness” note:
- What’s in the KPI pack (exact metric definition + what’s excluded)
- Rate card version we’re using + effective date range
- Day-boundary convention (explicit)
- Reconciliation tolerance band + current status (green/yellow/red)

2) Risk register (top 5):
- For each: risk description, how it would show up, detection (which alert/check), mitigation/backfill, and owner.

3) A simple language snippet we can reuse in the deck as a footnote (e.g., “blended cost includes X; pinned to rate card vY; refreshed hourly; confidence indicator shown when coverage incomplete”).

Marissa/Dylan: please ensure someone owns the “finance view” vs “live view” messaging so we don’t end up with competing numbers.

Laura/Rishi: please confirm the reconciliation tolerance band you’re comfortable with for QTD/quarterly.

Logan/Sean/Allison: please include how we’ll handle the scenario where output tokens are suspected to be undercounted in a window during close (i.e., do we freeze, backfill, or annotate).

Thank you — I’d like to treat this as a durable KPI starting this quarter, but only with the right guardrails.

— Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Chief Product Officer
Redwood Inference

title: Unit Economics dashboard — readiness for quarterly KPI reporting + data quality risks

source.subject: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)
source.body:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:08 AM
Subject: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

Team,

We need to lock the data freshness SLO + alert routing this week so we can bake it into ENG-8429/8435 and have something Finance can rely on for weekly and QTD reporting.

Proposed (v1) SLO targets (for internal dashboard + exec reporting):

1) Freshness / completeness (hourly rollups)
- Definition: hourly aggregates for the prior hour are available in the warehouse views used by Console.
- Target: 99% of hours available within 90 minutes of hour-end ("T+90m").
- Secondary target: 99.9% of hours available within 4 hours of hour-end ("T+4h").

2) Dimension coverage (per hour)
- model_id normalized present for >= 99.5% of output_tokens
- region normalized present for >= 99.5% of output_tokens
- customer_tier present for >= 99.0% of output_tokens
- quantization_profile present for >= 95.0% of output_tokens (lower for v1; will tighten once tagging is fully rolled out)

3) Correctness guardrails (basic)
- output_tokens should not drop >30% hour-over-hour for the same (model_id, region, customer_tier) slice unless total request_count also drops similarly.
- streaming parity check: streaming output_tokens / total output_tokens should remain within expected band (Caleb has a draft baseline by region).

Alerting / routing proposal:

A) Freshness breach
- If latest available hour is > 2 hours behind wall clock: page Telemetry & Analytics oncall (primary) + post to #eng-platform.
- If > 4 hours behind: escalate to Platform SRE oncall (secondary) + post to #eng-sre and #incidents (if widespread).

B) Coverage degradation
- If any of the dimension coverage thresholds above is breached for 2 consecutive hours: post to #eng-platform + create a ticket; no page.
- If breached for 6 consecutive hours OR affects Finance "finance" view reconciliation checks: page Telemetry & Analytics oncall.

C) Drift / anomaly (token drop, streaming parity)
- Slack alert only initially; page if persists 3 consecutive hours.

Ownership
- Telemetry/Analytics (Nadia/Caleb’s rotation) owns investigating pipeline + metering issues.
- SRE owns underlying infra incidents (warehouse availability, scheduler outages), and helps tune alerting.

Questions I need answered today so I can update the SLO doc:
1) Are the targets (T+90m / T+4h) reasonable for SRE? We currently see occasional lag during backfills.
2) Do we page at 2h behind, or only at 4h behind?
3) Which Slack channels do you want as canonical for these alerts? (I suggested #eng-platform + #eng-sre.)

Thanks,
Logan

—

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

T+90m @ 99% is fine from an SRE standpoint, as long as:
- we’re explicit that it’s measured on the “hourly aggregates available” view, not raw ingestion completion
- backfills are excluded from SLO measurement (otherwise we’re going to be paging ourselves)

On paging:
- I strongly recommend NOT paging at 2h behind unless it’s during Finance reporting windows.
- Page at 4h behind (hard). At 2h behind: Slack + auto-ticket.

Channels:
- #eng-sre should only get pages/escalations, otherwise it’s noise.
- #eng-platform is good for the "heads up" alerts.

One more: define a single PagerDuty service for "Unit Econ Pipeline" (owned by Telemetry/Analytics). SRE can be an escalation policy step rather than a separate PD service.

Sean

—

From: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:21 AM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

+1 to Sean.

Two specific suggestions:
1) Put the 2h-behind condition as a warning alert routed to Slack (#eng-platform) and create a Jira/Linear item automatically.
2) Use an explicit “business hours” override for paging at 2h. Finance generally pulls QTD in the morning PT; paging at 2h during 7a-11a PT on weekdays is reasonable.

Also: the dimension coverage thresholds are good, but we should include an "unknown" bucket in the views rather than dropping rows. Missing region/tier shouldn’t reduce totals.

Allison

—

From: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 11:03 AM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

On the alert rules, I can implement in our observability pack once we agree on the thresholds.

Proposed mapping to alert severities:
- SEV3 (Slack only): latest hour > 2h behind OR any dimension coverage below threshold for 2h
- SEV2 (Page): latest hour > 4h behind OR token-drop anomaly persists 3h OR dimension coverage below threshold for 6h
- SEV1 (Page + #incidents): latest hour > 8h behind OR aggregates missing for an entire region/tier unexpectedly

For “business hours”: happy to add a separate alert rule that pages only M-F 7a-11a PT at the 2h mark. Otherwise it’s SEV3 only.

Let me know which channel you want as the canonical landing zone for SEV3. If #eng-platform is the place, I’ll route there.

Aisha

—

From: Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 12:18 PM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

A couple notes from the data quality side:

- For the streaming parity check, we need to be careful about legitimately different mixes by customer tier (Dedicated tends to stream less). I suggest we do parity by route_type (streaming vs non-streaming requests) rather than expecting a stable overall ratio.

- For the token drop alert: recommend adding a minimum volume gate. Otherwise low-volume slices will flap.
  Example: only evaluate the 30% drop rule when output_tokens > 5M for the hour (or request_count > 500).

- Agree with Allison: never drop rows for missing dims. Put them in "unknown" and separately track the coverage ratio.

Caleb

—

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 2:37 PM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

Thanks all — summarizing decisions so I can update the SLO page + get ENG-8429 moving.

DECISIONS (v1):

SLO targets
- SLI: "hourly aggregates available" (warehouse views backing Console), not raw ingestion.
- Backfills excluded from SLO measurement.
- Target: 99% of hours T+90m; 99.9% T+4h.

Data handling
- Missing dims must not drop totals. Route to explicit "unknown" bucket and compute coverage ratios separately.

Alert routing / thresholds
- >2h behind:
  - SEV3 Slack alert to #eng-platform + auto-ticket
  - During M-F 7a-11a PT: also page (SEV2) via Unit Econ Pipeline PD service
- >4h behind: SEV2 page (always) via Unit Econ Pipeline PD service; SRE in escalation policy.
- >8h behind: SEV1 page + #incidents post.

Coverage degradation
- Breach for 2 consecutive hours: SEV3 Slack + ticket
- Breach for 6 consecutive hours: SEV2 page

Anomaly checks
- Token drop rule only evaluated above a minimum volume gate (Caleb to set exact thresholds; tentatively output_tokens > 5M OR request_count > 500).
- Streaming parity check: change to something stable (by route_type) to avoid tier mix false positives.

Ownership
- Primary: Telemetry & Analytics oncall (Unit Econ Pipeline PD service owner)
- Escalation: Platform SRE oncall via escalation policy step

Aisha — please proceed with implementing the alert rules as above, routed to #eng-platform for SEV3.
Caleb/Nadia — please update the runbook with the minimum-volume gate + recommended validation queries (we’ll link it from the alert payload).

I’ll update the Confluence SLO doc and cross-link the oncall guide.

Logan

Logan Wright
Engineering Manager, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

T+90m @ 99% is fine from an SRE standpoint, as long as:
- we’re explicit that it’s measured on the “hourly aggregates available” view, not raw ingestion completion
- backfills are excluded from SLO measurement (otherwise we’re going to be paging ourselves)

On paging:
- I strongly recommend NOT paging at 2h behind unless it’s during Finance reporting windows.
- Page at 4h behind (hard). At 2h behind: Slack + auto-ticket.

Channels:
- #eng-sre should only get pages/escalations, otherwise it’s noise.
- #eng-platform is good for the "heads up" alerts.

One more: define a single PagerDuty service for "Unit Econ Pipeline" (owned by Telemetry/Analytics). SRE can be an escalation policy step rather than a separate PD service.

Sean

—

From: Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:21 AM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

+1 to Sean.

Two specific suggestions:
1) Put the 2h-behind condition as a warning alert routed to Slack (#eng-platform) and create a Jira/Linear item automatically.
2) Use an explicit “business hours” override for paging at 2h. Finance generally pulls QTD in the morning PT; paging at 2h during 7a-11a PT on weekdays is reasonable.

Also: the dimension coverage thresholds are good, but we should include an "unknown" bucket in the views rather than dropping rows. Missing region/tier shouldn’t reduce totals.

Allison

—

From: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 11:03 AM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

On the alert rules, I can implement in our observability pack once we agree on the thresholds.

Proposed mapping to alert severities:
- SEV3 (Slack only): latest hour > 2h behind OR any dimension coverage below threshold for 2h
- SEV2 (Page): latest hour > 4h behind OR token-drop anomaly persists 3h OR dimension coverage below threshold for 6h
- SEV1 (Page + #incidents): latest hour > 8h behind OR aggregates missing for an entire region/tier unexpectedly

For “business hours”: happy to add a separate alert rule that pages only M-F 7a-11a PT at the 2h mark. Otherwise it’s SEV3 only.

Let me know which channel you want as the canonical landing zone for SEV3. If #eng-platform is the place, I’ll route there.

Aisha

—

From: Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 12:18 PM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

A couple notes from the data quality side:

- For the streaming parity check, we need to be careful about legitimately different mixes by customer tier (Dedicated tends to stream less). I suggest we do parity by route_type (streaming vs non-streaming requests) rather than expecting a stable overall ratio.

- For the token drop alert: recommend adding a minimum volume gate. Otherwise low-volume slices will flap.
  Example: only evaluate the 30% drop rule when output_tokens > 5M for the hour (or request_count > 500).

- Agree with Allison: never drop rows for missing dims. Put them in "unknown" and separately track the coverage ratio.

Caleb

—

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aisha Bello <aisha.bello@redwoodinference.com>, Caleb Johnson <caleb.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 2:37 PM
Subject: Re: Data freshness SLO + alert routing for Unit Economics pipeline (finalize)

Thanks all — summarizing decisions so I can update the SLO page + get ENG-8429 moving.

DECISIONS (v1):

SLO targets
- SLI: "hourly aggregates available" (warehouse views backing Console), not raw ingestion.
- Backfills excluded from SLO measurement.
- Target: 99% of hours T+90m; 99.9% T+4h.

Data handling
- Missing dims must not drop totals. Route to explicit "unknown" bucket and compute coverage ratios separately.

Alert routing / thresholds
- >2h behind:
  - SEV3 Slack alert to #eng-platform + auto-ticket
  - During M-F 7a-11a PT: also page (SEV2) via Unit Econ Pipeline PD service
- >4h behind: SEV2 page (always) via Unit Econ Pipeline PD service; SRE in escalation policy.
- >8h behind: SEV1 page + #incidents post.

Coverage degradation
- Breach for 2 consecutive hours: SEV3 Slack + ticket
- Breach for 6 consecutive hours: SEV2 page

Anomaly checks
- Token drop rule only evaluated above a minimum volume gate (Caleb to set exact thresholds; tentatively output_tokens > 5M OR request_count > 500).
- Streaming parity check: change to something stable (by route_type) to avoid tier mix false positives.

Ownership
- Primary: Telemetry & Analytics oncall (Unit Econ Pipeline PD service owner)
- Escalation: Platform SRE oncall via escalation policy step

Aisha — please proceed with implementing the alert rules as above, routed to #eng-platform for SEV3.
Caleb/Nadia — please update the runbook with the minimum-volume gate + recommended validation queries (we’ll link it from the alert payload).

I’ll update the Confluence SLO doc and cross-link the oncall guide.

Logan

Logan Wright
Engineering Manager, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls
source.body:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls

Marissa / Logan — looping in Laura + Rishi for alignment.

We’re close enough on the internal Unit Economics dashboard that we should lock rollout sequencing + access controls + support expectations now, so we don’t end up with Finance depending on something that’s still “dogfood grade.”

Proposed rollout sequence (phased, internal-only):
1) Engineering dogfood (Console + Telemetry/Analytics + Platform) — week of Feb 3
   - Goal: validate query performance + token metering parity (esp. streaming) + dimension coverage (tier/region/model)
   - Output: fix list before broader audience

2) Product (Console/Rollouts + Optimize) — week of Feb 10
   - Goal: confirm UX for drilldowns + top-N tables + default filters; make sure insights are actionable for Optimize

3) Finance (FP&A + CFO staff) — week of Feb 17
   - Goal: KPI-readiness; ensure reconciliation view is stable enough for QTD / monthly close narratives
   - Note: Finance should not be in the first wave until we have the reconciliation report + rate card provenance surfaced

Access control proposal (to avoid “who can see unit costs” churn):
- Feature flag: unit_econ_dashboard_internal (internal-only)
- RBAC: viewer role gated to an allowlist group (Finance + specific Product/Eng leads)
- No customer-identifiable drilldowns; keep everything aggregated to avoid accidental exposure
- Request: Marissa + Logan, please nominate initial allowlist names by EOD Thu so Kaitlyn can wire RBAC cleanly.

Support expectations / ownership:
- We need a clear oncall path because once Finance uses this, a missing hour of data becomes an escalation.
- Proposal:
  - Data freshness/completeness alerts route to Telemetry/Analytics oncall first
  - Console UI/API errors route to Console oncall
  - If reconciliation deltas exceed agreed thresholds, we page Telemetry/Analytics and loop in FP&A during business hours

Open questions we should decide this week:
- What’s the “go/no-go” gate for Finance wave? (I suggest: reconciliation view within agreed tolerance for 5 business days + streaming output tokens parity verified)
- Caching TTL for the dashboard endpoints (separate “live” vs “finance” views?)

If this plan works, I’ll convert into a short rollout checklist and tie it back to PM-482 / PM-487.

Dylan

—
Dylan Brooks
Director of Engineering, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls

Works for Product.

Two notes:
1) For wave 2, I’d like Optimize folks to be able to slice by quantization profile on day 1 (even if we hide it behind an “advanced filters” disclosure). If the data is there but the UI doesn’t expose it, they’ll assume we don’t have it.
2) UX expectation for Finance: the default view cannot be “too flexible.” They’ll take screenshots and those screenshots will end up in exec updates. I want us to pick a default saved view (QTD, all regions, blended, top models table) and label it clearly.

For allowlist: I’ll send a short list today (Product leads only; no broad PM list).

Marissa

—
Marissa Cole
Product Lead, Console & Rollouts
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 11:21 AM
Subject: Re: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls

I’m supportive of the sequencing.

From Finance perspective, the gating criteria for wave 3 need to be explicit and auditable. Otherwise this becomes a debate every close.

What I need before FP&A uses it for any exec reporting:
- A stable definition of “blended $/1M output tokens” (what’s included/excluded)
- A documented reconciliation process (what tables/views, what timing, what acceptable delta)
- Rate card provenance + versioning (if the rate card changes mid-quarter, what happens to historical views?)

Also +1 on access controls. Please make sure the UI makes it clear this is internal / not customer-facing, and that export/share is restricted if possible.

Laura

—
Laura Bennett
CFO
Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 12:07 PM
Subject: Re: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls

Agree on phases.

On support: we can own pipeline freshness/completeness alerts, but I want to avoid “Finance pings data eng directly” as the default.

Suggest:
- One shared Slack channel for the dashboard (triage + questions) and we’ll post runbook links in the alert payloads.
- Data SLOs:
  - Hourly aggregates available by T+90m (99% of hours)
  - Dimension coverage thresholds (e.g., unknown_region < 0.5%, unknown_tier < 0.5%); alert if breached
- Finance gating: I’m aligned with Dylan’s proposal (5 business days within tolerance) IF we define tolerance now.

Re: “live” vs “finance” view — I like the concept:
- Live view: fastest updates, might include partial last hour
- Finance view: only complete hours/days, rate-card version pinned

Rishi — can you propose tolerance for reconciliation deltas? In my experience it’s not zero; it depends on late arriving usage + credits.

Logan

—
Logan Wright
Engineering Manager, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 3:42 PM
Subject: Re: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls

Thanks all — summarizing decisions + next steps so we don’t lose the thread.

Rollout phases (locked):
- Wave 1 (Eng dogfood): week of Feb 3
- Wave 2 (Product): week of Feb 10
- Wave 3 (Finance): week of Feb 17, contingent on go/no-go gates below

Go/no-go gates for Finance wave (proposal; Laura/Rishi please confirm):
1) Definition doc finalized + signed off (blended $/1M output tokens; inclusion/exclusion)
2) Reconciliation report exists and is repeatable (documented query + expected behavior)
3) 5 consecutive business days where reconciliation delta is within tolerance (tolerance TBD by FP&A)
4) Streaming output token metering parity validated (canary + regression test green)
5) Access controls in place (feature flag + RBAC group) and default “Finance view” is set (complete hours only + rate-card pinned)

Access control implementation plan:
- Use feature flag unit_econ_dashboard_internal + RBAC allowlist group (Kaitlyn to implement; I’ll follow up separately)
- Initial allowlist: Finance (Laura/Rishi to nominate), Product (Marissa to nominate), Eng (Logan + myself + a small oncall set)

Support expectations:
- Pipeline/data freshness: Telemetry/Analytics oncall
- Console UI/API: Console oncall
- Finance questions/discrepancies: route through shared Slack channel; avoid DM-based support

Action items:
- Marissa: send Product allowlist names (today)
- Laura/Rishi: send Finance allowlist names + proposed reconciliation tolerance language (by Thu)
- Logan: confirm SLO numbers and alert routing (by Fri)
- Dylan: write a one-pager rollout checklist tied to PM-482/PM-487 and include the Finance gating criteria

If anyone disagrees with the gating criteria, please reply-all with edits by Thu so we can keep the Feb 17 target realistic.

Dylan

—
Dylan Brooks
Director of Engineering, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 12:07 PM
Subject: Re: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls

Agree on phases.

On support: we can own pipeline freshness/completeness alerts, but I want to avoid “Finance pings data eng directly” as the default.

Suggest:
- One shared Slack channel for the dashboard (triage + questions) and we’ll post runbook links in the alert payloads.
- Data SLOs:
  - Hourly aggregates available by T+90m (99% of hours)
  - Dimension coverage thresholds (e.g., unknown_region < 0.5%, unknown_tier < 0.5%); alert if breached
- Finance gating: I’m aligned with Dylan’s proposal (5 business days within tolerance) IF we define tolerance now.

Re: “live” vs “finance” view — I like the concept:
- Live view: fastest updates, might include partial last hour
- Finance view: only complete hours/days, rate-card version pinned

Rishi — can you propose tolerance for reconciliation deltas? In my experience it’s not zero; it depends on late arriving usage + credits.

Logan

—
Logan Wright
Engineering Manager, Telemetry & Analytics
Redwood Inference


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 3:42 PM
Subject: Re: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard — rollout plan + access controls

Thanks all — summarizing decisions + next steps so we don’t lose the thread.

Rollout phases (locked):
- Wave 1 (Eng dogfood): week of Feb 3
- Wave 2 (Product): week of Feb 10
- Wave 3 (Finance): week of Feb 17, contingent on go/no-go gates below

Go/no-go gates for Finance wave (proposal; Laura/Rishi please confirm):
1) Definition doc finalized + signed off (blended $/1M output tokens; inclusion/exclusion)
2) Reconciliation report exists and is repeatable (documented query + expected behavior)
3) 5 consecutive business days where reconciliation delta is within tolerance (tolerance TBD by FP&A)
4) Streaming output token metering parity validated (canary + regression test green)
5) Access controls in place (feature flag + RBAC group) and default “Finance view” is set (complete hours only + rate-card pinned)

Access control implementation plan:
- Use feature flag unit_econ_dashboard_internal + RBAC allowlist group (Kaitlyn to implement; I’ll follow up separately)
- Initial allowlist: Finance (Laura/Rishi to nominate), Product (Marissa to nominate), Eng (Logan + myself + a small oncall set)

Support expectations:
- Pipeline/data freshness: Telemetry/Analytics oncall
- Console UI/API: Console oncall
- Finance questions/discrepancies: route through shared Slack channel; avoid DM-based support

Action items:
- Marissa: send Product allowlist names (today)
- Laura/Rishi: send Finance allowlist names + proposed reconciliation tolerance language (by Thu)
- Logan: confirm SLO numbers and alert routing (by Fri)
- Dylan: write a one-pager rollout checklist tied to PM-482/PM-487 and include the Finance gating criteria

If anyone disagrees with the gating criteria, please reply-all with edits by Thu so we can keep the Feb 17 target realistic.

Dylan

—
Dylan Brooks
Director of Engineering, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)
source.body:
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

Rishi,

As discussed, I’d like to lock the Finance-aligned definition for “blended $/1M output tokens” for the internal Unit Economics dashboard v1 so we can use it in QTD reporting without re-litigating methodology weekly.

Attached is a v1 methodology write-up + the working calc sheet. Please review and confirm what you’re comfortable signing off on for v1.

Proposed v1 metric (Finance view):
- Numerator: GPU + inference-serving infra costs attributable to LLM inference serving (per rate card), incl. shared overhead allocation (logging/metrics/storage/network where applicable) and idle capacity as described below.
- Denominator: output_tokens from platform metering (NOT input tokens), aggregated hourly; streaming and non-streaming paths included.
- Displayed unit: $/1M output tokens.

Allocation & reconciliation expectations (proposed):
1) Rate card: Finance-owned, versioned quarterly. Dashboard should show the rate card version used.
2) Idle capacity: included in blended cost for Hosted + Dedicated pools (allocated by GPU-hours), excluded from Private where customer is charged fixed fees (we will show separately as “platform idle” in an internal-only note, but keep out of the tier drilldown to avoid misinterpretation).
3) Shared overhead: allocated by GPU-hours at region + tier + pool_type where feasible; otherwise global overhead allocated proportional to output tokens.
4) Reconciliation: we expect the dashboard’s ALL-UP monthly totals (aggregated across tiers/regions/models) to reconcile within +/- 3% to Finance cloud GPU spend after timing adjustments (cutoff/day-boundary) and explicit exclusions.

Explicit exclusions for v1:
- One-time credits/promos, sales discounts, and customer billing adjustments (these belong in revenue/margin views, not unit-cost of serving).
- Non-inference R&D GPU usage (training, eval clusters) not tagged as serving.

Caveats we should state in the dashboard (v1):
- “Blended” is not “marginal.” It includes idle + shared overhead per the methodology.
- Tier drilldowns may not sum cleanly to ALL-UP if we have missing tier mapping on a small subset of traffic (we should show a coverage/confidence indicator).
- Streaming output token parity is required; if metering coverage drops below threshold, metric should be flagged as degraded.

Ask:
- Can you confirm the above is acceptable for v1 sign-off?
- If yes, I’ll reply-all with “Finance sign-off granted” + the caveats we want product to include.

Thanks,
Laura

Laura Bennett
CFO, Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- Blended_Cost_Methodology_v1_2025-01-21.pdf
- blended-cost-per-1m-output-tokens-v1.xlsx


---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 11:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

Thanks Laura — reviewed the PDF and the spreadsheet.

I’m aligned with the structure and the explicit exclusions. A few tweaks/conditions for sign-off:

1) Idle capacity treatment (Hosted/Dedicated):
- Agree to include idle in “blended” as long as we are explicit that this is “cost of capacity + utilization,” not marginal request cost.
- We should NOT hide the idle impact. Even if we don’t show an “idle line item” on v1 UI, we need at minimum a tooltip/caveat and a drilldown note in the methodology panel.

2) Reconciliation tolerance:
- +/-3% monthly is fine as an objective, but please state that early v1 may be +/-5% while tier/region mapping and streaming token metering are hardening.
- Also note timing: Finance books spend by invoice period; platform meter is event-time. We need a consistent “reporting calendar” (PT day boundary is ok for internal) and we should call out any known late-arriving data.

3) Rate card versioning:
- Strongly agree. In the dashboard, we should show (a) rate card effective date and (b) last updated date.
- For any backfills, the backfill must use the rate card effective for that period (no retroactive re-rating unless Finance explicitly changes historical assumptions).

4) Denominator definition:
- We need to be crisp that output_tokens are “post-truncation tokens actually generated and returned.” If there are partial/aborted streams, I want the denominator to reflect what we actually emitted (not what was requested).
- For tool calls / structured output: clarify whether tool JSON is tokenized the same way; I assume yes as long as it’s output tokens.

If we incorporate those, I’m comfortable with Finance sign-off for v1.

Rishi

Rishi Malhotra
FP&A, Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 11:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

+1 to all of Rishi’s points.

From Product side, we can incorporate:
- A “Methodology (Finance view)” panel with the caveats and a link to the PDF.
- An always-visible label: “Blended $/1M output tokens (includes idle + overhead).”
- A “Data coverage” badge when tier/region coverage < threshold.

Nadia/Logan: can you confirm we can meet the “post-truncation, emitted tokens” definition for streaming + aborted streams? If not, we should spell out the exception (and I’ll adjust launch messaging).

Marissa

Marissa Cole
Product, Console & Rollouts


---

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 1:18 PM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

Confirming from Telemetry/Analytics:

- Streaming output tokens: we can make denominator be “tokens emitted” for completed streams and “tokens emitted up to cancel/abort” for partial streams. That aligns with “what we actually returned.”
- Truncation: output token counters are based on actual model tokens produced (post server-side truncation).
- Tool calls / JSON: token metering is based on the runtime tokenizer on the final bytes sent; so yes, they count as output_tokens.

Known v1 caveat:
- There is a small class of gateway timeouts where we might miss the terminal event; in that case, we may undercount output tokens for those requests (and that would inflate $/1M). We can quantify impact and put a coverage alert on it.

We will include the “data coverage/confidence” indicator and put a threshold in the SLO (suggest 99.5% of requests have region + tier + output_tokens populated; alert below).

Logan

Logan Wright
Engineering Manager, Telemetry & Analytics


---

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 2:09 PM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

Thanks all.

From Eng side, we’ll treat these as launch gates:
- Show rate card version + effective date.
- Denominator definition as Logan described (emitted tokens).
- Coverage badge + alerting (missing region/tier/output_tokens) and a clear “do not use for exec reporting when degraded” indicator.
- Reconciliation report (ALL-UP monthly) with variance threshold and an “expected reasons” section.

Laura/Rishi: if you’re good with the wording, Marissa can implement the exact caveat language in UI.

Dylan

Dylan Brooks
Director of Engineering, Console & Tooling


---

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 22, 2025 at 4:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

All — closing the loop.

Finance sign-off granted for v1 blended $/1M output tokens, with the following required caveats / expectations to be included in the dashboard:

Required caveats (UI + methodology panel):
1) This is a BLENDED metric. It includes idle capacity and allocated shared overhead per the Finance methodology (not marginal cost per request).
2) Denominator is output tokens actually emitted/returned (including partial tokens for canceled/aborted streams), post-truncation.
3) Data quality: if dimension coverage (tier/region/output_tokens) drops below threshold, the metric is “degraded” and should not be used for exec reporting until recovered/backfilled.
4) Reconciliation: monthly ALL-UP totals should reconcile to Finance GPU spend within +/-3% once stabilized; v1 may see up to +/-5% variance while metering/tier mapping hardens. Timing differences (invoice period vs event-time) are expected and must be documented in the reconciliation view.
5) Rate card versioning: dashboard must display rate card effective date + last updated date; backfills must apply the rate card effective for the backfilled period.

Please make sure the reconciliation report (ENG-8430) explicitly calls out expected sources of variance (late-arriving data, untagged non-serving GPU spend exclusions, missing terminal events) and links to the runbook.

Rishi — if you’d like any wording tightened for the methodology panel, send edits and we’ll incorporate before wider rollout.

Laura

Laura Bennett
CFO, Redwood Inference

2) Reconciliation tolerance:
- +/-3% monthly is fine as an objective, but please state that early v1 may be +/-5% while tier/region mapping and streaming token metering are hardening.
- Also note timing: Finance books spend by invoice period; platform meter is event-time. We need a consistent “reporting calendar” (PT day boundary is ok for internal) and we should call out any known late-arriving data.

3) Rate card versioning:
- Strongly agree. In the dashboard, we should show (a) rate card effective date and (b) last updated date.
- For any backfills, the backfill must use the rate card effective for that period (no retroactive re-rating unless Finance explicitly changes historical assumptions).

4) Denominator definition:
- We need to be crisp that output_tokens are “post-truncation tokens actually generated and returned.” If there are partial/aborted streams, I want the denominator to reflect what we actually emitted (not what was requested).
- For tool calls / structured output: clarify whether tool JSON is tokenized the same way; I assume yes as long as it’s output tokens.

If we incorporate those, I’m comfortable with Finance sign-off for v1.

Rishi

Rishi Malhotra
FP&A, Redwood Inference


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 11:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

+1 to all of Rishi’s points.

From Product side, we can incorporate:
- A “Methodology (Finance view)” panel with the caveats and a link to the PDF.
- An always-visible label: “Blended $/1M output tokens (includes idle + overhead).”
- A “Data coverage” badge when tier/region coverage < threshold.

Nadia/Logan: can you confirm we can meet the “post-truncation, emitted tokens” definition for streaming + aborted streams? If not, we should spell out the exception (and I’ll adjust launch messaging).

Marissa

Marissa Cole
Product, Console & Rollouts


---

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 1:18 PM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

Confirming from Telemetry/Analytics:

- Streaming output tokens: we can make denominator be “tokens emitted” for completed streams and “tokens emitted up to cancel/abort” for partial streams. That aligns with “what we actually returned.”
- Truncation: output token counters are based on actual model tokens produced (post server-side truncation).
- Tool calls / JSON: token metering is based on the runtime tokenizer on the final bytes sent; so yes, they count as output_tokens.

Known v1 caveat:
- There is a small class of gateway timeouts where we might miss the terminal event; in that case, we may undercount output tokens for those requests (and that would inflate $/1M). We can quantify impact and put a coverage alert on it.

We will include the “data coverage/confidence” indicator and put a threshold in the SLO (suggest 99.5% of requests have region + tier + output_tokens populated; alert below).

Logan

Logan Wright
Engineering Manager, Telemetry & Analytics


---

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 21, 2025 at 2:09 PM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

Thanks all.

From Eng side, we’ll treat these as launch gates:
- Show rate card version + effective date.
- Denominator definition as Logan described (emitted tokens).
- Coverage badge + alerting (missing region/tier/output_tokens) and a clear “do not use for exec reporting when degraded” indicator.
- Reconciliation report (ALL-UP monthly) with variance threshold and an “expected reasons” section.

Laura/Rishi: if you’re good with the wording, Marissa can implement the exact caveat language in UI.

Dylan

Dylan Brooks
Director of Engineering, Console & Tooling


---

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Nadia Rahman <nadia.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 22, 2025 at 4:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Unit Economics dashboard: Finance sign-off on blended $/1M output tokens (v1 methodology)

All — closing the loop.

Finance sign-off granted for v1 blended $/1M output tokens, with the following required caveats / expectations to be included in the dashboard:

Required caveats (UI + methodology panel):
1) This is a BLENDED metric. It includes idle capacity and allocated shared overhead per the Finance methodology (not marginal cost per request).
2) Denominator is output tokens actually emitted/returned (including partial tokens for canceled/aborted streams), post-truncation.
3) Data quality: if dimension coverage (tier/region/output_tokens) drops below threshold, the metric is “degraded” and should not be used for exec reporting until recovered/backfilled.
4) Reconciliation: monthly ALL-UP totals should reconcile to Finance GPU spend within +/-3% once stabilized; v1 may see up to +/-5% variance while metering/tier mapping hardens. Timing differences (invoice period vs event-time) are expected and must be documented in the reconciliation view.
5) Rate card versioning: dashboard must display rate card effective date + last updated date; backfills must apply the rate card effective for the backfilled period.

Please make sure the reconciliation report (ENG-8430) explicitly calls out expected sources of variance (late-arriving data, untagged non-serving GPU spend exclusions, missing terminal events) and links to the runbook.

Rishi — if you’d like any wording tightened for the methodology panel, send edits and we’ll incorporate before wider rollout.

Laura

Laura Bennett
CFO, Redwood Inference
